# How to Deposit? | Backpack Exchange

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#common-deposit-assets-and-supported-networks)

Common Deposit Assets and Supported Networks

Asset

Supported Deposit Networks

**USDC**

Solana, Ethereum, SUI, Arbitrum, Base, Optimism, Polygon, Sei EVM, Aptos

**USDT**

Solana, Ethereum, Tron

**BTC**

Bitcoin, Solana (Coinbase BTC)

**ETH**

Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, Optimism

**SOL**

Solana

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#important-notes)

⚠️**Important Notes**

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#id-1.-supported-stablecoin-deposits)

1\. Supported Stablecoin Deposits

Backpack Exchange only supports **native stablecoin deposits** on their respective networks. Depositing the wrong version of a stablecoin may result in your funds not being credited.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#example-optimism-network)

Example: Optimism Network

On the Optimism network, Backpack only supports deposits of **USDT0** with the contract address:

Copy

    0x01bFF41798a0BcF287b996046Ca68b395DbC1071

We do **not** support deposits of USDT with the contract address:

Copy

    0x94b008aA00579c1307B0EF2c499aD98a8ce58e58

triangle-exclamation

If you deposit USDT (not USDT0) on Optimism, the funds will not be credited to your Backpack account.

circle-info

**Note:** Backpack Exchange currently supports only native USDC on each supported network. Bridged or wrapped versions of USDC (such as USDC.e) are not supported. Depositing these versions may result in loss of funds or a lengthy recovery process, if recovery is possible.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#id-2.-how-to-avoid-deposit-errors)

2\. How to Avoid Deposit Errors

Before making a transfer:

1.  **Verify** the deposit address and network carefully
    
2.  **Read** all notices and warnings displayed on the deposit page
    
3.  **Contact** our official support team if you're unsure which network or token to use
    

circle-info

**Incorrect deposits may result in:**

*   ⚠️ Permanent loss of funds, or
    
*   ⏳ A lengthy refund process (if recovery is possible)
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#deposit-via-exchange-wallet-address)

Deposit via Exchange wallet address

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#id-1.-open-the-deposit-menu)

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fk5GSxJi5OxuMHmHxmM36%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De531a385-8fc6-4881-bb6d-28f35f4f89a6&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=1b11f4f5&sv=2)

Search for the token if you don't see it in list.

# Common Errors | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#common-deposit-issues)

Common Deposit Issues

chevron-right**Depositing an unsupported asset**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#depositing-an-unsupported-asset)

If you send a token or use a blockchain network that Backpack Exchange doesn’t support, it will **not** be credited to your account.

We will make a best effort to return these funds, but due to the complex nature of retrieval, **timelines cannot be guaranteed**.

chevron-right**Incorrect Deposit Address**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#incorrect-deposit-address)

If you send funds to the wrong deposit address, they will usually be **lost** and are not retrievable.

**No** exchange can recover assets sent to an unrelated or invalid address.

chevron-right**Missing or Incorrect Memo/Tag/Payment ID**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#missing-or-incorrect-memo-tag-payment-id)

If you forget to include or enter an incorrect memo, tag, or payment ID, your deposit **may not be credited automatically.**

We will make a best effort to recover the funds, but the process **requires time.**

chevron-right**Using the Wrong Blockchain Network**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#using-the-wrong-blockchain-network)

If you send a token using the wrong blockchain network (e.g., USDT via TRC20 to an ERC20 address), your assets **may be lost** or require complex technical recovery.

We will try our best to help you, but retrieval is **not guaranteed.**

chevron-right**Depositing Below the Minimum Amount**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#depositing-below-the-minimum-amount)

If you deposit less than the minimum required amount, the funds will **not** be credited to your account.

In most cases, these deposits **cannot** be refunded.

chevron-right**Depositing After Delisted Token/Network**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#depositing-after-delisted-token-network)

If you deposit a token or use a network that has already been delisted or suspended, the deposit will fail.

We will try our best to help, but recovery is typically not possible.

chevron-right**Network Congestion or Incorrect Gas Fee**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#network-congestion-or-incorrect-gas-fee)

If you send a deposit with too low a transaction fee, or during periods of network congestion, your transaction may be **delayed** or **fail to confirm**.

Funds may arrive once confirmed, but **timing is uncertain.**

chevron-right**Depositing From a Smart Contract (If Unsupported)**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/common-errors#depositing-from-a-smart-contract-if-unsupported)

If you deposit from a smart contract address that Backpack Exchange does not support, the deposit **may fail.**

We will try our best to help you, but recovery is **not always guaranteed.**

# How to Withdraw? | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/how-to-withdraw#withdrawing-to-a-wallet)

Withdrawing to a Wallet

1\. Open the **Withdraw** menu

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FsOrb8GxQDS6MOBnrjLAm%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dc744dc09-21e2-4466-9689-8122646cc660&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=5e50d2c5&sv=2)

2\. Choose the **cryptocurrency** you wish to withdraw and enter the **amount**

circle-info

**Note: If your available balance is insufficient, enabling margin will redeem outstanding lends or create a borrow to cover the withdrawal. If your balance is sufficient, enabling margin does nothing.**

3\. Enter the **destination address** or open **'Address book'** and select address

4\. Click **“Continue”** and then enter your 'Email verification code' and 'Authenticator app code', select **Submit button**

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F7A8AE6K2uav26FGBiLA9%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D64443766-9ee8-449d-9b1d-c97b0df47941&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c6fa6aca&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/how-to-withdraw#transfers-between-sub-accounts)

Transfers Between Sub-Accounts

1.  Click **“Withdraw.”**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FTsjv6mE6mQQf5IChDxP9%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D84fcf34c-b28a-41d5-b2f2-c17eb53c6cf3&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3518cbb8&sv=2)

1.  Under the **“Subaccounts”** tab, select the token you wish to transfer.
    
2.  Enter the transfer amount.
    
3.  From the dropdown menu, select the **source subaccount**.
    
4.  From the dropdown menu, select the **destination subaccount**.
    
5.  Click **“Transfer.”**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FLIrzAMEUEXBZdkaItzBv%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D39f9da0e-6153-48ef-8d5d-3df1a4d8d631&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=804ab67&sv=2)

🎉 Done! Your funds will be credited shortly.

# Travel Rule & Address Book | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/travel-rule-and-address-book#travel-rule)

**Travel Rule**

The Travel Rule is a global regulatory requirement issued by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF). It requires Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs), such as cryptocurrency exchanges, to share sender and recipient information when transferring digital assets above a certain threshold. This helps prevent money laundering, terrorist financing, and other illicit activities.

**Why Does the Exchange Ask for Additional Information When I Withdraw?**

Depending on the withdrawal amount and the destination, you may be asked to provide beneficiary details such as the recipient’s name or account reference. This information is required for Travel Rule compliance and ensures the security and transparency of crypto transfers.

**Is My Personal Information Safe Under the Travel Rule?**

Yes. All data collected for Travel Rule compliance is protected under strict data privacy and security standards. It is used **only** for regulatory compliance and will not be shared with unauthorized parties.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/travel-rule-and-address-book#adress-book)

Adress Book

The **Address Book** is a feature designed to make crypto withdrawals more secure and convenient. It allows you to save frequently used wallet addresses and manage them within your account.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/travel-rule-and-address-book#how-to-add-an-address-to-the-address-book)

**How to Add an Address to the Address Book**

1.  Click **“Portfolio.”**
    
2.  Under the **“Settings”** tab, click **“Address Book.”**
    
3.  From the **“Network”** dropdown menu, select the corresponding network.
    
4.  Click **“Add Address.”**
    
    ![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FkBgb2IOzLVy96518UpTq%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dad5e9075-2904-45fd-a32d-6903aa53f628&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=52847c99&sv=2)
    
5.  Fill in the required fields:
    
    *   Set the label for the address.
        
    *   Enter a brief description (optional).
        
    *   Paste your frequently used withdrawal address.
        
    *   Choose whether to enable **2FA for withdrawals** (recommended).
        
    
6.  Click **“Continue”** to save.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FhOYuHYqpEmdkruY8V1Ig%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D8d6a9614-b205-431e-a16d-5087f47b3f8c&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c20152d4&sv=2)

🎉 Done! Next time you make a withdrawal, you can quickly select this saved address directly from your Address Book.

# FAQs | Backpack Exchange

chevron-rightHow to transfers between exchange accounts?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#how-to-transfers-between-exchange-accounts)

*   The process is exactly the same as [Withdrawing to a Wallet](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#withdrawing-to-a-wallet)
    : simply paste the deposit address of the corresponding asset from the destination exchange.
    
*   **Please note:** Although a withdrawal fee may appear on the page during submission, the system will automatically determine whether the transfer is to another internal exchange address. If it is, the actual credited amount will incur **0 fees.**
    

chevron-right**How long does an on-chain withdrawal take?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#how-long-does-an-on-chain-withdrawal-take)

The processing time depends on:

*   The blockchain network selected (e.g., BTC, ETH, TRON).
    
*   The current level of network congestion.
    
*   The number of confirmations required by the receiving platform.
    

Withdrawals may take from a few minutes to an hour to be received.

chevron-right**Why hasn’t my withdrawal arrived yet?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#why-hasnt-my-withdrawal-arrived-yet)

Common reasons include:

*   The transaction is still waiting for blockchain confirmations.
    
*   Network congestion is delaying processing.
    
*   The receiving platform requires more confirmations before crediting funds.
    

You can track your transaction using the provided **TxID (Transaction ID)** on a blockchain explorer.

chevron-right**What is a TxID and where can I find it?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#what-is-a-txid-and-where-can-i-find-it)

A TxID (Transaction ID) is a unique identifier for your withdrawal on the blockchain. You can find it in your withdrawal history on the exchange and use it to check transaction status on a blockchain explorer.

chevron-right**Can I cancel an on-chain withdrawal after submitting it?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#can-i-cancel-an-on-chain-withdrawal-after-submitting-it)

No. Once a withdrawal request has been processed and broadcast to the blockchain, it cannot be canceled or reversed. Please double-check all withdrawal details before confirming.

chevron-right**What happens if I entered the wrong address?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#what-happens-if-i-entered-the-wrong-address)

If you withdraw to an incorrect or invalid address, the funds may be **lost permanently**. The exchange cannot retrieve funds sent to the wrong address. Always verify the destination address carefully.

chevron-right**Why do some withdrawals require a Memo/Tag?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#why-do-some-withdrawals-require-a-memo-tag)

Certain cryptocurrencies (e.g., XRP, XLM, EOS) require a **memo, tag, or payment ID** in addition to the wallet address. If you forget to include it, your funds may not be credited properly on the receiving platform. Manual recovery may be possible but is not guaranteed and may require service fees.

chevron-right**Are there fees for on-chain withdrawals?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#are-there-fees-for-on-chain-withdrawals)

Yes. Each on-chain withdrawal incurs a network fee (gas fee) to process the transaction on the blockchain. The fee amount depends on the selected network and current network conditions.

chevron-right**What if I chose the wrong network?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals/faqs#what-if-i-chose-the-wrong-network)

Sending a withdrawal via the wrong blockchain network may result in **irreversible loss** of funds. Some recoveries may be possible but are not guaranteed. Always confirm that the recipient platform supports the selected network.

# Fiat | Backpack Exchange

Our fiat solution enables you to deposit and withdraw USD via SWIFT wire transfers directly to and from your personal bank account, providing seamless global fiat access (subject to jurisdictional restrictions).

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat#is-this-service-available-in-all-countries)

Is this service available in all countries?

Due to regulatory requirements, our fiat services are not available in certain jurisdictions. Please refer to the list below for countries and regions where fiat deposits and withdrawals are currently not supported:

*   Afghanistan
    
*   Belarus
    
*   Central African Republic
    
*   Crimea / Sevastopol Region / Luhansk / Donetsk / Kherson / Zaporizhzhia / Dnipropetrovsk Oblast / Kharkiv Oblast
    
*   Cuba
    
*   Democratic People’s Republic of Korea (North Korea)
    
*   Democratic Republic of the Congo
    
*   Gaza
    
*   Iran
    
*   Libya
    
*   Myanmar (Burma)
    
*   Nicaragua
    
*   Russian Federation
    
*   South Sudan
    
*   Syria
    
*   United States
    
*   UAE
    
*   Venezuela
    
*   Zimbabwe
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat#can-i-link-a-business-bank-account)

Can I link a business bank account?

Individual accounts must use personal bank accounts only. For business accounts, onboarding as a corporate client is required. Contact support for assistance.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat#can-i-use-fintech-platforms-e.g.-wise-revolut)

Can I use fintech platforms (e.g., Wise, Revolut)?

Yes, but only if:

*   The account is in your name
    
*   The platform supports named SWIFT USD transfers
    
*   The transfer isn't from a pooled or anonymous account
    

Note: These platforms may reject transactions based on their own policies.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat#will-i-need-to-provide-additional-documentation)

Will I need to provide additional documentation?

To comply with regulatory and banking partner requirements, you may be asked to provide extra information such as:

*   Proof of source of funds (e.g., recent bank statement)
    
*   Purpose of transaction (e.g., “crypto trading”)
    
*   Any other compliance-related documents
    

Please respond promptly to avoid processing delays.

# How to Deposit via Wire | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire#overview)

Overview

Item

**Currency**

USD via SWIFT

**Minimum Deposit**

100 USD

**Processing Time**

Typically 1–5 business days

**Fees**

0 USD

> \*Backpack charges no fee for sending or receiving USD wires. Your sending/ intermediary bank may apply its own charges, which are outside our control.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire#prerequisites)

Prerequisites

*   ✅ **Verified Backpack Exchange account**
    
*   ✅ **Available USD balance in your Bank Account**
    

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire#step-by-step-guide)

Step-by-Step Guide


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire#get-your-backpack-wire-details)

Get Your Backpack Wire Details

*   Log in to [Backpack.exchangearrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/)
    .
    
*   **Open the Deposit modal**
    
    In the top-right corner, click Deposit
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXdO37hdnUVkwUxxZ4ggTxrwz__ZqPWMwtUOJQxBH1dAmPIJBhf_UIQl-U4WlEGP8M28PoTQP5leNjTWO6vUzaugt5bCMhWwzzQjIKiofNo9hM7DrRgoqnmXOuoCrGTEPECZYjEMTg%3Fkey%3DeaNSr6kZABRUBYvUjpI0kw&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f43b11e0&sv=2)

Select **Cash (USD)** → **Wire Transfer**

_**Note: The minimum deposit is $100, and we accept USD only.**_

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXdkQJoc0Y1K26EBecJ9XhsSqY0KcqoXpSfiLTVo467AqnaHSVVldCBTY-DDxWvK2GXnrI1nijseNbPQ_Zwoviro1Jaurl290cRGAudC7TCWpxcAjjFp2o-oX4PK4d1v8cqz2PbSMA%3Fkey%3DeaNSr6kZABRUBYvUjpI0kw&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=4f1f3597&sv=2)

click **Continue**.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FcGT0rBxDwSf2KjZa2qJV%252F1747841567392.jpg%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D079b2a98-a34a-408a-9d9e-931e3bb88970&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f0315f9f&sv=2)

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire#copy-your-bank-details)

**Copy your bank details**


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You’ll see everything you need on the “Wire transfer” screen:

*   Account name
    
*   SWIFT/BIC
    
*   IBAN / Account number
    
*   Sort code (if applicable)
    
*   Reference (include this exactly to credit your account)
    
*   Bank name & address
    
*   Recipient address
    

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire#send-the-wire-from-your-bank)

**Send the wire from your bank**


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

From your personal bank account, initiate a SWIFT transfer using the details above. Make sure:

*   The sending account is in _your_ name (third-party transfers are rejected).
    
*   You send USD only.
    

⚠️ **Important:** Deposits must come from a bank account in your name. Third-party deposits are not supported.

* * *

🎉 **All done!** Your USD deposit is now on its way to Backpack.

Need help or have questions? Reach our support team anytime at [\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
. Be sure to include your wire transfer reference for faster assistance.

# How to Withdraw via Wire | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-withdraw-via-wire#overview)

Overview

Item

**Currency**

USD via SWIFT

**Minimum Withdrawal**

100 USD

**Processing Time**

Typically 1–5 business days

Fees

0 USD\*

> \*Backpack charges no fee for sending or receiving USD wires. Your intermediary / receiving bank may apply its own charges, which are outside our control.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-withdraw-via-wire#prerequisites)

Prerequisites

*   ✅ **Verified Backpack Exchange account**
    
*   ✅ **Available USD balance in Backpack Exchange**
    
*   ✅ **Your Bank Account Number**
    

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-withdraw-via-wire#step-by-step-guide)

Step-by-Step Guide


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-withdraw-via-wire#log-in-to-backpack-and-open-withdrawal-menu)

Log in to Backpack and Open Withdrawal Menu

*   Log in to [Backpack.exchangearrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/)
    .
    
*   Click on **Withdraw**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXcGdUplVM_ltqgF9YmIUG0j_XYBClATvzYCpBYIwCGlMKVdwKnBjAeSqDN6i5WE-HLuAVgnGAsIyXV74KXdb11x2xK86o6Vwfi7ybB-3DhIP0ZN5t1jDQ1iyXNGmdGEZgjpZnmmfw%3Fkey%3DeaNSr6kZABRUBYvUjpI0kw&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=bcd5a409&sv=2)

*   Select **Cash (USD)**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXcMAtbMeORajy-HhtbeVPdUatKa9x7yi2Pl61mMvVwm3OHk_joCz3_bOjxayFztnjQlIUAnfKxgqY113E76a_wDZbKZrEsXiyC6TedDAFgzeJyxaJ1XZaDMxzxL2wQMYokGB0fYtg%3Fkey%3DeaNSr6kZABRUBYvUjpI0kw&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=18705831&sv=2)

**Add or select a bank account**

*   If you haven’t added a recipient before, click **"Select Recipient"** → click **Add Bank Account** :
    
    *   Recipient country
        
    *   Bank identifier type
        
        *   SWIFT/BIC for most international wires
            
        *   Sort code (UK/Ireland only)
            
        
    *   SWIFT/BIC code _or_ Sort code (depending on your choice)
        
    *   Account number
        
    *   Recipient details:
        
        *   Type (Individual / Business)
            
        *   Name, address, city, postcode
            
        
    *   Bank details:
        
        *   Bank name, branch address, city, postcode, country
            
        
    *   (Optional) Intermediary bank SWIFT/BIC
        
    
*   Click Add bank account to save.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXfvxzFdbgzqBsWMSfvoJc2Tkk-XTYCmIz7EI5wpbxgpIdGTysqIC_bAOetfvvx-4hYQpfb5SifSy4_DPKSlCXW1IOGluJ-gWQMjBsujMDapdm-jnH0uRH8PmV6OHEQFcvWLuuig%3Fkey%3DeaNSr6kZABRUBYvUjpI0kw&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f51fe1ff&sv=2)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-withdraw-via-wire#submit-your-withdrawal)

Submit Your Withdrawal

*   Enter the withdrawal amount (**Minimum: USD 100**).
    
*   Select the **account** you wish to withdraw from (if applicable).
    
*   Choose your saved **bank account** from the recipient list.
    
*   Click **Continue**.
    
*   Complete **2-factor authentication** to confirm and submit your withdrawal.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-withdraw-via-wire#track-your-funds)

Track Your Funds

*   Funds typically arrive in your bank account within **1–5 business days**.
    
*   If the funds haven't arrived after **5 business days**, contact Backpack Support and provide your withdrawal reference.
    

* * *

🎉 **That's it!** Your USD withdrawal is now on its way to your bank account.

Need assistance? Our support team is always here to help at [\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
. Please include your withdrawal reference for quicker support.

# Fiat Withdrawal | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-withdrawal#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions

chevron-rightHow long do USD withdrawals take?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-withdrawal#how-long-do-usd-withdrawals-take)

Withdrawals via SWIFT usually arrive within **1-5 business days**, subject to your bank and intermediaries.

chevron-rightWhat if I enter incorrect bank details?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-withdrawal#what-if-i-enter-incorrect-bank-details)

Incorrect or incomplete banking details may result in:

*   Delayed, returned, or lost funds
    
*   Additional recovery fees
    

Always ensure your information is correct and updated.

chevron-rightWhy was my transaction delayed, suspended, or canceled?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-withdrawal#why-was-my-transaction-delayed-suspended-or-canceled)

Transactions can be delayed or suspended due to compliance or risk checks by our banking partners, requiring additional documentation.

If additional information is needed, we'll request:

*   Proof of bank ownership (recent 3-month bank statement)
    
*   Source of funds
    
*   Purpose of transfer or other relevant documentation
    

⚠️ **Important:** Respond promptly to these requests to avoid delays, returned funds, or account restrictions.

chevron-rightWill I need to provide extra documentation?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-withdrawal#will-i-need-to-provide-extra-documentation)

In some cases, our banking partner or your own bank may request additional details—for example:

*   Purpose of transaction (e.g. “crypto purchase”)
    
*   Proof of source of funds (recent bank statement)
    
*   Any other compliance documentation
    

If you receive such a request, please reply promptly with the information to avoid processing delays.

[PreviousFiat Depositchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit)
[NextTradingchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading)

Last updated 2 months ago

---

# Fiat Deposit | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions

chevron-rightWhat details do I need for a wire transfer?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit#what-details-do-i-need-for-a-wire-transfer)

We provide:

*   Account name
    
*   SWIFT/BIC
    
*   IBAN / Account number
    
*   Sort code (if applicable)
    
*   Reference (include this exactly to credit your account)
    
*   Bank name & address
    
*   Recipient address
    

Please ensure accuracy to avoid delays.

chevron-rightHow long does a USD deposit take?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit#how-long-does-a-usd-deposit-take)

Deposits typically take **1–5 business days**, depending on your bank and intermediary banks.

chevron-rightCan I deposit from another person's bank account?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit#can-i-deposit-from-another-persons-bank-account)

**No.** We only accept deposits from bank accounts in your own name. Third-party deposits will be rejected or returned, potentially incurring additional fees.

chevron-rightMy deposit hasn't arrived—what should I do?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit#my-deposit-hasnt-arrived-what-should-i-do)

If a deposit doesn't appear after **5 business days:**

1.  Confirm with your bank that the wire was sent successfully.
    
2.  Verify that all wire details and required reference codes were included.
    
3.  Contact our support with your wire receipt and bank confirmation.
    

chevron-rightWhat happens if my deposit is rejected?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit#what-happens-if-my-deposit-is-rejected)

Rejected deposits (e.g., due to name mismatch or missing reference) are typically returned to the sending bank, potentially incurring third-party fees and delays.

chevron-rightWill I need to provide extra documentation?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/faqs/fiat-deposit#will-i-need-to-provide-extra-documentation)

In some cases, our banking partner or your own bank may request additional details—for example:

*   Purpose of transaction (e.g. “crypto purchase”)
    
*   Proof of source of funds (recent bank statement)
    
*   Any other compliance documentation
    

If you receive such a request, please reply promptly with the information to avoid processing delays.

# Fees | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures/fees#trading-fees)

Trading Fees


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Standard trading fees apply to buy and sell orders, which depend on your VIP level or maker/taker status. For more details, please refer to the section on [trading fees.](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees)

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures/fees#funding-fees)

Funding Fees


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures/fees#overview)

**Overview**

*   The funding rate is the rate paid between longs and shorts every 1 hour.
    
*   If positive, longs pay shorts, if negative, shorts pay longs.
    
*   The countdown shows the time remaining until the next payment.
    
    *   If you close your position before the countdown ends, you will neither pay nor receive the funding fee.
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F3kFMNR9BkoyWgfNx0cyK%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D5259490c-1765-4049-b48f-05d45e2ff8d5&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=b8286d50&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures/fees#settlement-cycle)

**Settlement Cycle**

*   The funding rate settlement cycle is currently 1 hour.
    
*   If there are any changes, they will be updated under **“Market Info”** for the corresponding token.
    
*   You can also check this information before trading.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FOMIf1z5ztGVmq9GucbyZ%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd28163f6-18a7-4014-9321-9f24fba39ddb&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=cf14787d&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures/fees#calculation)

**Calculation**

*   The funding rate reflects market conditions and is **not controlled by the exchange**.
    
*   It is mainly determined by the difference between the perpetual futures price and the spot price, market supply and demand, and borrowing costs.
    
*   For details on how funding rates are calculated, please refer to the documentation [**here**.](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/futures-specs#funding-rate)
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures/fees#how-to-view-the-funding-history-of-a-token)

**How to view the Funding History of a token?**

*   On the exchange page, click **“More”** → **“Market Info”** →
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FGcg5CWCMTBNQCOpwpDtV%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D101abd74-810e-49ea-8f90-8d54172ba620&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=cde548bd&sv=2)

*   Click **“Funding History”,** from the dropdown menu, select the token you want to review. You will see the funding rate details across different timestamps.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FOVLpw7AW7dyQhGsewxWk%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Df6770511-49fd-4bb9-ab5c-91ce775208dd&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8c457878&sv=2)

*   To view a line chart of the token’s historical funding rates:
    
    *   Go to the **Order Placement** page, select the token from the dropdown menu on the left.
        
    *   Under the **Funding** tab, you can see the historical line chart.
        
    *   Hover over the line to view the funding rate at a specific time.
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FiBLEBJyQ3qnLj552LF44%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D27036c5e-6cba-42bc-be52-8df4cd0edd2c&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f05ce030&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures/fees#view-your-funding-payments)

**View Your Funding Payments**

*   Click **Portfolio**.
    
*   Under the **Futures** tab, click **Funding**.
    
*   Use the dropdown menu on the right to select the token you want to view.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FyqyvxSmW7pawPwWYF8VR%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D8960e780-947a-4e2f-9105-1076c8fd536a&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=935ca6fb&sv=2)

# Spot Margin VS Futures | Backpack Exchange

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-vs-futures#what-are-the-differences-between-spot-margin-and-futures)

What are the differences between spot margin and futures?

Feature

Spot Margin

Futures

**Instrument**

You hold the **underlying asset** (borrowed or owned)

You trade a **derivative contract** that tracks an index price

**Leverage**

Typically lower ranges (e.g., ~2×–5×), varies by asset and collateral

Typically higher ranges (e.g., ~10×–50×), varies by market

**Carry / Ongoing costs**

Trading fees **\+ borrow interest**

Trading fees **± funding payments** (you may pay or receive)

**Settlement**

Spot settlement; inventory changes in your wallet

Perpetual contract; PnL settles continuously via funding

**Use case**

Directional exposure to the asset, shorting via borrow, inventory hedging

Directional exposure without holding the asset, hedging, basis/funding strategies

# How to trade Spot | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#spot-trading-on-the-web)

Spot Trading on the Web


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
2.  **Navigate to the Spot Trading section**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., SOL/USD)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
3.  **Select the** [**order type.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types)
    
4.  **Enter the required parameters**
    
    *   Price
        
    *   Quantity
        
    *   ... (and other necessary parameters)
        
    
5.  **Optional settings**
    
    *   **Post Only**: Ensures your order is placed as a maker order and does not take liquidity.
        
    *   **IOC (Immediate or Cancel)**: Executes the order immediately as much as possible; any unfilled portion is canceled. Suitable for quick entries and exits.
        
    *   **Margin**: Uses leverage to improve capital efficiency (note: this increases liquidation risk).
        
    *   ... (and other Optional settings)
        
    
6.  **Submit the order.**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FJCZQhPZIBcVM10EEMeR4%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd2828105-e7fa-45d4-bff5-6c3e428d003f&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c8224456&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#spot-trading-on-mobile)

Spot Trading on Mobile


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
2.  **Navigate to the Spot Trading section**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., SOL/USD)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
3.  **Select the** [**order type.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types)
    
4.  **Enter the required parameters**
    
    *   Price
        
    *   Quantity
        
    *   ... (and other necessary parameters)
        
    
5.  **Optional settings**
    
    *   **Post Only**: Ensures your order is placed as a maker order and does not take liquidity.
        
    *   **IOC (Immediate or Cancel)**: Executes the order immediately as much as possible; any unfilled portion is canceled. Suitable for quick entries and exits.
        
    *   **Margin**: Uses leverage to improve capital efficiency (note: this increases liquidation risk).
        
    *   ... (and other Optional settings)
        
    
6.  **Submit the order.**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Ffy32YqPOQwyrHdeJvFrh%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D11fffa42-56b1-4bfa-a48a-23548b0e54ed&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3891fb83&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

chevron-right**What types of orders can I place in spot trading?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#what-types-of-orders-can-i-place-in-spot-trading)

Available order types include:

*   [**Market Order**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/market-orders)
     – Buy or sell immediately at the best available price.
    
*   [**Limit Order**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/limit-orders)
     – Set your own price and wait for the market to match it.
    
*   [**Conditional Order**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders)
     – Set specific conditions for the order to be triggered when market criteria are met.
    
*   [**TWAP (Time-Weighted Average Price)**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap)
     – Execute a large order over a set period of time at regular intervals to minimize market impact and achieve an average price.
    
*   [**Scaled Order**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/scaled-order)
     – Place multiple orders at different price levels, either progressively higher or lower, to take advantage of price fluctuations.
    

chevron-right**How do I check my open orders and trade history?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#how-do-i-check-my-open-orders-and-trade-history)

You can view open orders in the “Open Orders” tab on the trading page. Completed trades are available under “Fill History” or “Position History” and cancellations are available under "Order history"

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FzJrZiRPvcDrFJWygctVJ%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D30e56e69-693a-42d7-b384-f78c059b0c58&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=30c5f7fd&sv=2)

chevron-right**What fees apply to spot trading?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#what-fees-apply-to-spot-trading)

Different trading levels correspond to different fee rates. For more details, please refer to the section on [trading fees.](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees)

chevron-right**What is the minimum amount I can trade?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#what-is-the-minimum-amount-i-can-trade)

The minimum trade amount depends on the specific trading pair and the exchange’s requirements. This information is usually displayed on the order entry page.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FcH6GHgk6rTyq5xo8Xyl5%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Df8b46540-9e27-4fc8-9ea6-4bf53d60a0ef&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=773a56ab&sv=2)

chevron-right**Can I cancel an open spot order?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#can-i-cancel-an-open-spot-order)

**Yes.** You can cancel any open order from the “Open Orders” section before it is executed. Once the order is filled, it cannot be canceled.

chevron-right**How do I withdraw my assets after a trade?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/how-to-trade-spot#how-do-i-withdraw-my-assets-after-a-trade)

After completing a trade, your assets will be available in your account. You can withdraw them to an external wallet by navigating to the withdrawal page, entering the recipient address, and confirming the transaction. For more details, please refer to the [withdrawal guide.](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/withdrawals)

# Trading Bots | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots#what-is-a-grid-bot)

What Is a Grid Bot?

A Grid Bot is an automated trading tool that executes buy and sell orders within a predefined price range. It divides that range into multiple "grids" (price levels) and automatically places orders at each level—buying at lower prices and selling at higher prices.

Grid bots are typically used in **range-bound markets** where prices oscillate within a defined range. Performance depends entirely on market conditions and configuration—**grid bots do not guarantee profits and may result in losses.**

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots#key-features)

Key Features

*   **Automated Execution** — Trades execute automatically based on your configured parameters, with no manual order placement required.
    
*   **Two-Way Trading** — The bot places buy orders at lower grid levels and sell orders at higher levels.
    
*   **Flexible Configuration** — Customize price ranges, grid count, investment amount, and other parameters to match your strategy.
    
*   **Real-Time Monitoring** — View bot status, executed orders, positions, and accumulated profits at any time.
    
*   **Secure Operation** — Strategies run entirely within the exchange environment. No API keys are required, minimizing operational risk.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots#understanding-the-bot-hierarchy)

Understanding the Bot Hierarchy

Before setting up your bot, it's important to understand how bots and grids are organized:

Level

Scope

Description

Bot

Subaccount

A bot operates at the subaccount level. One bot = one subaccount dedicated to automated trading.

Grids

Symbol

Within a bot subaccount, you create grids for specific trading pairs (e.g., SOL/USD, BTC/USD). Each grid operates independently on its symbol

**Example:** You create a bot subaccount called "My Grid Bot." Within that bot, you can set up a SOL/USD grid and a BTC/USD grid—each with its own price range, grid count, and investment amount.

> **Tip:** Hover over any parameter in the interface to see its tooltip for additional guidance.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots#risk-warnings)

Risk Warnings

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots#market-risks)

Market Risks

*   **No Guarantee of Profit** — Grid bots do not guarantee returns. Users may lose some or all of their invested capital.
    
*   **Market Conditions** — Grid strategies are designed for range-bound markets. Trending markets, breakouts, or unexpected volatility can result in significant losses.
    
*   **Liquidation Risk** — When using leverage or futures grids, price movements may trigger liquidation or forced closure, resulting in loss of funds.
    
*   **Extreme Volatility** — Sharp price swings or low liquidity can cause slippage, execution delays, order failures, or strategy invalidation.
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots#technical-risks)

Technical Risks

*   **Exchange-Side Issues** — While your local network doesn't affect the bot (it runs on the exchange), exchange system instability may temporarily interrupt strategy execution.
    

> ⚠️ **Disclaimer:** Trading bots involve significant risk. You may lose some or all of your invested capital. Past performance does not guarantee future results. This documentation explains how the feature works and does not constitute financial advice or a recommendation to use this product.

# FAQs | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/scaled-order/faqs#does-placing-a-scaled-order-count-as-a-market-order-or-a-limit-order)

**Does placing a Scaled Order count as a market order or a limit order?**

A Scaled Order is made up of multiple **limit orders** distributed across a selected price range. It is not a market order.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/scaled-order/faqs#scaled-order-vs.-twap)

Scaled Order vs. TWAP

Feature

**Scaled Order**

**TWAP (Time-Weighted Average Price)**

**Execution Logic**

Places multiple **limit orders** at predefined price intervals within a chosen price range.

Splits a large order into smaller **market orders** when executed evenly over a fixed time period.

**Control Factor**

Controlled by **price levels**.

Controlled by **time**.

**Execution Guarantee**

Execution not guaranteed – only orders at reached price levels will fill.

Full order is typically executed as long as liquidity exists.

**Primary Objective**

Cost averaging and laddered entry/exit across multiple price levels.

Minimize market impact and achieve an average price over time.

**Market Dependency**

Highly dependent on price movement; orders fill only if the market hits your levels.

Less dependent on price movement; trades are executed over time regardless of volatility.

[PreviousHow to Set a Scaled Order?chevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/scaled-order/how-to-set-a-scaled-order)
[NextTake Profit & Stop Losschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss)

# Conditional Orders | Backpack Exchange

A Conditional Order is an instruction to buy or sell an asset once certain market conditions are met, such as reaching a specific trigger price. When the trigger condition is activated, the order is placed automatically as a market or limit order.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders#when-to-use)

**When to Use？**

*   When you want to **automate entries or exits** based on price movement.
    
*   To **protect against losses** (e.g., stop-loss) or **lock in profits** (e.g., take-profit).
    
*   When you cannot monitor the market continuously but want orders to be placed automatically at key levels.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders#key-points)

**Key Points**

*   Provides flexibility by combining trigger conditions with order execution.
    
*   Can be set as a **stop-market** or **stop-limit** order after the condition is met.
    
*   Execution is not guaranteed if the market does not reach the trigger price, or if there is insufficient liquidity at the chosen limit price.
    
*   Commonly used for **risk management** and **strategic trading automation**.
    

[PreviousLimit Orderschevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/limit-orders)
[NextHow to Set a Conditional Order?chevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders/how-to-set-a-conditional-order)

# Market Orders | Backpack Exchange

A **Market Order** is an instruction to buy or sell immediately at the best available price in the order book.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/market-orders#when-to-use)

**When to Use？**

*   When you want your order to be executed instantly.
    
*   Suitable for entering or exiting a position quickly, regardless of slight price changes.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/market-orders#key-points)

**Key Points**

*   Execution is guaranteed, but the price may vary slightly due to market liquidity (slippage).
    
*   Best for urgent trades, not for precise price control.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/market-orders#how-to-avoid-slippage-risk-when-using-market-orders)

How to Avoid Slippage Risk When Using Market Orders？

*   On the **Futures Order Placement** page, select the token you wish to trade.
    
*   After confirming the trade direction, choose **Market Order**.
    
*   Enter the order quantity.
    
*   Click **Max Slippage Setting**, then enter the maximum slippage you are willing to allow.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FYgpoJSWNOHOoxj52RAf3%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D50ae884f-2e80-46f7-a57b-724542c54a44&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=e6eccb1f&sv=2)

*   Click **Submit** to save the setting.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fl7qX1AK0UDaIFNCXBllw%252Fd16488e0-86d6-4b38-81f9-6277183ac1f8.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D9abc0ae9-4bd8-41fb-842b-feb725c00835&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=fe3bc487&sv=2)

*   Confirm the order.

# Limit Orders | Backpack Exchange

A **Limit Order** allows you to set the maximum price you are willing to pay when buying, or the minimum price you are willing to accept when selling.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/limit-orders#when-to-use)

**When to Use?**

*   When you want to control the exact entry or exit price.
    
*   Suitable for patient traders waiting for favorable market conditions.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/limit-orders#key-points)

**Key Points**

Execution is not guaranteed — the order will only be filled if the market reaches or exceeds your set price.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/limit-orders#why-did-my-limit-order-end-up-being-executed-as-a-market-order)

Why Did My Limit Order End Up Being Executed as a Market Order?

*   The logic of a limit order is:
    
    *   A **buy order** will be executed at a price **≤ your set price**.
        
    *   A **sell order** will be executed at a price **≥ your set price**.
        
    
*   If you do **not** enable the **Post Only** option, the system will match your order against existing orders in the order book immediately, as long as the price condition is satisfied.
    
*   To ensure your order is always placed into the order book as a **Maker** order, you need to check **Post Only**. Otherwise, your order may be executed immediately as a **Taker** order.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FfU6QACUDBjaEdi2UiLk1%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De66992bf-c1d0-45e0-a873-72a7c38f9e0d&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3884204a&sv=2)

**Example:**

*   You set a **buy limit order** for BTC/USD at 30,000.
    
*   The current best ask price is 29,900.
    
*   **Result:** The system will execute the order immediately at 29,900 instead of placing it at 30,000 to wait.

# Trading Fees | Backpack Exchange

At Backpack Exchange, our commitment to providing a transparent and competitive fee structure is paramount.

The table below outlines the Spot Trading & Perpetual Futures fee tiers, enabling traders to easily identify the fees associated with their trading volume.

*   ‍[**Visit Backpack Exchange today.**arrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/)
    

We may change these fees for the next phase of launch and will post the updated fees here beforehand.

* * *

**\*SUBJECT TO CHANGE\***

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees#backpack-exchange-trading-fees)

**Backpack Exchange Trading Fees**

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FDG8xldTUDgKc0LAW17kY%252FBackpack%2520Exchange%2520Fee%2520Structure.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dfdfa4d7c-1a61-453f-82fa-54f3ab0abed8&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a6e00562&sv=2)

_**Important Note on USDT/USDC Trading:**_

Our USDT/USDC trading pair is subject to a **0% fee**, which means that trades on this pair **do not** count toward your 30-day volume requirements for fee tier upgrades.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees#what-is-the-mad-lads-vip-program)

**What is the Mad Lads VIP program?**

Mad Lads holders who connect their Backpack Wallet containing the NFT to Backpack Exchange will automatically qualify for VIP Tier 1 trading fees. The Mad Lads VIP program is in place for both Spot and Perpetual futures.

[**See details about the Mad Lads VIP program here.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/mad-lads-vip)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees#what-are-maker-and-taker-fees)

**What are Maker and Taker Fees?**

*   **Maker Fee:** A "maker" is someone who places a limit order that adds liquidity to the market. This means the order isn’t filled immediately but waits for a matching order (opposite buy or sell) to come along. The fee incurred for such orders is called the "maker fee". Makers typically pay a lower fee as they contribute to the market's liquidity.
    
*   **Taker Fee:** A "taker" is someone who places an order that matches immediately with an existing order on the order book. This takes liquidity away from the market. The fee for such orders is called the "taker fee". Takers typically pay a slightly higher fee due to the immediate nature of their trades.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees#how-do-fee-tiers-work)

**How do Fee Tiers Work?**

The more you trade, the more you save! Our fee structure is designed to reward high-volume traders with reduced fees. As your 30-day trading volume increases, you ascend through our tier levels, and your applicable fees decrease. Both maker and taker fees decrease as you move to a higher tier, allowing for a more cost-effective trading experience.

Please refer to the table provided to view the fee structure across different tiers and the corresponding 30-day volume requirements. We aim to offer a seamless trading experience with a clear understanding of the associated costs.‍

**Note:** Fee tiers are recalculated **every hour**, so your tier status is updated regularly.

# FAQ | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders/faq#why-was-my-conditional-order-not-triggered-even-though-the-market-price-reached-my-trigger-price)

Why was my conditional order not triggered even though the market price reached my trigger price?

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders/faq#logic-of-a-conditional-order)

**Logic of a Conditional Order**

A Conditional Order follows an **“If–Then” logic structure**:

*   **IF** the market price reaches the predefined **trigger price**,
    
*   **THEN** the system automatically submits an actual order (either a **market order** or a **limit order**).
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders/faq#step-by-step-logic-flow)

**Step-by-Step Logic Flow**

1.  **Set Trigger Price** → You define the condition (e.g., “If BTC falls to $25,000”).
    
2.  **Choose Execution Type** → You decide whether the system should place a **market order** (immediate execution) or a **limit order** (at your chosen price).
    
3.  **Wait for Condition** → Until the trigger price is reached, the order stays inactive and does not appear in the order book.
    
4.  **Activation** → Once the market price hits the trigger, the system automatically places your chosen order.
    
5.  **Execution** →
    
    *   If it’s a **market order**: it executes instantly at the best available price.
        
    *   If it’s a **limit order**: it executes only at your specified price or better, if market liquidity allows.
        
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders/faq#troubleshooting-checklist)

**Troubleshooting Checklist**

1.  **Confirm the benchmark price selected for the trigger** — Mark Price, Index Price, or Last Traded Price.
    
    *   If you set it to **Mark Price** but were monitoring the **Last Traded Price**, differences may occur.
        
    *   A conditional order only triggers when the **chosen benchmark** actually reaches your trigger level.
        
    *   If that benchmark never reaches the trigger price, the order will not activate, even if another price type appears to do so.
        
    
2.  **Check whether you placed a limit order.**
    
    *   When using a limit order, once the trigger price is reached, the system will only place your order on the book.
        
    *   The order will only be executed if the **market price reaches or exceeds your specified limit price.**
        
    
3.  In highly volatile markets, there may be **rapid fluctuations** or **gaps** between price updates. If the benchmark price only touches the trigger price momentarily and does not register in the system, the conditional order may not be activated.
    

[PreviousHow to Set a Conditional Order?chevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders/how-to-set-a-conditional-order)
[NextTWAPchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap)

# Scaled Order | Backpack Exchange

A Scaled Order is a trading strategy that places multiple buy or sell orders at predefined price intervals, either above or below the current market price. This creates a series of smaller orders that scale into or out of a position gradually, rather than executing a single large order.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/scaled-order#when-to-use)

**When to Use?**

*   When you want to **average your entry or exit price** over a range instead of committing at one price point.
    
*   Suitable for traders who expect **gradual market movements** or who want to reduce the impact of volatility and slippage.
    
*   Often used to **ladder into positions** during uncertain market conditions or to **ladder out** when taking profits.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/scaled-order#key-points)

**Key Points**

*   Helps reduce the risk of poor timing by spreading orders across different price levels.
    
*   Can be configured with the number of orders, price range, and distribution (equal or weighted).
    
*   Execution depends on market movements — some or all orders may remain unfilled if the price does not reach those levels.
    
*   Useful for both **long-term accumulation strategies** and **short-term trading in volatile markets**.

# Risks | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/risks#id-1.-main-risks-for-lenders-depositors)

**1\. Main Risks for Lenders (Depositors)**

*   **Liquidity Risk:** At times of high utilization, it may be difficult to withdraw funds immediately, as most liquidity is already borrowed.
    
*   **Market Risk (Asset Depreciation):** Lenders deposit crypto assets; if the market value of these assets drops significantly, the real return (in fiat terms) may decrease despite earning interest.
    
*   **Variable Interest Rates:** Lending yields are not fixed and may fluctuate based on borrowing demand. Lower demand results in lower returns.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/risks#id-2.-main-risks-for-borrowers)

**2\. Main Risks for Borrowers**

*   **Liquidation Risk:** If the collateral value falls below the required maintenance margin, the borrower’s position may be liquidated, potentially at a loss.
    
*   **High Interest Costs:** During periods of high utilization, borrowing rates may increase sharply, making loans more expensive.
    
*   **Collateral Volatility:** Since most collateral is in volatile crypto assets, rapid price declines can quickly trigger margin calls and liquidations.
    
*   **Over-Leverage Risk:** Borrowing against volatile assets may amplify losses if the market moves unfavorably.
    

circle-info

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/risks#learn-more-about-risks)

**👉**[**Learn more about** Risks](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/borrow-and-lend/risks)

# FAQs | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions

chevron-right**What fees are charged when using Grid Trading Bots?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#what-fees-are-charged-when-using-grid-trading-bots)

Grid Trading Bots do **not** charge any additional bot-specific fees. You’ll only pay the [**standard trading fees**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees)
 that apply to each executed order, the same as when placing manual trades.

chevron-right**Are all orders placed by Grid Trading Bots limit orders?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#are-all-orders-placed-by-grid-trading-bots-limit-orders)

Yes, Grid Trading Bots primarily use limit orders to place trades.

However, please note that **using a limit order does not guarantee maker fees.** Depending on market conditions and price movements, a limit order may be filled immediately against existing orders on the order book, in which case it would be treated as a taker order and incur taker fees.

chevron-right**Why was my grid order charged taker fees?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#why-was-my-grid-order-charged-taker-fees)

Although Grid Trading Bots always place **limit orders**, whether an order is executed as **maker** or **taker** depends on market conditions at the time the order is submitted.

*   A limit order may be executed as a taker order in the following situations:
    
    *   **Rapid price movements** If the market price moves quickly, a limit order may immediately match against existing orders in the order book.
        
    *   **Order price overlaps with the best available price** When a limit order is placed at or better than the current best bid or ask, it may be filled instantly instead of resting on the order book.
        
    *   **Low market liquidity** In markets with thinner order books, immediate execution is more likely.
        
    *   **Tight grid spacing or high order frequency** Smaller grid intervals increase the chance of immediate matching.
        
    
*   While taker executions cannot be eliminated entirely, you can minimize their frequency by:
    
    *   Trading **high-liquidity pairs**(e.g., BTC, ETH, SOL).
        
    *   Using **wider grid spacing**
        
    *   Avoiding grid activation during high volatility.
        
    *   Setting a reasonable price range that is not too close to the current market price
        
    

chevron-right**Does the Grid Bot guarantee profits?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#does-the-grid-bot-guarantee-profits)

No. Grid bots do not guarantee profits. You may lose some or all of your invested capital depending on market conditions, configuration, and other factors.

chevron-right**What happens if the price moves outside my grid range?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#what-happens-if-the-price-moves-outside-my-grid-range)

If the price moves above your upper limit or below your lower limit, the bot will not execute new trades until the price re-enters the range. Depending on your positions, this may result in unrealized losses.

chevron-right**What happens if the price range is set too wide?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#what-happens-if-the-price-range-is-set-too-wide)

Trade frequency decreases, but price differences between grid levels increase.

chevron-right**How do I choose the number of grids?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#how-do-i-choose-the-number-of-grids)

*   High volatility → More grids
    
*   Low volatility → Fewer grids
    

Users may consider historical volatility when configuring.

chevron-right**Can I stop the strategy at any time?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#can-i-stop-the-strategy-at-any-time)

Yes. When stopping, you can choose whether to close all positions automatically or leave them open.

chevron-right**Why can't I trade manually in my bot subaccount?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#why-cant-i-trade-manually-in-my-bot-subaccount)

When the bot is ON, manual trading is disabled to prevent conflicts between your orders and the bot's automated strategy. Turn the bot OFF if you need to trade manually.

chevron-right**Can I run multiple grids in one bot subaccount?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/faqs#can-i-run-multiple-grids-in-one-bot-subaccount)

Yes. A single bot subaccount can contain multiple grids for different trading pairs. Each grid operates independently on its symbol. Note that take-profit and stop-loss settings apply to the entire subaccount, not individual grids.

[PreviousUsing Grid Bots on Mobilechevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile)
[NextBorrow and Lendchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend)

Last updated 6 days ago

# How to Set a Conditional Order? | Backpack Exchange

1.  **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
2.  **On the Order Placement page, select the appropriate market (Spot or Futures — here we take Futures as an example).**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., BTC-PERP)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
3.  **Select Conditional Order as the order type.**
    
4.  **From the dropdown menu, choose the benchmark for the trigger price.**
    
5.  **If you want the order to be executed as soon as the trigger price is reached**
    
    *   Enter the required parameters directly（Trigger Price and Quantity ）
        
    
    ![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FUQ50DDLpr6LG0Gt1wTzp%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D699c7af9-1eb0-4365-9549-23ea1ddeea85&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=583d7d5b&sv=2)
    
6.  **If you want the order to be executed at a specified price after the trigger price is reached**
    
    *   Check **“Limit Order”** to set the specified execution price.
        
    *   Enter the required parameters（Trigger Price, Limit Price and Quantity ）
        
    
    ![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FoKn8stgkHkxqlyEftIj6%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Ddac53567-7011-4af4-9fc7-546c63dcc168&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=86fc1ad4&sv=2)
    
7.  **Optional settings**
    
    *   **Post Only**: Ensures your order is placed as a maker order and does not take liquidity.
        
    *   **IOC (Immediate or Cancel)**: Executes the order immediately as much as possible; any unfilled portion is canceled. Suitable for quick entries and exits.
        
    *   **Reduce Only**: Ensures your order will only reduce your existing position and will not open a new position or increase the size of the current position.
        
    *   **TP/SL (Take Profit/Stop Loss)**: Sets predefined price levels at which your position will be automatically closed to lock in profits (TP) or limit losses (SL).
        
    *   ... (and other Optional settings)
        
    
8.  **Submit the order.**
