# Margin System | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#overview-of-backpacks-margin-system)

Overview of Backpack’s Margin System


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Backpack’s margin system is designed to be simple and resilient. At a high level you:

*   keep one pool of collateral,
    
*   earn yield on idle balances,
    
*   borrow automatically when you need to,
    
*   and isolate risk with sub-accounts.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#account-and-efficiency)

Account & Efficiency

**Unified cross-margin.** Your eligible assets (spot balances, perps margin, lending balances) live in one collateral pool, so you don’t have to shuffle funds between products.

**Sub-accounts for risk isolation.** Create independent sub-accounts. Each has its own collateral, PnL, and margin—so a mistake in one doesn’t spill into another.

**Fewer transfers, more utility.** Because funds are pooled, you can deploy capital quickly without moving assets around.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#collateral-rules)

Collateral Rules

**How collateral is valued.** Collateral Value = **Quantity × Mark Price × Collateral Weight** (a haircut).

*   **Weights vary by asset** (more volatile/less liquid → lower weight).
    
*   **Size tiers / concentration controls:** very large balances may receive a lower **marginal** weight to limit concentration risk.
    
*   **Dynamic updates:** weights can change with market/liquidity conditions; see the live table [herearrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/market-info/collateral)
     for current values.
    

**👉For more details about Collateral, please refer to the** [**Collateral**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/margin#collateral)
 **and** [**Non-USD Collateral**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/margin#non-usd-collateral)
 **sections in the technical docs.**

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#equity-and-margin-terms)

Equity & Margin Terms

**Net Equity** = Total Collateral Value + Unrealized PnL + Unsettled Balances − Borrowed Liabilities. **Available Equity** = Net Equity − Locked Equity (reserved for open orders/positions).

**IMR (Initial Margin Requirement).** Measures how much equity is committed to current exposure.

*   At **100% IMR**, you can’t increase exposure (no new/size-up positions).
    

**MMR (Maintenance Margin Requirement).** Minimum equity required to keep positions open.

*   At **100% MMR**, liquidation protection triggers.
    

**👉 For full formulas and examples, see the the** [**Equity**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/margin#equity)
 **and** [**Margin**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/margin#margin)
 **sections in the technical docs**

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#yield-and-pnl)

Yield & PnL

**Auto-lend.** Eligible assets in your margin wallet can be lent into the pool and **accrue yield** while still counting as collateral. Rates vary with pool utilization.

**PnL treatment.** **PnL is continuously realized** and flows into **Net Equity** without requiring you to close positions. Profits increase usable equity; losses reduce it.

**Interest interactions.** Borrowed balances **accrue interest** continuously; surplus balances may **earn** via auto-lend. Funding paid/received on perps posts to PnL and therefore Net Equity each interval.

**👉See the** [**Settlement and Realization**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/settlement-and-realization)
 **page for more detailed information**

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#borrowing)

**Borrowing**

*   Auto-Borrowing: When executing trades that exceed available balances (e.g., short-selling), the system automatically borrows the deficit from the lending pool.
    
*   Manual Borrowing: Users can manually borrow assets via the borrow/ lend interface.
    
*   Repayment Methods:
    
    *   proceeds from auto-lend,
        
    *   repurchasing the borrowed asset, or
        
    *   manual repay in the Borrow dashboard.
        
    

**👉For more information about how Borrowing and Lending works, refer to the technical docs** [**here**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/borrow-and-lend)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#risk-and-liquidation)

Risk & Liquidation

**Transparent Risk Engine**

*   **Dynamic Interest Rates:** Borrowing costs adjust based on pool utilization — higher utilization drives higher rates.
    
*   **Public parameters:** collateral weights, IMR/MMR thresholds, and rate curves can be found [herearrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/market-info/market-specs/futures)
    
*   **Real-time monitoring:** positions and margin health are checked continuously using **mark prices** from exchange/oracle sources.
    

**Liquidation flow**

*   **Trigger:** when **MMR reaches 100%**.
    
*   **Partial first:** the system reduces exposure incrementally to restore margin health.
    
*   **Orderbook then backstop:** liquidations route through the **orderbook** first; any remainder can be closed to **backstop liquidity providers**.
    

circle-info

**For full technical details, see the** [**Margin docs**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/margin)

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#important-update-effective-october-6-2025-07-00-utc)

Important Update (Effective October 6, 2025 — 07:00 UTC)


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We’re introducing a liquidation fee.

*   Rate: 1% on all system-triggered perps and borrow/spot-margin liquidations.
    
*   Scope: Applied only to the filled amount of each liquidation order (per fill).
    
*   Unchanged: Regular user trades and fees are unaffected.
    

If you have any questions, please reach out to [\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
.

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

chevron-right**Why is my 50x maximum leverage not taking effect?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#why-is-my-50x-maximum-leverage-not-taking-effect)

The 50x leverage setting may not apply if the trading pair has its own maximum leverage limit or if additional risk parameters are in place. Please check the leverage limits for the specific market you are trading.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FT7M8PkLeWTwdkMaxOyla%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Daa401103-f6a9-4dd3-a264-cbac51a1a47b&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c6770285&sv=2)

chevron-right**Why am I unable to open a position even though my account has sufficient funds?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#why-am-i-unable-to-open-a-position-even-though-my-account-has-sufficient-funds)

*   In such cases, it is usually because the remaining net equity in your account is insufficient to support the minimum margin requirement for the new position. You may try reducing the position size to open a new position until your account’s IMR equals 100%.
    
*   Once your account margin ratio reaches ≥ 100%, you will no longer be able to open new positions.
    

chevron-right**Why does my account still have a balance after liquidation (partial liquidation)?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#why-does-my-account-still-have-a-balance-after-liquidation-partial-liquidation)

*   This happens because Backpack utilises a gradual liquidation mechanism. When your **Maintenance Margin Ratio (MMR)** exceeds 100%, the system will attempt to minimize losses by liquidating only the portion necessary to restore your account to a healthy state. (By contrast, some exchanges may fully liquidate your position at this point.)
    
*   However, please note that if the market continues moving against you for a prolonged period and you do not add collateral, or close your position in time, even partial liquidations may continuously erode your funds until the balance is fully depleted.
    

chevron-right**Why was my position liquidated even though it was still far from the estimated liquidation price?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#why-was-my-position-liquidated-even-though-it-was-still-far-from-the-estimated-liquidation-price)

*   The estimated liquidation price is only a reference. Actual liquidation is triggered when **MMR ≥ 100%**.
    
*   In addition, **Backpack follows a cross-margin model**, where all positions within a sub-account share the same margin balance, and PnL from different positions can offset each other. If the sub-account holds complex positions (e.g., borrowing, using another asset as margin while also shorting it), the system may not be able to provide an accurate estimated liquidation price, since multiple factors influence the calculation and the functions involved are relatively complex.
    

chevron-right**How are liquidation fees charged**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#how-are-liquidation-fees-charged)

A 1% Liquidation fee is charged per order-book conversion when the system liquidates your account (deducted from proceeds; not charged on user-closed positions).

chevron-right**How can I view liquidated positions?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/margin-system#how-can-i-view-liquidated-positions)

*   If a **borrow position** was liquidated, please visit: [https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/borrow-lend/liquidationsarrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/borrow-lend/liquidations)
    
*   If a **futures position** was liquidated, please visit: [https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/futures/liquidationsarrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/futures/liquidations)

# TWAP | Backpack Exchange

A TWAP (Time-Weighted Average Price) Order is an algorithmic trading strategy that splits a large order into smaller trades and executes them evenly over a specified time period. The goal is to achieve an average execution price close to the market’s time-weighted average price.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap#when-to-use)

**When to Use?**

*   When executing a **large order** that might otherwise move the market if placed all at once.
    
*   Suitable for **institutional traders or whales** seeking discreet execution with minimal market impact.
    
*   When you want steady execution over time, rather than immediate fulfillment at one price.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap#key-points)

**Key Points**

*   Helps minimize slippage and reduce market impact.
    
*   Execution is spread out over time at regular intervals.
    
*   Full order is usually completed as long as there is sufficient market liquidity.
    
*   Focuses on achieving a price close to the **average over time**, not a specific single price.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap#key-parameters)

Key Parameters

**Item**

**Explanation**

**Total Quantity**

The complete amount of the asset you intend to trade over the entire strategy duration

**Sub-Order Size**

The size of each individual order slice executed at each interval

**Running Time**

The total duration over which the strategy executes all sub-orders

**Frequency**

How often sub-orders are placed within the running time. _Note: Higher frequency creates smoother execution but requires more market liquidity_

**Randomize**

Each sub-order quantity is automatically adjusted within a range of up to 20% above or below the original quantity

**Reduce Only**

Orders can only reduce the size of an existing open position

**Margin**

Indicates whether the order utilizes leveraged positions

[PreviousFAQchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/conditional-orders/faq)
[NextHow to Use the TWAP Strategychevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/how-to-use-the-twap-strategy)

# Take Profit | Backpack Exchange

A Take-Profit (TP) order automatically closes your position once the market price reaches your chosen profit target. It’s designed to help you secure profits without needing to constantly monitor the market.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/take-profit#trigger-conditions)

Trigger Conditions

A Take-Profit order is activated when your selected trigger price source — Latest, Mark, or Index — reaches or exceeds the Take-Profit trigger price you set.

*   Long (Sell) → Triggered when the market price rises to or above your Take-Profit trigger.
    
*   Short (Buy) → Triggered when the market price falls to or below your Take-Profit trigger.
    

Once triggered, the TP order converts into a Market or Limit order depending on the execution type selected.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/take-profit#execution-types)

Execution Types

**Take-Profit Market Order**

*   Executes immediately at the best available market price once triggered.
    
*   The final execution price may differ from your trigger price due to slippage or price gaps, especially during high volatility.
    
*   Full or partial fills may occur depending on available liquidity.
    

**Take-Profit Limit Order**

*   Places a limit order at your specified limit price once triggered.
    
*   Provides price control, but execution is not guaranteed — if the market reverses quickly, the order may remain partially filled or unfilled.
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/take-profit#parameter-recommendations)

Parameter Recommendations

Long (Sell)

Trigger Price ≤ Limit Price

Setting the limit price slightly above the trigger price improves the likelihood of execution.

Short (Buy)

Trigger Price ≥ Limit Price

Setting the limit price slightly below the trigger price improves the likelihood of execution.

circle-info

**Tip**: Adding a small “spread” between your trigger and limit prices helps balance price control with execution probability.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/take-profit#trigger-price-source)

Trigger Price Source

You can choose which price source triggers your Take-Profit order:

*   **Mark Price** — Helps avoid false triggers from short-term market spikes.
    
*   **Last Traded Price** — More sensitive to price movement but can trigger early in volatile conditions.
    
*   **Index Price** — Reflects a weighted average across multiple markets and helps maintain consistent triggering.
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/take-profit#risk-reminder)

Risk Reminder

*   During high volatility, Market TP orders may execute far from your trigger price.
    
*   Limit TP orders may not execute if the price moves away too quickly.
    
*   To improve reliability, set a small buffer between your trigger and limit prices.
    
*   Ensure your trigger source matches your trading intent (Mark Price recommended for most users).
    

circle-info

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/take-profit#for-details-on-how-to-set-a-take-profit-order-please-refer-to-this-page)

👉**For details on how to set a take-profit order, please refer to** [**this page.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#how-to-correctly-set-take-profit-and-stop-loss-orders-for-open-positions)

# How to Set a Scaled Order? | Backpack Exchange

1.  **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
2.  **On the Order Placement page, select the appropriate market (Spot or Futures — here we take Futures as an example).**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., BTC-PERP)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
3.  **Select Scaled Order as the order type.**
    
4.  **Enter the required parameters**
    
    *   Price Range
        
    *   Order Count(2-50)
        
    *   Total Quantity
        
    
5.  **Select the order distribution type**
    
    *   Flat, Increasing, Decreasing
        
    *   Click **“Preview”** to review the order
        
    
6.  **Optional settings**
    
    *   **Post Only**: Ensures your order is placed as a maker order and does not take liquidity.
        
    *   **IOC (Immediate or Cancel)**: Executes the order immediately as much as possible; any unfilled portion is canceled. Suitable for quick entries and exits.
        
    *   **Reduce Only**: Ensures your order will only reduce your existing position and will not open a new position or increase the size of the current position.
        
    
7.  **Submit the order.**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FMmMviC2KuJj7wMXdsG2Z%252F036f4abf827a7bc83629dfc73de6edf3.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D7c10bb86-9d97-469f-8694-732069f6d69e&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8f458c9&sv=2)

# FAQ | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/faq#why-a-filled-order-may-not-appear-on-the-k-line)

Why a Filled Order May Not Appear on the K-Line？

A common reason for this situation is how candlestick (K-line) charts are constructed versus how individual trades are executed and displayed.

1.  **Candlestick Aggregation**
    
    *   Each candlestick (K-line) represents the **open, high, low, and close prices** within a set time interval (e.g., 1 minute, 5 minutes).
        
    *   If your trade occurred at a price between the recorded high and low, but it did not exactly match the open, close, or extreme values, it will not appear as a labeled point on the candlestick itself.
        
    
2.  **Trade Matching vs. Chart Data**
    
    *   The execution price of your order comes from the **order book matching engine**.
        
    *   The candlestick chart is based on a **stream of trades** during each interval, but depending on chart compression and data aggregation, not every individual trade is visible.
        
    
3.  **Low Volume or Rapid Price Movements**
    
    *   If there was very little trading activity, your trade might have occurred at a price level not revisited by other trades within that candle.
        
    *   Charts often display only the **highest and lowest trade prices** in the timeframe, so intermediate prices (including yours) may not be obvious.
        
    
4.  **Chart Provider Variations**
    
    *   Depending on the charting system or data feed, some platforms may round, filter, or slightly delay certain ticks.
        
    *   This can cause differences between what you see on the chart and the precise execution record of your trade.
        
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/faq#key-takeaway)

Key Takeaway

Your order was executed correctly at the market price available in the order book. However, because candlestick charts only show summarized price points (open, high, low, close) for each interval, **not every individual execution price will be visible on the K-line**. **For exact trade prices, always refer to your order history or trade log rather than the chart.**

[PreviousCharts, candlesticks, Indicatorschevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/charts-candlesticks-indicators)

# Spot Margin Trading | Backpack Exchange

Spot margin trading lets you **borrow assets against your collateral** to increase the size of a spot position. You trade the underlying asset, and borrowed amounts **accrue interest** and are repaid later.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#spot-margin-trading-on-the-web)

Spot Margin Trading on the Web


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
2.  **Navigate to the Spot Trading section**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., SOL/USD)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
3.  **Select** [**Order Type**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types)
    
4.  **Enter the required parameters**
    
    *   Price
        
    *   Quantity
        
    
5.  **Check the "Margin" box**
    
6.  **Submit the order.**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F0nfcElq4S85qE0hAK6Je%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D136c9335-7656-4406-ba8c-6563e8ce0708&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f213bca8&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#spot-margin-trading-on-mobile)

Spot Margin Trading on Mobile


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
2.  **Navigate to the Spot Trading section**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., SOL/USD)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
3.  **Select** [**Order Type**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types)
    
4.  **Enter the required parameters**
    
    *   Price
        
    *   Quantity
        
    
5.  **Check the "Margin" box**
    
6.  **Submit the order.**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FOUkj8WuHnSsHgkNwyVux%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dc7c24441-31bd-4736-b7fc-99799f260cdb&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3754912f&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

chevron-right**What are the fees for spot margin trading?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#what-are-the-fees-for-spot-margin-trading)

Spot margin trading typically incurs the following fees:

*   **Interest on Borrowed Funds** – You are charged interest for borrowing funds. The rate varies depending on the currency. Simply select a different currency on the **lending page** to view the real-time lending and borrowing interest for that currency.
    
*   **Trading Fees** – Standard trading fees apply to buy and sell orders, which depend on your VIP level or maker/taker status.
    

chevron-right**What is leverage in spot margin trading?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#what-is-leverage-in-spot-margin-trading)

Leverage allows you to control a larger position than your initial investment. For example, with 3x leverage, you can trade three times the amount of your deposited collateral. Leverage increases both the potential for profit and the risk of loss.

chevron-right**Can I use spot margin trading with multiple leverage options?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#can-i-use-spot-margin-trading-with-multiple-leverage-options)

*   Yes. You can select different leverage levels for each trade, depending on your risk tolerance and the specific pair you are trading. However, it’s important to consider the increased risk associated with higher leverage.
    
*   Please note that each currency has its own maximum leverage limit, which changes based on the position value.
    

chevron-right**Can I be liquidated with spot margin trading?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#can-i-be-liquidated-with-spot-margin-trading)

Liquidation occurs when your margin balance falls below the required maintenance margin. If this happens, your position will be automatically closed to repay the borrowed funds. This can result in a loss of your collateral.

chevron-right**How do I avoid liquidation in spot margin trading?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#how-do-i-avoid-liquidation-in-spot-margin-trading)

*   Always monitor your account's MMR. At 100% or greater, your account will enter liquidation.
    
*   You can also set up **Stop-Loss orders** to automatically close your position if the market moves against you.
    

circle-info

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/spot/spot-margin/spot-margin-trading#learn-more-about-spot-margin)

👉[**Learn more about Spot Margin.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/trading/spot-margin)

# Welcome - ようこそ - | Backpack Exchange

This page explains Backpack’s **Borrow / Lend** features, with a focus on how assets are handled and the rules around withdrawals.

このページでは、**Backpackの 借入/ 貸出 機能**について、資産の取扱いや出金ルールを中心に説明します。

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/welcome-youkoso#about-auto-lend-rendingunitsuite)

About Auto Lend / 自動レンディングについて

On Backpack, eligible crypto assets are **automatically lent out immediately upon deposit under a 7-day maturity date loan**, and interest begins to accrue.

Auto Lend is not a configurable setting. It is **always applied as part of the asset handling rules**, and no action is required from the user to enable or disable it. Backpackでは、対象となる資産は**入金と同時に、**自動レンディングの対象となり、利息の付与が開始されます。

この自動レンディングは、内部的には**7日間の期間を持つ貸付**（**ローン）**として管理されており、 この期間中は当該資産を出金することができません（出金不可期間）。

自動レンディング は設定項目ではなく、**資産の取扱いルールとして常に適用**されており、 ユーザーによる有効化・無効化の操作は不要です。

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/welcome-youkoso#interest-accrual-nonitsuite)

Interest Accrual / 利息の発生について

*   Interest from lending **accrues automatically**
    
*   Interest continues to accrue **as long as no withdrawal is made**
    
*   **Interest does not accrue while a withdrawal request is pending**
    

_Estimated APY and estimated annual earnings may vary depending on market conditions._

*   レンディングによる利息は **自動的に発生**します
    
*   出金を行わない限り、**利息は継続して付与**されます
    
*   出金申請中は、利息の付与は行われません
    

※ 推定年率利回りおよび想定年間収益は、マーケット状況に応じて変動します。

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/welcome-youkoso#basic-withdrawal-rules-nisururru)

Basic Withdrawal Rules / 出金に関する基本ルール

For the same deposited asset, a new **7-day maturity loan** is created upon the most recent deposit, and the asset **cannot be withdrawn until the loan matures**.

This withdrawal lock is managed **per subaccount and per asset**.

For detailed examples, please refer to the [FAQ](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru)
 section. 同一の資産を追加入金した場合、**直近の入金時点を起点として**、内部的に管理されている貸付（ローン）の期間が、**あらためて7日間に設定されます。**

この期間中は当該資産を出金することができません（出金不可期間）。

この出金制限は、**サブアカウントごと × 資産ごと** に管理されます。

詳細な例については、[FAQ](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru)
をご確認ください。

Last updated 1 month ago

# Stop Loss | Backpack Exchange

A Stop-Loss (SL) order automatically closes your position when the market moves against you and reaches your defined loss threshold. It’s designed to help you manage risk and limit potential losses.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/stop-loss#trigger-conditions)

Trigger Conditions

A Stop-Loss order is activated when your selected trigger price source — Latest, Mark, or Index — reaches or exceeds the stop price you set.

*   Long (Sell) → Triggered when the market price falls to or below your stop price.
    
*   Short (Buy) → Triggered when the market price rises to or above your stop price.
    

Once triggered, the Stop-Loss order converts into either a Market or Limit order based on your chosen execution type.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/stop-loss#execution-types)

Execution Types

**Stop-Market Order**

*   Once triggered, the order converts to a market order, executing immediately at the best available price.
    
*   The execution price may differ from your stop price due to slippage or price gaps, particularly in fast-moving markets.
    
*   Partial fills may occur depending on liquidity at the time of execution.
    

**Stop-Limit Order**

*   Once triggered, the order becomes a limit order at your specified limit price.
    
*   Provides price control, but execution is not guaranteed — if the market continues to move away from your limit price, the order may remain unfilled and fail to provide stop-loss protection.
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/stop-loss#parameter-recommendations)

Parameter Recommendations

Long (Sell)

Trigger Price ≥ Limit Price

Setting the limit price slightly below the trigger price increases the chance of execution during downward movement.

Short (Buy)

Trigger Price ≤ Limit Price

Setting the limit price slightly above the trigger price increases the chance of execution during upward movement.

circle-info

**Tip**: Adding a small “spread” between your trigger and limit prices helps improve execution reliability while maintaining price control.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/stop-loss#trigger-price-source)

Trigger Price Source

Choose which price source triggers your Stop-Loss order:

*   **Mark Price** — Reduces the chance of false triggers caused by short-term price spikes.
    
*   **Last Traded Price** — More sensitive to rapid market movements but may trigger early during volatility.
    
*   **Index Price** — Reflects an aggregated market rate and offers stability during abnormal price fluctuations.
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/stop-loss#risk-reminder)

Risk Reminder

*   During extreme volatility, Stop-Market orders may execute far from your trigger price.
    
*   Stop-Limit orders may fail to execute if the market price moves past the limit price too quickly.
    
*   In both cases, your position may remain open longer than expected, increasing potential losses.
    
*   For most users, Mark Price triggers offer the best balance between stability and timely activation.
    

circle-info

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss/stop-loss#for-details-on-how-to-set-a-stop-loss-order-please-refer-to-this-page)

👉**For details on how to set a stop loss order, please refer to** [**this page.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#how-to-correctly-set-take-profit-and-stop-loss-orders-for-open-positions)

# Using Grid Bots on Web | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#how-to-set-up-a-grid-bot)

How to Set Up a Grid Bot？

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#step-1-create-a-grid-bot)

Step 1: Create a Grid Bot

1.  Log in to your exchange account.
    
2.  Click on your **account avatar**.
    
3.  Click the **"+" icon** next to Bot Accounts.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FEoJv8mWNaGdJ1z4x3Gpz%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D1584c0d5-e35c-4fe6-a1ee-de3b42774a83&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=5aa6b99f&sv=2)

1.  Enter a name for your bot (e.g., "SOL Grid Strategy").
    
2.  Click **Add subaccount**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FwSTHQezdQx56AYM9HPPh%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D46d44c4b-0303-42e8-aa76-67f037aa6f60&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c4dbde1d&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#step-2-fund-your-bot)

Step 2: Fund Your Bot

Transfer funds to your bot subaccount using one of these methods:

*   [Transfer funds from your main account](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#deposit-via-subaccounts)
    
*   [Deposit from an external wallet into the subaccount.](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit)
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#step-3-select-the-trading-pair)

Step 3: Select the Trading Pair

1.  Enter your bot subaccount.
    
2.  Navigate to the **Bots** section.
    
3.  Choose **Futures Trading**.
    
4.  Select your target trading pair (e.g., SOL-PERP).
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FN33SudZi5sjmmpi8pjC6%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D5d4f368d-258f-4139-99cd-ea0a05d39df7&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=9083d094&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#step-4-configure-parameters)

Step 4: Configure Parameters

1.  Set the **Price Lower Limit** — The lowest price in your grid range.
    
2.  Set the **Price Upper Limit** — The highest price in your grid range.
    
3.  Enter the **Number of Grids** — How many price levels to create within the range.
    
4.  Enter the **Total Grid Value** — The capital allocated to this grid.
    
5.  Click **Create Grid** — The bot will begin running automatically.
    

> **Note:** More grids = more frequent trades with smaller price differences between levels. Fewer grids = less frequent trades with larger price differences between levels.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F2YFfgiPpER0nt78PaV25%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd761b69c-3dfd-4743-a559-fd98f2cdd980&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=50e6c7fa&sv=2)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#take-profit-and-stop-loss-settings)

Take-Profit and Stop-Loss Settings

> ⚠️ **Important:** Take-profit and stop-loss apply to the **entire bot subaccount**, not to individual grids.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#stop-loss)

Stop-Loss

Defines a drawdown threshold that will stop the bot when reached.

**How it works:**

*   The bot tracks the starting equity of your subaccount from the moment the strategy is activated.
    
*   If equity falls X% below the starting equity, the bot stops automatically.
    

**Example:** Starting equity is $1,500. With a 20% stop-loss setting, the bot stops if equity drops to $1,200.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FW4wAcIQZcDwCtBxVqTfb%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd5c446c6-0322-4e83-ae41-693f1128200f&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=2416b9ae&sv=2)

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#take-profit)

Take-Profit

Defines an equity threshold that will pause the bot when reached.

**How it works:**

*   The bot tracks the starting equity of your subaccount from the moment the strategy is activated.
    
*   If equity rises X% above the starting equity, the bot pauses automatically.
    

**Example:** Starting equity was $1,000. With a 20% take-profit setting, the bot pauses when equity reaches $1,200.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FhpAeFTdpNjjcBeoud0S8%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dfa473f45-6a16-42c4-b3ea-ce55d53ac75b&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=38df969e&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#auto-close-positions)

Auto-Close Positions

When setting up take-profit or stop-loss, you can choose whether to **automatically close all positions** when either threshold is triggered.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FpZx61AWQiIPRf6fWBt1P%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D5a0b7faa-d303-40fe-b8dc-798157ee6b25&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=1b62c65a&sv=2)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#bot-management)

Bot Management

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#understanding-bot-on-off-states)

Understanding Bot ON/OFF States

The ON/OFF toggle controls whether the bot is actively managing trades for the entire subaccount.

State

What Happens

ON

The bot actively executes trades according to your grid configurations. **Important:** You cannot manually trade in this subaccount while the bot is ON.

OFF

The bot stops executing new orders. Existing pending orders remain but can be cancelled manually. You can trade manually in the subaccount.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FXTZYLDLYBuEcMaxaUfRJ%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De4ae3c83-413c-4c76-bcdb-90678f117310&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=e023438b&sv=2)

> ⚠️ **Key Point:** If the bot is ON but you have no active grids, no trading will occur. The bot needs at least one grid to execute trades.

> ⚠️ **Manual Trading Restriction:** You cannot manually place orders in a bot subaccount while the bot is ON. Turn the bot OFF first if you need to intervene manually.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#adjusting-grid-settings)

Adjusting Grid Settings

To modify an active grid:

1.  Go to the **Grids** tab.
    
2.  Select **Edit**.
    
3.  Adjust parameters such as:
    
    *   Expanding or narrowing the price range
        
    *   Pausing or restarting the grid
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FcO0zXpgSbuc7FG4zu5X0%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D183be48b-7b73-4dd8-a1af-e5a153fc392a&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a27114fe&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web#deleting-a-grid)

Deleting a Grid

1.  Go to the **Grids** tab.
    
2.  Select **Delete**.
    
3.  Choose whether to close all remaining positions automatically.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fn8Oyrb3C4EyyTzLWJpIZ%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D86a28add-adf3-45e4-8ad5-71e87bc26c33&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8cd13f7c&sv=2)

# Interest Rates | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/interest-rates#what-are-interest-rates)

**What Are Interest Rates?**

**Interest rates** represent the cost of borrowing assets or the yield earned by lenders who supply liquidity. Rates are typically **variable** and adjust dynamically based on market conditions, primarily driven by **Utilization** (the percentage of funds in the pool that have been borrowed).

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/interest-rates#how-are-interest-rates-determined)

**How Are Interest Rates Determined?**

1.  **Utilization-Based Model**
    
    *   When **Utilization is low**, borrowing demand is weak → interest rates are lower.
        
    *   When **Utilization is high**, borrowing demand is strong → interest rates increase to encourage repayment and attract more deposits.
        
    
2.  **Borrower Interest Rate**
    
    *   This is the rate borrowers pay on borrowed assets.
        
    *   The rate adjusts dynamically depending on the pool’s Utilization level.
        
    
3.  **Lender (Deposit) Interest Rate**
    
    *   This is the yield lenders earn by supplying assets.
        
    *   It is derived from the borrowing interest rate, after accounting for the exchange/protocol’s fee (if applicable).
        
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/interest-rates#why-do-interest-rates-change)

**Why Do Interest Rates Change?**

*   **Market Supply & Demand:** More borrowing increases rates, while higher deposits reduce rates.
    
*   **Liquidity Conditions:** High utilization raises rates to incentivize lenders to supply more liquidity.
    
*   **Platform Risk Management:** Dynamic adjustments help maintain balance and protect against liquidity shortages.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/interest-rates#how-can-i-view-the-current-lending-rates)

How can I view the current lending rates?

1.  Click **“Lend”** to enter the Lending page.
    
2.  Open the **token selection dropdown menu**.
    
3.  You will then be able to view the **real-time borrow and lend rates** for different tokens.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FxN7g5Hi1tf3ybBos65AL%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D7b54daa8-126e-43c0-a84e-9432376a3f1c&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=621991f8&sv=2)

# Utilization | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/utilization#what-is-utilization)

**What Is Utilization?**

**Utilization** refers to the percentage of total available funds in a lending pool that have been borrowed by users. It reflects how much of the pool’s liquidity is currently in use.

**Formula:**

Utilization=Total Borrowed / Total Lent × 100%

For example:

*   If a pool has **1,000 USDT deposited** and **800 USDT borrowed**,
    
*   Then **Utilization = 80%**.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/utilization#why-is-utilization-important)

**Why Is Utilization Important?**

*   **For Lenders:**
    
    *   High utilization means more of their funds are actively lent out, potentially earning **higher interest.**
        
    *   Very high utilization, however, may **limit withdrawal flexibility**, as most funds are borrowed.
        
    
*   **For Borrowers:**
    
    *   High utilization can lead to **higher interest rates**, as demand for liquidity is strong.
        
    *   Low utilization generally means **lower borrowing costs** and easier access to funds.
        
    
*   **For the Exchange:**
    
    *   Utilization helps balance supply and demand in the lending market.
        
    *   It influences the **dynamic interest rate model** — interest rates often increase when utilization is high, encouraging repayment and new deposits.
        
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/utilization#key-parameters)

**Key Parameters**

*   **Optimal Utilization:** The point where interest rates begin to increase rapidly to encourage more lending and loan repayments.
    
*   **Throttled Utilization:** The utilization rate threshold where lend redeems and new borrows are paused to prevent 100% utilization.
    
*   **Max Utilization:** The maximum utilization rate threshold where no lend redeems or new borrows are allowed, and positions may be automatically deleveraged.
    

circle-info

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/utilization-interest-rates-risks/utilization#learn-more-about-utilization)

**👉**[**Learn more about** Utilization](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/borrow-and-lend/utilization-and-interest-rates)

# Points | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points#backpack-exchange-points-program)

**Backpack Exchange Points Program**

*   **Season Timeline**
    
    *   Season 1: March 21, 2025 – May 29, 2025
        
    *   Season 2: July 3, 2025 - September 10, 2025
        
    *   Season 3: September 11, 2025 - November 19, 2025
        
    *   Season 4: Started on November 20, 2025
        
    
*   **Points Distribution**
    
    *   Points are distributed weekly, based on user activity across all products.
        
    *   Criteria for points are continuously updated and intentionally opaque.
        
    *   Goal: Reward users who contribute to Backpack’s growth.
        
    *   Distribution schedule:
        
        *   Every Friday at 02:00 UTC
            
        *   Based on activity ending Thursday at 00:00 UTC
            
        
    *   Backpack reserves the right to adjust previous point distributions.
        
    
*   **Ranks**
    
    *   Ranks are tied to each season.
        
    *   When a new season begins, all users start from zero.
        
    *   Rank tiers: **Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Challenger**
        
    
*   **Special Drops**
    
    *   **Legacy Drop**: Snapshot taken on March 20, 2025, at ~09:00 UTC, allocated to historical users since late 2023.
        
    *   **Bonus Drop**: 10 million points distributed to active users during the off-season between Season 1 and Season 2.
        
    
*   **Affiliate Point Matching**
    
    *   Qualified affiliates earn elevated points based on the activity of referred users.
        
    *   Granted at Backpack’s discretion.
        
    
*   **User Access**
    
    *   View your points and rank: [https://backpack.exchange/pointsarrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/points)
        
    *   Get started: Sign up at [https://backpack.exchange/arrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/)
         or download the Backpack app [herearrow-up-right](https://backpack.app/download)
        .

# FAQs | Backpack Exchange

chevron-right**When should you use TWAP?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#when-should-you-use-twap)

*   When placing a large order that might cause price slippage
    
*   When markets are volatile and you want to average your entry/exit
    
*   When you want to reduce visibility to bots or other traders
    

chevron-right**Will using the TWAP strategy incur additional fees?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#will-using-the-twap-strategy-incur-additional-fees)

Using the TWAP strategy does **not** incur additional fees. Users only pay standard trading fees based on actual matched trades.

chevron-right**Is a longer running time and higher frequency always better?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#is-a-longer-running-time-and-higher-frequency-always-better)

Not necessarily. Choose execution duration and frequency based on:

**Setting**

**Advantages**

**Risks**

**Longer duration**

\- Lower market impact - Closer to average market price

\- Exposed to longer volatility - Slower completion

**Shorter duration**

\- Faster execution - Useful for fast markets

\- Higher slippage risk - Needs deeper liquidity

**Higher frequency**

\- Smoother execution - Better for high-frequency price moves

\- High system request rate - More demand on account/data updates

**Lower frequency**

\- Simpler operations - Potentially lower fees (per order)

\- Less smooth - Higher chance of missing market opportunities

chevron-righta**re TWAP orders guaranteed to execute?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#are-twap-orders-guaranteed-to-execute)

No. TWAP orders are executed based on market liquidity and may not fill completely if market conditions change.

chevron-right**Are executed TWAP orders considered market or limit orders?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#are-executed-twap-orders-considered-market-or-limit-orders)

TWAP orders are split into smaller **market orders** when executed. Each slice follows the current best bid/ask price with slight adjustments based on your randomization settings.

chevron-right**Can TWAP run across multiple pairs or markets simultaneously?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#can-twap-run-across-multiple-pairs-or-markets-simultaneously)

No. Each TWAP strategy instance supports **only one trading pair**. To use TWAP across multiple pairs, create **separate strategy instances**.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F6vBIRL6VsphEUTNf5zni%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Defaf9225-71ac-46d3-bd0a-c53cbfbd84a2&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=23f0f7c2&sv=2)

chevron-right**Under what circumstances could a TWAP strategy fail or be interrupted?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#under-what-circumstances-could-a-twap-strategy-fail-or-be-interrupted)

TWAP may fail or terminate early due to:

*   **Insufficient account balance** (Cannot place next sub-order)
    
*   **Manual cancellation by the user**
    
*   **System errors or network issues** (Maintenance, data issues, or connectivity disruptions)
    

> After interruption, no new orders will be placed. Check your **strategy history** for filled and unfilled parts.

chevron-right**Does TWAP guarantee execution at or better than the target average price?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#does-twap-guarantee-execution-at-or-better-than-the-target-average-price)

No. TWAP's goal is to **approximate** the time-weighted average price. However, due to real-time **price fluctuations** and **liquidity changes**, final prices may deviate. The platform **does not guarantee** a specific execution price.

> Proper configuration of time, frequency, and price control parameters can help minimize deviation.

chevron-right**How to view your order history**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#how-to-view-your-order-history)

**View Your Order History on Web**

1.  On the order entry page, select **‘TWAP’**
    
2.  Select **‘Running’**（To view past orders, click the **‘History’** tab on the right）
    
3.  Click **‘Details’** to view your strategy execution history
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FnavBd7cLPmRMa8n3n6do%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D4c17ee11-d305-462d-a6fe-ebde280cbcd2&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8dbfb2da&sv=2)

**View Your Order History on Mobile**

1.  On the order entry page, select **‘TWAP’**
    
2.  Select **‘Running’**（To view past orders, click the **‘History’** tab on the right）
    
3.  Click **‘Details’** to view your strategy execution history
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F2JS0gVRY2Vq4Gsgc8ioY%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D12c1344b-228d-42bf-8756-1b0726811a59&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c13af8a9&sv=2)

chevron-right**How to terminate your TWAP strategy**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/faqs#how-to-terminate-your-twap-strategy)

**Terminate Your TWAP Strategy on Web**

1.  On the order entry page, select **‘TWAP’**
    
2.  Click **‘Cancel’** to immediately stop the strategy (Unfilled sub-orders will be withdrawn)
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FLXYVwLXzMoHZJbT38mLK%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D36efd2c9-93e6-4853-a5f5-4b32a5ef4f9f&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=ff293df3&sv=2)

**Terminate Your TWAP Strategy on Mobile**

1.  On the order entry page, select **‘TWAP’**
    
2.  Click **‘Cancel’** to immediately stop the strategy (Unfilled sub-orders will be withdrawn)
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FsvhxHOGwgf2FN6Rz8ptl%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De1135e98-5ce2-4755-ae3c-9661e5772b3c&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=1696e53b&sv=2)

# Charts, candlesticks, Indicators | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/charts-candlesticks-indicators#overview)

**Overview**

When participating in crypto lending and borrowing, it is important to monitor market trends and assess risks. Exchanges provide **charts, candlestick views, and trading indicators** to help users analyze asset price movements, evaluate borrowing opportunities, and manage lending risks more effectively.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/charts-candlesticks-indicators#id-1.-charts)

**1\. Charts**

Charts provide a **visual representation of price and market activity** for each cryptocurrency. They help users:

*   Track historical price trends.
    
*   Identify support and resistance levels.
    
*   Monitor volatility, which may impact interest rates and borrowing risk.
    

**Key Tip:** For lenders, monitoring charts helps assess the stability of the asset being lent. For borrowers, it helps time entry and exit to avoid borrowing during highly volatile conditions.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/charts-candlesticks-indicators#id-2.-candlesticks)

**2\. Candlesticks**

A **candlestick chart** shows how the price of an asset has moved during a specific time interval (e.g., 1 minute, 1 hour, 1 day). Each candlestick contains four key data points:

*   **Open** – The price at the beginning of the interval.
    
*   **Close** – The price at the end of the interval.
    
*   **High** – The highest price reached during the interval.
    
*   **Low** – The lowest price reached during the interval.
    

**Usage in Lending & Borrowing:**

*   Candlesticks help identify short-term vs. long-term market trends.
    
*   Sharp price movements may signal higher borrowing risk (liquidation risk).
    
*   Stable price patterns suggest safer lending or borrowing conditions.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/charts-candlesticks-indicators#id-3.-indicators)

**3\. Indicators**

Indicators are **technical analysis tools** applied to charts that help interpret market conditions. Commonly used indicators include:

*   **Moving Averages (MA / EMA):** Show overall trend direction. Helpful to decide when to borrow or repay.
    
*   **Relative Strength Index (RSI):** Measures overbought or oversold conditions. Can indicate potential price reversals.
    
*   **Bollinger Bands:** Reflect price volatility; wide bands suggest high volatility, which may increase liquidation risk for borrowers.
    
*   **Volume Indicators:** Show trading activity; high volume may mean stronger support/resistance levels.
    

**Usage in Lending & Borrowing:**

*   Borrowers can use indicators to avoid entering positions in overheated markets.
    
*   Lenders can use them to gauge market sentiment before supplying assets.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/concepts-and-tools/charts-candlesticks-indicators#key-points-to-remember)

**Key Points to Remember**

*   Charts and candlesticks **visualize market data**, helping both borrowers and lenders make informed decisions.
    
*   Indicators provide **deeper insights** into trends, momentum, and volatility.
    
*   Monitoring these tools helps **reduce risks**, such as unexpected liquidations for borrowers or asset devaluation for lenders.

# Rank Referral Program (Points) | Backpack Exchange

The Rank Referral Program rewards top badge holders with a percentage match of the points earned by their referred users.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#eligibility)

Eligibility

*   **Who qualifies: Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Challenger** (Season 2 only)
    
*   **What you earn:** a weekly points match on the points your referrals generate
    
*   **First distribution:** **Week 8 of Season 2** (included in the standard weekly points payout)
    
*   **Available to Affiliate participants.** If you're already enrolled in the Affiliates program, you're still eligible.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#match-rates-season-2)

Match Rates (Season 2)

*   **Gold:** 2%
    
*   **Platinum:** 3%
    
*   **Diamond:** 4%
    
*   **Challenger:** 5%
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#how-it-works)

How it works

1.  A user signs up with your **referral code** and completes KYC.
    
2.  Each week, your referred users earn points based on their activity.
    
3.  You receive a percentage match (per the rates above) on those points. The match is included in the weekly points distribution.
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#example)

Example

If your referred user earns **10,000 points** in a week and your rank is **Diamond**, you receive **4% = 400 points** that week.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#notes-and-policies)

Notes & Policies

*   **Season-specific:** Only **Season 2** ranks are eligible.
    
*   **Affiliate exclusion:** Users in the **Affiliates** program are **not** eligible for Rank Referral matches.
    
*   **Weekly cadence:** Matches follow the normal weekly distribution schedule.
    
*   The referral points will be included in the standard 10M weekly allocation.
    
*   **Program discretion:** We may adjust or correct prior distributions at our discretion.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#faqs)

FAQs

chevron-rightAre Affiliates eligible for Rank Referral matches?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#are-affiliates-eligible-for-rank-referral-matches)

Yes. If you're in the **Affiliate** program, you can also receive Rank Referral matches.

chevron-rightWhich ranks count—Season 1 or Season 2?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#which-ranks-count-season-1-or-season-2)

Only **Season 2** ranks count for this program.

chevron-rightWhat qualifies as a "referral"?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points#what-qualifies-as-a-referral)

A user who registers using your referral code and completes KYC. You can find and share your code from the **Referrals** page.

# How to Use the TWAP Strategy | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/how-to-use-the-twap-strategy#use-the-twap-strategy-on-the-web)

Use the TWAP strategy on the web


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/how-to-use-the-twap-strategy#spot-trading-with-twap)

**Spot Trading with TWAP**

1.  Navigate to the **Spot Trading** page
    
2.  Select your desired **trading pair** and **position direction** (Buy/Sell)
    
3.  Choose **‘TWAP’** as your order type
    
4.  Enter required parameters:
    
    *   Total Quantity
        
    *   Running Time
        
    *   Frequency
        
    
5.  Optional settings:
    
    *   Check **‘Randomize’** to enable randomized order execution
        
    *   Check **‘Margin’** to use leverage
        
    
6.  Place your order
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fz0HA51raHglwTpmvPy1l%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D6e72923b-4a7a-4a7c-8f03-82ca59107626&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=20b2b983&sv=2)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/how-to-use-the-twap-strategy#futures-trading-with-twap)

**Futures Trading with TWAP**

1.  Navigate to the **Futures Trading** page
    
2.  Select your desired **trading pair** and **position direction** (Buy/Sell)
    
3.  Choose **‘TWAP’** as your order type
    
4.  Enter required parameters:
    
    *   Total Quantity
        
    *   Running Time
        
    *   Frequency
        
    
5.  Optional settings:
    
    *   Check **‘Randomize’** to enable randomized order execution
        
    *   Check **‘Reduce Only’** to exclusively reduce existing positions
        
    
6.  Place your order
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F7XXYrWcuopAAV6gCPkqM%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dc2a03bba-c6e1-4eb2-87f7-914b6e8c6b5b&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=1fce8003&sv=2)

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/how-to-use-the-twap-strategy#use-the-twap-strategy-on-mobile)

Use the TWAP strategy on mobile


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/how-to-use-the-twap-strategy#spot-trading-with-twap-1)

**Spot Trading with TWAP**

1.  After entering the trading interface via **'Trade'**, click **'Spot'** in the top-left corner
    
2.  Select your desired **trading pair** and **position direction** (Buy/Sell)
    
3.  Choose **‘TWAP’** as your order type
    
4.  Enter required parameters:
    
    *   Total Quantity
        
    *   Running Time
        
    *   Frequency
        
    
5.  Optional settings:
    
    *   Check **‘Randomize’** to enable randomized order execution
        
    *   Check **‘Margin’** to use leverage
        
    
6.  Place your order
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FfM8YMNiEKQi8BRu21aBZ%252F1754996410237.jpg%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D79caa2f6-5035-4e05-ae42-ae8956f0dc84&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=41503764&sv=2)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/twap/how-to-use-the-twap-strategy#futures-trading-with-twap-1)

**Futures Trading with TWAP**

1.  After entering the trading interface via '**Trade'**, click '**Futures'** in the top-left corner
    
2.  Select your desired **trading pair** and **position direction** (Buy/Sell)
    
3.  Choose **‘TWAP’** as your order type
    
4.  Enter required parameters:
    
    *   Total Quantity
        
    *   Running Time
        
    *   Frequency
        
    
5.  Optional settings:
    
    *   Check **‘Randomize’** to enable randomized order execution
        
    *   Check **‘Reduce Only’** to exclusively reduce existing positions
        
    
6.  Place your order
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fnfq71uw3ZcaDEptPdf90%252F1754996572452.jpg%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Db0b91e08-c77c-4dbc-82c5-e5051e71104f&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=54e2ba72&sv=2)

# Futures | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#why-choose-backpack-for-futures-trading)

Why choose Backpack for futures trading?


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Unified Cross-Margin Account**
    
*   **Competitive trading fees**
    
*   **Passive Yield from Auto Lending**
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#trading-futures-on-the-web)

Trading Futures on the Web


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
*   **Navigate to the Futures Trading section**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., SOL-PERP)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
*   **Select the** [**order type.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types)
    
*   **Enter the required parameters**
    
    *   Price
        
    *   Quantity
        
    *   ... (and other necessary parameters)
        
    
*   **Optional settings**
    
    *   **Post Only**: Ensures your order is placed as a maker order and does not take liquidity.
        
    *   **IOC (Immediate or Cancel)**: Executes the order immediately as much as possible; any unfilled portion is canceled. Suitable for quick entries and exits.
        
    *   **Reduce Only**: Ensures your order will only reduce your existing position and will not open a new position or increase the size of the current position.
        
    *   **TP/SL (Take Profit/Stop Loss)**: Sets predefined price levels at which your position will be automatically closed to lock in profits (TP) or limit losses (SL).
        
    *   ... (and other Optional settings)
        
    
*   **Submit the order.**
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#trading-futures-on-mobile)

Trading Futures on Mobile


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Deposit funds into your exchange account (crypto or fiat).**
    
    *   [Crypto Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit#how-to-deposit)
        
    *   [Fiat Deposits](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/fiat/how-to-deposit-via-wire)
        
    
*   **Navigate to the Futures Trading section**
    
    *   Select your desired trading pair (e.g., SOL-PERP)
        
    *   Choose the trading direction (Buy/Sell)
        
    
*   **Select the** [**order type.**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types)
    
*   **Enter the required parameters**
    
    *   Price
        
    *   Quantity
        
    *   ... (and other necessary parameters)
        
    
*   **Optional settings**
    
    *   **Post Only**: Ensures your order is placed as a maker order and does not take liquidity.
        
    *   **IOC (Immediate or Cancel)**: Executes the order immediately as much as possible; any unfilled portion is canceled. Suitable for quick entries and exits.
        
    *   **Reduce Only**: Ensures your order will only reduce your existing position and will not open a new position or increase the size of the current position.
        
    *   **TP/SL (Take Profit/Stop Loss)**: Sets predefined price levels at which your position will be automatically closed to lock in profits (TP) or limit losses (SL).
        
    *   ... (and other Optional settings)
        
    
*   **Submit the order.**
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

chevron-rightHow can I view the PnL of a specific position?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#how-can-i-view-the-pnl-of-a-specific-position)

*   On the **Order** page or the **Portfolio** page, locate the open position you want to review.
    
*   Hover your mouse over **“PnL (ROI)”** to see how PnL is calculated under different price benchmarks and the corresponding amounts.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FkRqJP9gzCDerrIxbORji%252Fad18225c22793f5c89a4aeb27bb94e6f.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D867739c5-24bf-48ea-ae73-02bf4a16332f&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=be1575ff&sv=2)

*   Hover over the numbers below to view the **Open PnL**, **Closed PnL**, and the **All Time PnL** for that position.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FSIcanju6A3VXs4utw1qz%252Fd2c010cd130a1b040ae91bb0e375b7f5.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd4061929-3dda-4c15-8cbc-459dd9eedc54&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3dafad68&sv=2)

chevron-rightHow to correctly set take-profit and stop-loss orders for open positions[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#how-to-correctly-set-take-profit-and-stop-loss-orders-for-open-positions)

On the **Order Placement** page or the **Portfolio** page, locate the open position you want to manage and click the **Settings** button under the **TP/SL** tab.

*   **For closing the entire position:**
    
    *   Select **“Entire Position.”**
        
    *   From the dropdown menu, **set the benchmark for the trigger price.**
        
    *   Enter the trigger prices for take-profit and stop-loss, then click **Confirm** to complete the **TP/SL market order** setup.
        
    *   **If you prefer a limit order trigger**, check **“Limit Price,”** fill in the required parameters, and then click **Confirm.**
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fe7cAj1smr7QYKwzh0XA8%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De9dacce5-8046-4152-8807-78414e3d46c7&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=d584f7d0&sv=2)

*   **For partially closing a position:**
    
    *   Select **“Partial Position.”**
        
    *   Manually input or drag the slider to set the **quantity** you want to close.
        
    *   From the dropdown menu, **set the benchmark for the trigger price.**
        
    *   Enter the trigger prices for take-profit and stop-loss, then click **Confirm** to complete the **TP/SL market order** setup.
        
    *   **If you prefer a limit order trigger,** check **“Limit Price,”** fill in the required parameters, and then click **Confirm.**
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Ffs95ILX2ia2qM4OptNX1%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd7e7f49a-a490-4561-b599-f85d07f83e9c&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=1c9fea25&sv=2)

For more details on TP/SL, please refer to [the documentation here.](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/order-types/take-profit-and-stop-loss)

chevron-right**How can I view and export my trading history?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#how-can-i-view-and-export-my-trading-history)

**Completed Order History**

*   Go to **Portfolio** → under the **Trades** tab, select **Fills**.
    
*   From the dropdown menu on the right, choose the corresponding token to view completed orders.
    
*   Hover your mouse over **“13 Days Ago”** to see the exact completion timestamp of the order.
    
*   If you need to export data for analysis, click the cloud icon under **Export**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FlXb7YzasvkO6aRLLFuwl%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Daffd2737-384c-459a-9668-b9c38cb15df7&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=92a02159&sv=2)

* * *

**Order Placement History**

*   Go to **Portfolio** → under the **Trades** tab, select **Order History**.
    
*   From the dropdown menu on the right, choose the corresponding token to view your order placements and the price levels at which they were submitted.
    
*   Hover your mouse over **“13 Days Ago”** to see the detailed timestamp of the order placement.
    
*   If you need to export data for analysis, click the cloud icon under **Export**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F0wpKqzuCSkZ2rDxD0MbJ%252F6b309b21-1f4f-40cc-b1e3-a485d441091f.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D8dc61259-4304-4229-b15b-7f755cb499e3&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=881cd6ef&sv=2)

chevron-right**How to view my liquidation history?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/futures#how-to-view-my-liquidation-history)

please visit [https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/futures/liquidationsarrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/futures/liquidations?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
