# FAQ - よくある質問 - | Backpack Exchange

This page answers frequently asked questions about asset handling and the Borrow / Lend features for Japan-based accounts. If you have questions about withdrawal rules or borrowing and lending, please review this page first. このページでは、借入／貸出機能および資産の取扱いについて、よくある質問をまとめています。 出金ルールや借入／貸出に関する疑問がある場合は、まずこちらをご確認ください。

chevron-rightQ1. When is Auto Lend enabled? **自動レンディングはいつ有効になりますか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q1-when-is-auto-lend-enabled-rendinguhaitsuninarimasuka)

**Eligible assets are automatically lent out when they are deposited.** Auto Lend is always applied as **part of the asset handling rules** and cannot be toggled on or off by the user.

* * *

**対象となる資産は、入金された時点から自動的にレンディングの対象となります。**

この自動レンディングは、**内部的には貸付（ローン）として管理されており**、 **資産の取扱いルールとして常に適用されています。**

そのため、**自動レンディングを個別に ON / OFF することはできません。**

chevron-rightQ2. What is the maturity date for each asset? **入金した資産の満期日（出金可能日）はいつですか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q2-what-is-the-maturity-date-for-each-asset-shitanohaitsudesuka)

The maturity date for all eligible assets is **7 days**. During this period, the asset is considered **lent under a loan** and **cannot be withdrawn until the loan matures**. This loan is calculated **on a per-subaccount and per-asset basis**.

* * *

**対象となるすべての資産の満期日は、入金時点から7日間です。**

この期間中、当該資産は**自動レンディング（貸出）の対象**となり、 **出金することはできません（出金不可期間）**。

この貸出は、**内部的には「貸付（ローン）」として管理**されています。

**ロック期間および貸付の管理は、「サブアカウントごと × 資産ごと」に個別に行われます。**

chevron-rightQ3. What happens to the maturity date if I make an additional deposit? **追加入金をすると、満期日（出金可能日）はどうなりますか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q3-what-happens-to-the-maturity-date-if-i-make-an-additional-deposit-wosurutohadounarimasuka)

Each additional deposit of the same asset into the same subaccount **creates a new 7-day maturity loan**, restarting the maturity period. As a result, the asset **cannot be withdrawn until the most recent loan has matured**.

**Example:**

*   Day 0: Deposit BTC
    
*   Day 3: Deposit additional BTC → A new 7-day maturity loan starts on Day 3 → BTC cannot be withdrawn until Day 10 → On Day 10, the entire balance becomes withdrawable once the loan matures
    

Once the 7-day maturity period ends, the asset **automatically becomes withdrawable**. **No user action, renewal, or reconfirmation is required.**

* * *

同一のサブアカウントにおいて、同じ資産を追加入金した場合、 **直近の入金時点を起点として、満期日があらためて7日後に設定されます。**

この満期日を迎えるまで、当該資産は出金することができません（出金不可期間）。

**例：**

初日：BTC を入金 3日目：BTC を追加入金 → **3日目時点で、満期日があらためて7日後（10日目）に再設定** → **BTC は 10日目まで出金不可** → **10日目に、満期到来後、全額が出金可能**

満期日を迎えると、当該資産は**自動的に出金可能な状態**になります。 **ユーザー側での操作、更新、再同意は一切不要です。**

※ この出金不可期間は、貸出（自動レンディング）に伴い、**内部的には貸付（ローン）の満期として管理**されています。

chevron-rightQ4. Can I withdraw assets that I have borrowed? **借入した資産は出金できますか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q4-can-i-withdraw-assets-that-i-have-borrowed-shitahadekimasuka)

Yes, borrowed assets can be withdrawn. If you use a deposited asset as collateral **to borrow a different asset, the borrowed asset is withdrawable.**

Example: Deposit BTC (BTC is locked for 7 days) Use BTC as collateral to borrow SOL → **SOL can be withdrawn**

* * *

はい、出金できます。

入金した資産を担保にして**別の資産を借入した場合、その借入資産は出金可能**です。

**例：**

*   BTC を入金（BTC は7日間出金不可）
    
*   BTC を担保に SOL を借入
    
*   **SOL は出金可能**
    

chevron-rightQ5. How do I borrow assets? **借入はどのように行いますか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q5-how-do-i-borrow-assets-hadonoyouniimasuka)

To borrow assets, go to the **Withdrawal screen** and select the currency that you wish to borrow in the amount field. Ensure that **\[Margin\] is enabled in the bottom-left corner** of the screen.

After selecting the currency, the following information will be displayed at the bottom of the screen:

*   Borrowable amount
    
*   Applicable interest rate
    

* * *

借入を行う場合は、**出金画面**に移動し、数量欄で**借入したい通貨**を選択してください。 あわせて、画面左下にある **［Margin］ が有効になっていること**を確認してください。

通貨を選択すると、画面下部に以下の情報が表示されます。

*   借入可能な数量
    
*   適用される金利
    

chevron-rightQ6. **How does repayment work for borrowed assets?** **借入した資産の返済はどうなりますか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q6-how-does-repayment-work-for-borrowed-assets-shitanohadounarimasuka)

If you make a deposit while you have an outstanding borrowed balance, **the deposited asset will automatically be applied toward repayment.** No manual repayment action is required from the user.

* * *

借入残高がある状態で入金を行うと、**入金された資産は自動的に返済に充当**されます。

ユーザー側で返済操作を行う必要はありません。

chevron-right**Q7. What deposit methods are available? 　　どのような入金方法を利用できますか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q7-what-deposit-methods-are-availabledonoyounawodekimasuka)

The following deposit methods are available:

*   **Crypto deposits**
    
*   **Debit Card Deposit (Banxa)** Some users may experience failed card payments when using Banxa (including Apple Pay / Google Pay). Common causes include temporary account restrictions triggered by fraud detection (error code 227), missing billing address information (especially the city field), or processing limitations on the bank or payment processor side. In such cases, the decision is made by the bank or payment processor, and unfortunately these issues are not directly controllable by us or Banxa.
    

* * *

以下の入金方法をご利用いただけます。

*   **暗号資産による入金**
    
*   **カード入金（Banxa）** 一部のユーザーにおいて、Banxaを利用したカード決済（Apple Pay / Google Pay 含む）が失敗するケースがあります。 主な要因として、不正検知（エラーコード227）による一時的な制限、カードの請求先住所（特に市区町村）の入力漏れ、または銀行・決済代行業者側での処理制限が挙げられます。 これらは銀行または決済代行業者側の判定による場合があり、Backpack、Banxa側で直接コントロールできないケースがあります。
    

chevron-right**Q8. What happens if I deposit an asset that is not supported for Lend / Borrow? 　　貸出に対応していない資産を誤って入金した場合はどうなりますか？**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/faq-yokuaru#q8-what-happens-if-i-deposit-an-asset-that-is-not-supported-for-lend-borrownishiteinaiwotteshitahado)

If a user accidentally deposits an asset that is not supported for Lend, the user must contact Customer Support. ([\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
)

If Customer Support has technical access to the asset, it will be returned to the user on a best-effort basis. This handling is consistent with how most exchanges treat unsupported assets.

**Regarding UI behavior:**

*   **Lend:** The list of lend-eligible assets may change over time and is not maintained as a fixed public list
    
*   Assets that are not eligible for Lend are not proactively hidden in the UI
    

Each case will be handled individually by Customer Support.([\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
)

* * *

貸出に対応していない資産を誤って入金した場合は、カスタマーサポートまでお問い合わせください。 ([\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
)

技術的に対応可能な場合に限り、返却対応を行う場合があります。 この取り扱いは、多くの暗号資産取引所における一般的な対応と同様です。

UI上の挙動について：

*   **貸出：** 貸出対象資産の一覧は固定された公開リストではなく、将来的に変更される可能性があります
    
*   貸出対象外の資産は、UI上で自動的に非表示にはなりません
    

個別のケースについては、カスタマーサポートにて対応いたします。

問い合わせ先：[\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

[Previous借入の自動返済についてchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/for-japanese-users/how-to-use-borrowlend-jp/auto-repay-jp)
[NextTrading Feeschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees)

# Borrowing | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#how-to-borrow)

How to Borrow?

1.  Ensure you have sufficient collateral in your account.
    
2.  Navigate to the Lend section and switch to the Borrow tab.
    
3.  Choose the asset you want to borrow, enter the desired amount, and confirm the transaction.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FBbB6MQpLZYYXVERf1uGC%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dc7c6ef98-4a2e-4295-9470-006527b8f386&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=dd86e02&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions

chevron-right**How much can I borrow on Backpack Exchange?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#how-much-can-i-borrow-on-backpack-exchange)

Your borrowing capacity depends on your remaining available equity—the higher your available equity, the more you can borrow.

This is determined by your subaccount leverage setting, the token’s maximum borrowable leverage under different Notional Borrow Size tiers, and your net margin equity.

No manual calculation is needed—you can easily view the maximum borrowable amount for your sub-account directly under the borrow button on the lending page.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FNozdULX4eiHEEfKhv6ZN%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd3b6a4aa-5589-4d0b-a252-87adaaa35b68&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c4029ce0&sv=2)

chevron-right**Adjusting subaccount leverage for borrowing?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#adjusting-subaccount-leverage-for-borrowing)

1.  On the **Futures** or **Lending** page, go to the **Order Placement** section.
    
2.  Next to **“Cross Margin Overview”**, you will see the leverage indicator. Click on it to adjust the setting.
    
3.  This leverage setting applies as a **global leverage configuration for the subaccount**, affecting **Lending, Futures, and Spot Margin**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FOxd12CAkMk6B2NzKnizv%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D43f01dbc-e249-41b8-b4b1-ffa829864465&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=ea516b12&sv=2)

⚠️**Note:** The selected leverage multiple represents the maximum leverage available to the subaccount. However, **in practice**, it is also **constrained** by the maximum leverage allowed for the specific token in different markets.

chevron-right**Viewing maximum leverage by notional borrow size?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#viewing-maximum-leverage-by-notional-borrow-size)

1.  Click **“Lending”** to enter the Lending page.
    
2.  Under the **“Models”** tab, click **“Margin.”**
    
3.  Hover your mouse over the curve chart to view the leverage multiples for the selected token under different **Notional Borrow Size** tiers.
    
4.  The value shown as **“Max Initial Leverage”** indicates the maximum multiple of the subaccount’s net equity that can be borrowed for that token.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FflV9mKoaPz3lIDicWWpG%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De04e3533-5287-4e70-ac6c-125417e9d949&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3805e678&sv=2)

chevron-right**Why can’t I borrow funds?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#why-cant-i-borrow-funds)

1.  The **initial margin ratio** has reached **100%**, which means you no longer have any available equity to use as collateral for borrowing.You can borrow funds once you've either added more margin or reduced your position size, bringing the initial margin ratio below 100%.
    
2.  When the **auto-lend** feature is **enabled**, borrowing can only occur through spot margin or withdrawals. If you wish to initiate a manual borrow,simply click the lightning icon next to the leverage multiplier to disable the auto-lend feature. Once it’s turned off, you’ll be able to proceed with the manual loan.
    

chevron-right**What fees are associated with borrowing on Backpack Exchange?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#what-fees-are-associated-with-borrowing-on-backpack-exchange)

1.  Once a position is opened, **an entry fee** is immediately charged. This fee is equivalent to **a portion** of the hourly interest.
    

For example, if you collateralize 1 BTC and borrow 2,000 USDC at 12:50 AM, and the next interest calculation occurs at 1:00 AM, then the 10 minutes of accrued interest will be your opening fee.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FHxEEOj0ashIkGD7Wrrgc%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D8f14b308-b3bd-4488-a42f-28e48f2cb869&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8047d80e&sv=2)

1.  **An hourly interest charge** is applied at every hour on the hour. The hourly interest = (Borrowed Amount × Annual Interest Rate) ÷ 365 days ÷ 24 hours.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FmAVHewsnYYpj77Vj9mN1%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dc213e931-fbbf-4333-a7ef-9ed8add177d1&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=55677409&sv=2)

chevron-right**How do I repay a borrow?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#how-do-i-repay-a-borrow)

To repay your borrowed assets:

1.  Go to the **Lend section** and switch to the **Borrow tab.**
    
2.  Select the asset you wish to repay, enter the repayment amount, and confirm the transaction.
    

You can also enable Auto Lend, which will automatically use available balances to repay loans when possible.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fqwjiq6NnJFLNRItQ5cbe%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D63bce891-f4f7-4625-b1fe-4bb2c625b352&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8f653bb1&sv=2)

chevron-right**Can I withdraw my collateral while I have an open borrow position?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#can-i-withdraw-my-collateral-while-i-have-an-open-borrow-position)

Yes, you can withdraw a portion of your collateral, provided that doing so doesn’t lower your margin level below the required thresholds. If withdrawing collateral would put your account at risk of liquidation, the system will prevent the withdrawal.

Always check your margin level before attempting to withdraw.

chevron-right**Can I opt out of using specific assets as collateral?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#can-i-opt-out-of-using-specific-assets-as-collateral)

On Backpack Exchange, your account operates on a cross-margin basis, meaning **all eligible assets** are used as collateral **by default**. There is no option to disable or opt out of using specific assets as collateral within the same account.

The only way to prevent an asset from being used as collateral is to move it to a sub-account, **isolating** it from your main account’s margin positions.

chevron-right**What happens if the value of my collateral drops while I have an open borrow position?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#what-happens-if-the-value-of-my-collateral-drops-while-i-have-an-open-borrow-position)

If the value of your collateral decreases significantly, your margin level will also drop. If it falls below the Maintenance Margin Fraction, your positions will be at risk of **liquidation**.

The system will automatically sell off your collateral to **repay** the borrowed funds.

**To prevent this, monitor your margin level and add more collateral if needed.**

chevron-right**How does the liquidation process work for borrowed funds?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#how-does-the-liquidation-process-work-for-borrowed-funds)

If your **Maintenance Margin Ratio reaches 100%**, the liquidation engine will begin reducing your positions and selling collateral in an orderly manner to cover the borrowed amount. This helps protect both you and the platform.

If your account is liquidated, the engine may sell or convert assets on the order book. A 1% fee is charged for each order-book conversion performed during liquidation

chevron-right**Can I use borrowed funds for trading on Backpack Exchange?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/borrowing#can-i-use-borrowed-funds-for-trading-on-backpack-exchange)

Yes, you can use borrowed funds for both spot and margin trading. This feature provides extra capital to take advantage of market opportunities.

However, using leverage increases your risk, so it’s important to manage your collateral carefully and monitor your margin level to avoid liquidation.

circle-info

# Lending | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#how-can-i-earn-interest-on-my-assets)

**How can I earn interest on my assets?**


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Go to the **Lend section** at the top of your screen
    
2.  Select the **asset** you wish to lend, specify the **amount**, and click Lend.
    
3.  Enable **auto-lending**
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FfdycJWHE1bxiM7H5tQ7n%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D2ba0debf-74a1-4519-832b-bcaaebb05a0a&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=cac68360&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#earn-extra-sol-lending-rewards)

Earn extra SOL lending rewards


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Additional earnings on Backpack come from **staking rewards**. Backpack operates a **validator on the Solana network**, and any block rewards earned through on-chain staking are **passed directly back to users**.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#reward-distribution)

**Reward Distribution**

*   **Staking APY** The staking APY varies depending on the **total amount of SOL staked** on the platform.
    
*   **Reward Schedule** Rewards are typically distributed **every Friday**, based on the on-chain **Solana epoch schedule**. If the current epoch hasn’t ended by Friday, rewards will **roll over** and be included in the **following week’s** distribution.
    
*   **Source of Rewards** Rewards are distributed proportionally and come from:
    
    *   Solana’s **inflationary staking rewards**
        
    *   **Validator block rewards**
        
    *   **Jito MEV tips**
        
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#yield-calculation)

**Yield Calculation**

Yield is calculated based on a user’s share of total lending on the platform **throughout each Solana epoch**.

*   The system takes **multiple snapshots** during each epoch to track:
    
    *   How much each user has lent
        
    *   Relative to the **total lending pool** on the platform
        
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#notes-on-performance-and-variability)

**Notes on Performance & Variability**

*   This is a **best-effort distribution** of staking returns from deployed assets.
    
*   **Actual yields may differ** from advertised rates due to:
    
    *   Validator performance
        
    *   Warm-up and cooldown periods of stake accounts
        
    *   Market and network conditions during the epoch
        
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#how-to-start-earning)

**How to Start Earning?**

To begin earning staking rewards:

1.  **Enable the Auto Lend feature** in your Backpack account.
    
2.  Your **SOL will be automatically staked**—no manual steps needed.
    
3.  While staked:
    
    *   Your SOL continues to **earn rewards**
        
    *   You can still use it as **collateral** for trading or margin purposes
        
    

> This gives users the dual benefit of **passive yield** and **active asset utility**.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FQcEg394QIwyplQiQK89N%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D2f721a73-8719-42cf-b3b7-8fd3893ed119&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=68e06d24&sv=2)

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#earn-extra-usd-lending-rewards)

Earn extra USD lending rewards


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **additional yield** on Backpack’s stablecoin comes from returns generated by the **internal stablecoin pool**.

When you deposit:

*   **Fiat**
    
*   **USDC**
    
*   Or other supported stablecoins (e.g., **pyUSD**, coming soon)
    

...your assets are **automatically converted to Backpack USD** at a **1:1 ratio**.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#what-is-backpack-usd)

**What Is Backpack USD?**

**Backpack USD** is an internal unit of account used **exclusively within the Backpack Exchange**. It is:

*   **Not directly withdrawable**
    
*   Designed to **improve user experience** and **share yield benefits**
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#two-key-purposes)

**Two Key Purposes:**

1.  **Smoother User Experience** Enables **instant, fee-free conversions** between fiat and USDC, with **no manual steps** required.
    
2.  **Sharing Stablecoin Yield** Redistributes the **earnings typically retained by stablecoin issuers**—such as staking rewards or on-chain yield—**back to users**.
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#yield-distribution-details)

**Yield Distribution Details**

*   **Frequency**: Monthly
    
*   **Type**: Extra APY (annual percentage yield) applied to your Backpack USD balance
    
*   **Source**: Earnings from the platform’s internal stablecoin pool
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#how-to-start-earning-1)

**How to Start Earning?**

To earn stablecoin yield:

1.  **Enable the Auto Lend feature** in your account settings
    
2.  Your USD will automatically start **earning interest**, no further action needed
    
3.  While lent, your USD:
    
    *   **Continues earning rewards**
        
    *   **Can still be used as collateral** for trading or margin—giving you both yield and utility
        
    

> **No lockups, no hidden steps—just passive yield while staying fully flexible.**

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXfmPApBCBTIlKFCp5C8MHmzATRD6IG7-HH4_cD-YsbolFizWf1suL-nP4UgiKYVJBGak3DSyZ1YADCBn3BSeitW1Wqfere9hEBtl8ozROdFVCbH9UbncHq9S0K3sLbL26efmqsF%3Fkey%3DwuKTha0gdY6-p825UF2t6Pla&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a9ecf542&sv=2)

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

chevron-right**What are the benefits of lending assets on Backpack Exchange?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#what-are-the-benefits-of-lending-assets-on-backpack-exchange)

1.  Lending assets on Backpack Exchange allows you to earn passive yield with minimal effort. If you enable **Auto-Lend**, your assets will be continuously lent, ensuring they generate interest whenever available.
    
2.  The interest accrues over time and **compounds**, making it an efficient way to put your idle assets to work.
    

chevron-rightHow can I redeem my lended assets on Backpack exchange?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#how-can-i-redeem-my-lended-assets-on-backpack-exchange)

1.  First, make sure the auto-borrow feature is **turned off.**
    
2.  Go to the Lending page, select the corresponding token, click Lend, then **click Redeem.**
    
3.  Enter **the amount** you wish to redeem and confirm the transaction.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FZ7nKepmsmFFKPPi0ICQ1%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D9e32aa21-d6cb-44b4-ac03-73cb6ad3990e&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8f6b103d&sv=2)

chevron-rightHow do I check the [U**tilization Rate**](https://support.backpack.exchange/technical-docs/borrow-and-lend/utilization-and-interest-rates)
 of a lending pool?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/borrow-and-lend/lending#how-do-i-check-the-utilization-rate-of-a-lending-pool)

1.  Click **“Lend”** to enter the Lending page.
    
2.  Select the corresponding token.
    
3.  Under the **“Models”** tab, click **“Interest.”**
    
4.  You will then see the **utilization rate** for that token.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F4rUyoqUpPaGN9txckh2P%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D39549514-37f7-4507-b2a5-53ce65f8c24b&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=e5d3bab8&sv=2)

# Referrals | Backpack Exchange

Registered Exchange members who onboard KYC'd users with their Referral Code will **earn 10%** of the trading fees generated by that new user.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals#how-do-i-refer-someone-to-backpack-exchange)

How do I refer someone to Backpack Exchange?

To invite a new user:

**1\. Locate Your Referral Code**

*   On the web: Go to [backpack.exchange/referralsarrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/referrals)
    .
    
*   On the mobile app: Navigate to your Exchange Account → Points Screen → Referrals tab.
    
    *   _**Customization Tip**__:_ You must have $1,000,000 in lifetime trading volume to customize your code. Otherwise, a default code is assigned.
        
    

**2\. Share Your Code**

*   share the code directly with a friend or community member.
    

**3\. Track Referral Activity**

*   Once they sign up using your code and complete KYC, you’ll start earning referral rebates based on their trading fees.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals#how-do-i-use-a-referral-code)

How do I use a referral code?

**1\. Create a New Account**

*   On desktop: Visit [backpack.exchange/signuparrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/signup)
    .
    
*   On mobile: Download the [Backpack apparrow-up-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/~/revisions/DBo79HudgGfEJIvVhxiq/start-here/downloads)
    , open it, and follow the prompts to create an account.
    

**2\. Enter the Referral Code**

*   During signup, look for the “Add referral” field.
    
*   Input the code you received from your referrer.
    

**3\. Complete KYC**

*   After verifying your identity, you’re all set. Your referrer will begin receiving rebates, and you’ll gain access to all of Backpack Exchange’s features.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals#important-notes)

⚠️Important Notes

*   **Referral Rebate Claims:** Available for up to 24 months after they become eligible.
    
*   **Processing Time:** If your rebate claim hasn’t processed within one day of claiming, reach out to [\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
    
*   **VIP4/5 Rebates (Reinstated)**: Referrals at the VIP4/5 tier do pay rebates so long as the referred user’s API-executed trading is under 25% of their total trading volume for the rebate period.
    
*   **Weekly Updates:** Referral rebates are populated weekly, and trading volume from referrals is not instantly claimable.
    
*   **No Retroactive Adds:** We cannot add referrals for users who have already completed KYC without using your code.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals#important-update-effective-september-29-2025)

Important Update (Effective September 29, 2025)

We’ve reinstated rebates for VIP4/5 referrals with an API-trading threshold.

*   Eligibility is assessed weekly.
    
*   If API-executed trades are ≥25% of a user’s total weekly volume, no rebate is paid for that week.
    
*   Manual/UI trading continues to qualify as usual.
    

If you have any questions regarding this update, please reach out to [\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

[PreviousRank Referral Program (Points)chevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/points/rank-referral-program-points)

# Backpack VIP Program | Backpack Exchange

If you already hold VIP status — or trade significant volume — on **any other exchange**, you can unlock Backpack’s premium perks from day one.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-vip-program#why-move-to-backpack)

Why Move to Backpack?

*   **Ultra-low trading fees** _As low as_ _**0 % maker / 0.025 % taker**_ _on Spot and_ _**0 % maker / 0.018 % taker**_ _on Futures._
    
*   **0-fee global fiat on / off-ramp** Seamless **USD ⇄ USDC 1 : 1** conversions.
    
*   **Attractive yield** on USDC and SOL _while they still count as margin collateral._
    
*   **Dedicated VIP account manager** available 24 / 7.
    
*   Invitations to **private events & exclusive networking**.
    
*   **VIP merchandise** drops and early product previews.
    
*   **Fast-track onboarding** – we mirror your current VIP tier the moment you’re approved.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-vip-program#who-we-invite)

Who We Invite

We extend invitations to traders who can show:

*   **Established VIP status** on another regulated exchange **or**
    
*   **Substantial recent trading volume** (proof required).
    

_Not sure you meet the mark? Apply anyway – every submission is reviewed individually._

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-vip-program#apply-in-two-minutes)

Apply in Two Minutes

> **Step 1 →** [**VIP Application Form**arrow-up-right](https://forms.gle/JH224irteXhgxdFd7)
>  _You’ll be asked for:_
> 
> *   Preferred **contact email**
>     
> *   **Telegram handle**
>     
> *   **Proof of 30-day trading performance or VIP tier** (screenshot / PDF – P2P volume excluded)
>     
> 
> **Step 2 →** Our team reviews and responds.
> 
> **Step 3 →** Once invited, your Backpack VIP perks activate immediately.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-vip-program#need-help)

Need Help?

Email **[\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
** – we’re online 24 / 7.

# Using Grid Bots on Mobile | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#how-to-set-up-a-grid-bot)

How to Set Up a Grid Bot？

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#step-1-create-a-grid-bot)

Step 1: Create a Grid Bot

1.  Log in to your exchange account.
    
2.  Click on your **account avatar**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FGhEyNSkUH0spwsHaE6cs%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De5ee8677-0cfa-49c2-b9cc-94345e1f7eae&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=d9d323a2&sv=2)

1.  Click the **"+" icon** next to Trading Bots.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FluSW6v6ywqjiLCETmetG%252Fa72425a9-0356-453c-b923-1b8b7bdc474a.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D391003b4-863f-444b-b657-7d083f2f37d6&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=37df750c&sv=2)

1.  Enter a name for your bot (e.g., "SOL Grid Strategy").
    
2.  Click **Add subaccount**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F7PjYACedVD2OczGRJcjd%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd4691622-ff60-4eb5-834a-bd6e6c5fa5ba&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=360f3a2&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#step-2-fund-your-bot)

Step 2: Fund Your Bot

Transfer funds to your bot subaccount using one of these methods:

*   [Transfer funds from your main account](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit#deposit-via-subaccounts)
    
*   [Deposit from an external wallet into the subaccount.](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/deposit-and-withdraw/crypto/deposit/how-to-deposit)
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#step-3-activate-the-bot)

Step 3: Activate the Bot

1.  Enter your **bot sub\_account.**
    
2.  Go to the trading interface and select **Futures Trading**.
    
3.  Click the **bot icon** next to the trading pair, then toggle the bot **ON**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FZhHBBMwpw8wVcUXJsiGO%252F259c7d1040c150a3a0032ca284c34082.jpg%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D800decf2-c437-460c-a78b-3efbdd0e2c86&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=53aa435e&sv=2)

* * *

1.  Set your **take-profit and stop-loss** levels.
    

> ⚠️ **Important:** Take-profit and stop-loss apply to the **entire bot subaccount**, not to individual grids.

**Stop-Loss**

Defines a drawdown threshold that will stop the bot when reached.

**How it works:**

*   The bot tracks the starting equity of your subaccount from the moment the strategy is activated.
    
*   If equity falls X% below the starting equity, the bot stops automatically.
    

**Example:** Starting equity is $1,500. With a 20% stop-loss setting, the bot stops if equity drops to $1,200.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FoJVegcRhVIAyrqSXnUGM%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D777ce407-e53d-4914-8408-2e50aff6581e&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=cf64f11e&sv=2)

**Take-Profit**

Defines an equity threshold that will pause the bot when reached.

**How it works:**

*   The bot tracks the starting equity of your subaccount from the moment the strategy is activated.
    
*   If equity rises X% above the starting equity, the bot pauses automatically.
    

**Example:** Starting equity was $1,000. With a 20% take-profit setting, the bot pauses when equity reaches $1,200.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FuKkmArrjcb8qgb8sA4Xp%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D1a4dcdd2-9baf-4b66-b4a8-128734d8afed&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=dad66f7c&sv=2)

* * *

**Auto-Close Positions**

If you want the position to close automatically when a take-profit or stop-loss level is reached, enable **“Close Position on TP/SL.”**

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FiRDQ83vM3tJ3ZJLCTiAa%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Ddb0010d4-e305-4815-acb8-6e1f95bf89a9&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f9d3e6cc&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#step-4-configure-parameters)

Step 4: Configure Parameters

1.  Select your target trading pair (e.g., SOL-PERP) and adjust the **leverage** as needed.
    
2.  Set the **Price Lower Limit** — The lowest price in your grid range.
    
3.  Set the **Price Upper Limit** — The highest price in your grid range.
    
4.  Enter the **Number of Grids** — How many price levels to create within the range.
    
5.  Enter the **Total Grid Value** — The capital allocated to this grid.
    
6.  Click **Create Grid** — The bot will begin running automatically.
    

> **Note:** More grids = more frequent trades with smaller price differences between levels. Fewer grids = less frequent trades with larger price differences between levels.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FhI7LyDI9tQqYPPfIGJqE%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D54124d5f-6b7c-4fd5-bd56-7f7b6a81fa51&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=135ddded&sv=2)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#bot-management)

Bot Management

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#understanding-bot-on-off-states)

Understanding Bot ON/OFF States

The ON/OFF toggle controls whether the bot is actively managing trades for the entire subaccount.

State

What Happens

ON(Active)

The bot actively executes trades according to your grid configurations. **Important:** You cannot manually trade in this subaccount while the bot is ON.

OFF(Inactive)

The bot stops executing new orders. Existing pending orders remain but can be cancelled manually. You can trade manually in the subaccount.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F0bMkezklyoFjfeAaUbFa%252F259c7d1040c150a3a0032ca284c34082.jpg%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dcb759f1b-5432-4939-a45e-6ceb0c556ed5&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=5c9c492d&sv=2)

> ⚠️ **Key Point:** If the bot is ON but you have no active grids, no trading will occur. The bot needs at least one grid to execute trades.

> ⚠️ **Manual Trading Restriction:** You cannot manually place orders in a bot subaccount while the bot is ON. Turn the bot OFF first if you need to intervene manually.

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#adjusting-grid-settings)

Adjusting Grid Settings

To modify an active grid:

1.  Go to the **Grids** tab.
    
2.  Select **Edit**.
    
3.  Adjust parameters such as:
    
    *   Expanding or narrowing the price range
        
    *   Pausing or restarting the grid
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FZYYoTzazISfvxh1sCnvM%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D61dfbf7a-3321-44de-a91a-7a669fb364b1&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=df7eb937&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-mobile#deleting-a-grid)

Deleting a Grid

1.  Go to the **Grids** tab.
    
2.  Select **Delete**.
    
3.  Choose whether to close all remaining positions automatically.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FTDELGw4RSzMUmjoEAABc%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D271a2db2-70a6-4382-995d-c3c0f388bf93&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=82badc74&sv=2)

[PreviousUsing Grid Bots on Webchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-bots/using-grid-bots-on-web)

# Korean Market Compliance Commitment | Backpack Exchange

Backpack remains dedicated to fostering a sustainable and legally compliant digital asset ecosystem. As an exchange that holds licenses in major developed countries, we have always prioritized compliance as the core of our operations. In light of the current regulatory environment, we would like to reiterate our existing policies regarding services and promotional activities in South Korea.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals/korean-market-compliance-commitment#our-unwavering-adherence-to-local-regulations)

Our Unwavering Adherence to Local Regulations

Backpack has consistently monitored and complied with the regulatory guidelines issued by financial authorities in every region we operate. We have long been aware of the advisories concerning virtual asset services targeting domestic users in Korea. Accordingly, it has always been Backpack’s firm policy to market and provide services only in jurisdictions where we are fully aligned with local legal requirements.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals/korean-market-compliance-commitment#reiteration-of-affiliate-and-referral-guidelines)

Reiteration of Affiliate & Referral Guidelines

We would like to remind our affiliates and partners of our long-standing rules regarding the referral program, which remain strictly in effect:

*   **Prohibition of Targeted Marketing** As previously established, all affiliates are strictly prohibited from marketing or promoting Backpack services specifically to the Korean market or Korean residents.
    
*   **Continuous Monitoring** We maintain active monitoring of all promotional channels (Social Media, Telegram, etc.). Any activity targeting Korean users—including the use of Korean-language promotional materials—remains a violation of our terms.
    
*   **Enforcement of Policy** We continue to enforce strict penalties for violations, which include the immediate suspension of affiliate accounts, forfeiture of commissions, and termination of partnerships.
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals/korean-market-compliance-commitment#our-perspective-on-compliance)

Our Perspective on Compliance

We value our community, but our top priority has always been the protection of our users through strict legal compliance. Our record of seeking professional legal counsel and operating within the boundaries of the law reflects our commitment to being a transparent and responsible platform.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals/korean-market-compliance-commitment#reporting-violations)

Reporting Violations

To help us maintain these standards, please continue to report any unauthorized marketing activities targeting Korean users to our support team at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
**.

Backpack will continue to uphold its rigorous standards and work proactively to ensure a secure and compliant trading environment for everyone.

[PreviousReferralschevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/referrals)
[NextAffiliate Programchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/affiliate-program)

# Backpack Captain Program | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-captain-program#who-can-join)

🎯 Who Can Join

Whether you’re a…

*   Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs)
    
*   Channel Partners
    
*   Trading Community Organizers
    
*   YouTubers & Content Creators
    
*   Pro Traders
    
*   Live Streamers
    
*   Or just a loyal Backpack supporter from day one
    

Backpack now officially invites you to join as one of our certified Captains!

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-captain-program#captain-level-system)

🧱 Captain Level System

**5-Tier Growth System with High Commissions from Day One**

⚠️**Important Notes:**

*   All rebates are based on **actual fees** paid by your invited users
    
*   Rewards are settled **weekly**
    
*   Monthly bonus is for reference only — **no earnings guaranteed.**
    
*   **Self-referring is strictly prohibited** and will not be tolerated.
    
*   Each level comes with a **minimum volume expectation**.
    
    *   **Account performance is reviewed monthly** and may be considered for promotion.
        
    *   If the required volume criteria are **not met for 3 consecutive months**, the level **may be reset**.
        
    
*   **Referred users can only be reassigned if they are inactive**.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-captain-program#long-term-incentives-stackable-and-automatic-for-all-captains)

🧭 Long-Term Incentives — Stackable & Automatic for All Captains

Beyond rebates, every Captain can also unlock four long-term incentive plans — no sign-up needed. Just hit the targets, and claim anytime.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-captain-program#id-1.-new-captain-invitation-rewards)

🤝 1. New Captain Invitation Rewards

Top-level Captains can recruit new leaders and earn extra perks:

*   👤 **Criteria**: Successfully refer a new **Lv.4 or higher** Captain
    
*   💸 **Reward**: 200 USDT + 5% commission share from new Captain’s earnings (first 3 months)
    

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/backpack-captain-program#id-2.-growth-boost-for-small-channels-lv.3-and-below-only)

🌱 2. Growth Boost for Small Channels (Lv.3 and Below Only)

Helping new Captains level up from scratch:

*   ⬆️ **Level-Up Reward**: Get 200 USDT cash every time you level up

# What is Backpack Wallet? | Backpack Exchange

Backpack is a **security conscious self-custodial crypto wallet** for Solana, Ethereum, Optimism, Arbitrum, Polygon, Base, Sonic and many more blockchains. It enables you to have complete control over their crypto assets.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/what-is-backpack-wallet#backpack-wallet-is-used-for-all-your-crypto-needs)

**Backpack Wallet is used for all your crypto needs.**


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   sending and receiving crypto assets
    
*   swapping crypto tokens
    
*   staking crypto tokens
    
*   viewing, securing and trading NFTs
    
*   connection with cold storage hardware wallets Ledger, Trezor and Keystone
    
*   multi-sig wallet protection
    
*   connecting to developer testnets
    

[**Download the Backpack wallet for browser, iOS and Android here.**arrow-up-right](https://backpack.app/)

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/what-is-backpack-wallet#backpack-is-a-secure-place-to-store-nfts)

Backpack is a secure place to store NFTs.


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NFT **collection locking** is an easy to use feature that **automatically rejects transaction requests** when an NFT is locked in the Backpack Wallet. Note: Collection locking is currently limited to select projects.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66e0be450f09a6f6779a3aad_66e0be2da1e582580bd26ad5_what_is_backpack_wallet.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=c88c1a30&sv=2)

# Affiliate Program | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/affiliate-program#how-it-works)

How It Works

Affiliates who meet the qualifications below can earn an increased cash rebate on top of the standard 10 % rebate, resulting in higher direct commissions. This program is designed for partners who bring significant and consistent trading activity to Backpack Exchange.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/affiliate-program#eligibility-requirements)

Eligibility Requirements

**Backpack‑Specific**

*   $20 million in **monthly referral volume** on Backpack
    
*   15 **active referred users** per month on Backpack
    
*   5 **new user acquisitions** per month on Backpack
    

**OR**

**External**

*   **More than 5,000** Twitter (X) followers
    
*   **OR** an equivalent volume of referrals on other exchanges
    

_**Note:**_ _These are_ _**minimum requirements**_ _and are s__**ubject to our team’s due diligence**__. Meeting these thresholds does_ _**not**_ _guarantee acceptance into the program._

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/affiliate-program#sign-up)

Sign Up

If you meet these requirements and would like to join, please complete your application by following the steps below:

*   [Log in to your exchange account arrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/)
    and click **“More”**.
    
*   Click **“Affiliate Program”**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FWj7pqPdvdlu7vQdKnMyN%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D30a57a12-e499-4ce5-a415-48bd33c11f69&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f9c1076f&sv=2)

*   Enter your **social media account** name in the corresponding input field.
    
*   Select your **contact platform** in the corresponding field and provide your contact information.
    
*   Click **Submit**.
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FBc3XiYzlOPpyOJnLvNdY%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D46a4df82-ca56-4100-957f-1dd0310085bd&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=9ca2909d&sv=2)

# Get Started | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started#where-to-download-backpack-wallet)

Where to download Backpack Wallet

[Download the Backpack wallet for browser, iOS and Android here.arrow-up-right](https://backpack.app/)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started#how-to-make-a-new-account-on-backpack-wallet)

How to make a new account on Backpack Wallet

This process applies to both mobile and browser extension.

1.  Download the Backpack app or extension
    
2.  Open the application or extension
    
3.  Select '_**Create a new wallet**_'
    
4.  Choose the appropriate blockchain network
    
5.  Note: you can add additional networks to the same account after creating the account
    
6.  Review the Secret Recovery Phrase Warning and confirm
    
7.  Record the Secret Recovery Phrase in a secure location
    
8.  Note: This is the only way to recover your account
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started#how-to-import-an-existing-wallet-to-backpack-wallet)

How to import an existing wallet to Backpack Wallet

1.  Open the Backpack Wallet main menu and select '_**\+ Add Account**_'
    
2.  Select 'Import Wallet'
    
3.  Choose the appropriate blockchain network
    
4.  Pick the appropriate import route
    
    *   Import secret recovery phrase
        
    *   Use a hardware wallet
        
    *   Import private key
        
    *   Add view-only public key
        
    
5.  Follow the specific instructions for each import route

# Mad Lads VIP | Backpack Exchange

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66999c22d74fb908f225e1df_7733bc12-5c3a-4c0b-b131-5341a7365f50.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3688946b&sv=2)

 which holds a Mad Lads NFT to the [**Backpack Exchange**arrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/)
 and qualify for discounted [VIP Tier 1 trading fees](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees)
 on both Spot markets and Perpetual Futures products.

*   Fee tiers are updated every hour.
    
*   If your Mad Lads NFT leaves your Wallet, your fee tier will be adjusted accordingly.

# API Broker Program | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#api-broker-program)

API Broker Program


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

API Brokers on Backpack are eligible to earn broker rebates by offering their users access to Backpack's trading infrastructure and unique features across spot and futures markets.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#who-can-join)

Who Can Join

Qualified trading platforms and order execution service providers including, but not limited to:

*   Exchange Aggregators
    
*   Trading Terminals
    
*   Trading Bots
    
*   Algorithmic Trading Platforms
    
*   Social Trading Platforms
    
*   Asset Managers
    

circle-info

To be eligible for this program please, reach out to us at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
**

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#how-to-integrate)

How to Integrate

1.  Open up a business account at Backpack Exchange.
    
2.  Check **API Docs** and set up connections to the endpoints relevant to your service.
    
3.  Send your unique broker code as the API request header.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#api-broker-vs.-affiliate)

API Broker vs. Affiliate

API brokers receive rebates on the trading fees generated by orders submitted via API by them. Affiliates receive rebates on the trading fees generated by new users onboarded with their referral code.

Please note that the API Broker Program and the Backpack [Affiliate Program](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/affiliate-program)
 are mutually exclusive. Enrollment in one program will preclude enrollment in the other.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#api-broker-rebates)

API Broker Rebates

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#backpack-api-broker-rebates)

Backpack API Broker Rebates

Tier

Volume

Rebate

BKR 0

$0

20%

BKR 1

$5,000,000

30%

BKR 2

$10,000,000

35%

BKR 3

$20,000,000

40%

BKR 4

$60,000,000

45%

BKR 5

$100,000,000

50%

BKR tiers take into account both spot and futures trading volume from orders submitted via API.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#tier-calculation-and-payout)

Tier calculation and payout

On the first day of every month, broker tiers are updated and payouts calculated. The formula is:

**Payout = API Volume × Fee Rate % × min {Broker Rebate %, max{0, 50% - Affiliate Rebate %}}**

Where:

*   Fee Rate is determined by each user's [**trading fees**](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/trading/trading-fees)
     and applicable discounts.
    
*   Broker Rebate is determined by the API broker's BKR tier.
    
*   Affiliate Rebate is determined by the referral code (if any) used by each user to sign up.
    
*   API Volume is the spot and futures trading volume from orders submitted via API with the unique broker code of each API Broker as the API request header.
    

Rebates will be settled in USD and credited to the broker's Backpack account every month.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/api-broker-program#get-in-touch)

Get in touch

We are always ready to discuss your business needs and provide 24/7 integration support.

Reach out to: [**\[email protected\]**envelope](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#44322d34042625272f3425272f6a213c272c252a2321)

# Python WebSocket Guide for Backpack Exchange API | Backpack Exchange

This guide demonstrates how to use the Backpack Exchange WebSocket API with Python. WebSockets provide real-time data streams for market data and account updates.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#prerequisites)

Prerequisites

Get your API keys if you are going to use private streams: [https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/settings/api-keysarrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/settings/api-keys)

Install the required Python libraries:

*   websockets - for WebSocket connections
    
*   cryptography - for X-Signature (private streams only)
    

Copy

    pip install websockets cryptography

Install dotenv-python to securely manage your keys using environment variables if you are going to use private streams

Copy

    pip install python-dotenv

Create a .env file and store your keys like this:

Copy

    PUBLIC_KEY=zDIJj9qneWIY0IYZ5aXoHcNMCm+XDhVcTssiT0HyY0A=
    SECRET_KEY=4odxgSUxFrC/zsKWZF4OQwYAgnNu9hnWH3NxWfLAPz4=

Create a .gitignore file and add .env to exclude it from version control.

Copy

    .env

Import the necessary libraries:

Copy

    import json
    import asyncio
    import websockets
    import base64
    from time import time
    import os
    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ed25519
    from dotenv import load_dotenv, find_dotenv

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#websocket-api-basics)

WebSocket API Basics

The Backpack Exchange WebSocket API is available at `wss://ws.backpack.exchange`.

WebSocket streams are named using the format: `<type>.<symbol>`

For example:

*   `depth.SOL_USDC` - Order book for SOL/USDC
    
*   `trade.SOL_USDC` - Trades for SOL/USDC
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#why-use-async-with-websockets)

Why Use Async with WebSockets

WebSockets are designed for long-lived connections that receive data in real-time. Using asynchronous programming with WebSockets offers several advantages:

1.  **Non-blocking I/O**: Async allows your application to handle multiple connections without blocking the main thread.
    
2.  **Resource Efficiency**: Async uses fewer resources than creating multiple threads for concurrent connections.
    
3.  **Better Performance**: Async can handle many connections with less overhead than synchronous approaches.
    
4.  **Real-time Processing**: Async is ideal for real-time data streams where you need to continuously receive and process data.
    

Synchronous approaches have several drawbacks:

1.  **Complex Threading**: Requires manual thread management
    
2.  **Resource Intensive**: Each connection needs its own thread
    
3.  **Difficult Error Handling**: Error propagation across threads is complex
    
4.  **Scaling Issues**: Does not scale well with many connections
    

The async approach (as shown in our examples) is much cleaner, more efficient, and easier to maintain.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#public-streams)

Public Streams

Public streams don't require authentication. You can subscribe to them directly.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#example-subscribing-to-a-public-stream)

Example: Subscribing to a Public Stream

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#example-subscribing-to-multiple-public-streams)

Example: Subscribing to Multiple Public Streams

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#private-streams)

Private Streams

Private streams require authentication with your API keys. These streams are prefixed with `account.` and provide updates about your account.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#authentication-for-private-streams)

Authentication for Private Streams

To authenticate for private streams, you need to:

1.  Create a signature string of the form: `instruction=subscribe&timestamp=1614550000000&window=5000`
    
2.  Sign it with your private key
    
3.  Include the signature data in your subscription message as an array: `"signature": ["<verifying key>", "<signature>", "<timestamp>", "<window>"]`
    

Private streams are prefixed with `account.` and require signature data to be submitted in the subscribe parameters. The verifying key and signature should be base64 encoded.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#example-subscribing-to-a-private-stream)

Example: Subscribing to a Private Stream

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#websocket-ping-pong)

WebSocket Ping/Pong

WebSocket connections require a ping-pong mechanism to keep the connection alive. The good news is that the Python `websockets` library handles this automatically

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api#sources)

Sources

For more information visit the official documentation: [https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Streamsarrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Streams)

Copy

    async def subscribe_to_public_stream():
        uri = "wss://ws.backpack.exchange"
    
        async with websockets.connect(uri) as websocket:
            # Subscribe to the depth stream for SOL/USDC
            subscribe_message = {
                "method": "SUBSCRIBE",
                "params": ["depth.SOL_USDC"]
            }
    
            await websocket.send(json.dumps(subscribe_message))
            print(f"Subscribed to depth.SOL_USDC stream")
    
            # Process incoming messages
            while True:
                response = await websocket.recv()
                data = json.loads(response)
                print(f"Received: {data}")
    
                # You can process the data here based on your needs
                # For example, update a local order book
    
    # To run the async function in a Jupyter notebook, use:
    # await subscribe_to_public_stream()
    #
    # TO run in your code
    # asyncio.run(subscribe_to_public_stream())  

Copy

    async def subscribe_to_multiple_streams():
        uri = "wss://ws.backpack.exchange"
    
        async with websockets.connect(uri) as websocket:
            # Subscribe to multiple streams
            subscribe_message = {
                "method": "SUBSCRIBE",
                "params": ["depth.SOL_USDC", "trade.SOL_USDC"]
            }
    
            await websocket.send(json.dumps(subscribe_message))
            print(f"Subscribed to multiple streams")
    
            # Process incoming messages
            while True:
                response = await websocket.recv()
                data = json.loads(response)
                print(f"Received: {data}")
    
                # Process different stream data based on the stream name
                if "stream" in data and "data" in data:
                    stream_name = data["stream"]
                    stream_data = data["data"]
    
                    if stream_name.startswith("depth."):
                        # Process order book data
                        print(f"Order book update: {stream_data}")
                    elif stream_name.startswith("trade."):
                        # Process trade data
                        print(f"Trade update: {stream_data}")
    
    # Run the async function in a Jupyter notebook:
    # await subscribe_to_multiple_streams()
    #
    # To run in your code
    # asyncio.run(subscribe_to_multiple_streams())

Copy

    # Load API keys from .env file
    # load_dotenv(find_dotenv())
    # public_key = os.getenv("PUBLIC_KEY")
    # secret_key = os.getenv("SECRET_KEY")
    
    # For demonstration purposes only - don't hardcode keys in production
    public_key = "5+yQgwU0ZdJ/9s+GXfuPFfo7yQQpl9CgvQedJXne30o="
    secret_key = "TDSkv44jf/iD/QCKkyCdixO+p1sfLXxk+PZH7mW/ams="
    
    # Create private key from secret key
    private_key = ed25519.Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes(
        base64.b64decode(secret_key)
    )

Copy

    async def subscribe_to_private_stream():
        uri = "wss://ws.backpack.exchange"
    
        # Generate authentication parameters
        timestamp = int(time() * 1e3)  # Unix time in milliseconds
        window = "5000"  # Time window in milliseconds
    
        # Create signature string
        sign_str = f"instruction=subscribe&timestamp={timestamp}&window={window}"
    
        # Sign the string
        signature_bytes = private_key.sign(sign_str.encode())
        encoded_signature = base64.b64encode(signature_bytes).decode()
    
        async with websockets.connect(uri) as websocket:
            # Subscribe to the order stream with authentication
            subscribe_message = {
                "method": "SUBSCRIBE",
                "params": ["account.orderUpdate"],
                "signature": [public_key, encoded_signature, str(timestamp), window]
            }
    
            await websocket.send(json.dumps(subscribe_message))
            print(f"Subscribed to account.order stream")
    
            # Process incoming messages
            while True:
                response = await websocket.recv()
                data = json.loads(response)
                print(f"Received: {data}")
    
                # Process order updates
                if "stream" in data and data["stream"] == "account.order" and "data" in data:
                    order_data = data["data"]
                    print(f"Order update: {order_data}")
    
    # Run the async function in a Jupyter notebook:
    await subscribe_to_private_stream()
    # 
    # If using nest_asyncio (recommended):
    # asyncio.run(subscribe_to_private_stream())

# Market Maker Program | Backpack Exchange

Market makers on Backpack are eligible for maker rebates across spot and futures markets, and participate in a monthly equity reward pool valued at ~$195,000 for providing liquidity on perpetual futures markets.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#overview)

Overview

Market makers on Backpack are eligible for maker rebates across spot and futures markets, and participate in a monthly equity reward pool valued at ~$195,000 for providing liquidity on perpetual futures markets.

**To be eligible for this program, please reach out to us at** [**\[email protected\]**envelope](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#a1d7c8d1e1c3c0c2cad1c0c2ca8fc4d9c2c9c0cfc6c4)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#market-maker-rebates)

Market Maker Rebates

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FX6IEYTYQTIYMRdGNWPoN%252FBackpack%2520MM%2520Rebates.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D1b495086-69e6-45fa-a581-e2aecb092edc&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=3fc4230&sv=2)

Note: Values shown without a “%” symbol are expressed in basis points (bps).

MM tiers can be reached **either** by Total Maker Share or Adjusted Maker Share, whichever is highest. The Adjusted Maker Share model rewards traders for providing liquidity on low liquidity markets. Each market has a multiplier (as presented below), which is used to calculate the Adjusted Volume.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FqUl3QuSBYnM7sgvR2rjz%252FBackpack%2520Spot%2520and%2520Futures%2520Volume%2520Multiplier.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Ddc89c8e3-6223-491b-91b7-439644eb2fc0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8182d46f&sv=2)

Adjusted maker share is calculated by dividing a user's total adjusted maker volume from that month by the total adjusted maker volume of all users across all markets in each instrument category.

MMs can also unlock MM tiers through their Total Maker Share, which does not factor in market multipliers. For Total Maker Share, we take the user’s total maker volume and divide it by the total maker volume of all users across all markets in each instrument category without applying any multipliers.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#mm-tier-calculation-and-payout)

MM Tier Calculation and Payout

On the last day of every month, we will calculate the Total Maker Share and Adjusted Maker Share for each user and choose the **highest** between the two to determine their MM tier / fees for the month.

Rebates will be credited manually to the user’s Backpack account the following month. In the meantime, users participating in the MM program will have their fee tier set to VIP2, which has 0 maker fees.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#market-maker-monthly-rewards)

Market Maker Monthly Rewards

Backpack has allocated 5% of the company’s equity as of December 2023 to reward market makers that provide liquidity on the exchange. 2.5% has been allocated to spot and futures each, which nets to ~$195,000 in monthly rewards for each instrument (using our latest valuation of $120m as of February 2024).

Spot rewards started on January 1 2024 and ended on December 31 2024. The futures program starts on February 1, 2025.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#monthly-futures-reward-distribution)

Monthly Futures Reward Distribution

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FvLb7fWfGB1O0JOxWwnJH%252FBackpack%2520Futures%2520Equity%2520and%2520Rewards.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Df7ff2144-74c7-4267-88f0-2d20a143dac9&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=93160ca8&sv=2)

**Pair Categories**

*   **Primary Pairs**: BTC-PERP, ETH-PERP, and SOL-PERP
    
*   **Secondary Pairs**: All other futures pairs
    

**Primary Pairs Distribution**

The total primary reward pool is split equally among BTC-PERP, ETH-PERP, and SOL-PERP. Each primary pair receives 0.03472% of the total reward pool.

**Secondary Pairs Distribution**

The reward for secondary pairs is distributed based on the number of days each pair was active during the month (the timestamp of first trade is considered the start). The formula is:

_**Individual Pair Reward % = Pair's Active Days / Sum of All Pairs' Active Days**_

**Example**

Let's say we have three secondary pairs in a 30-day month:

*   Pair A: Active for 30 days
    
*   Pair B: Active for 30 days
    
*   Pair C: Active for 10 days
    

Total active days across all pairs = _**70 days (30 + 30 + 10)**_

Therefore:

*   Pair A receives: (30/70) = 37.5% of secondary rewards
    
*   Pair B receives: (30/70) = 37.5% of secondary rewards
    
*   Pair C receives: (10/70) = 25% of secondary rewards
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#scoring-system-for-monthly-rewards)

Scoring System For Monthly Rewards

To determine a user’s share of the reward pool for a given pair, we will look at their Blended Score, which takes into account their Volume Score and Liquidity Score.

**For each market:**

*   **Volume Score** = your maker volume / volume of all MMs in the program
    
*   **Liquidity Score** = Your Liquidity Metric / Sum of all MMs Liquidity Metric
    
    *   Liquidity Metric = Average Size / (Average Spread) \* Uptime Score
        
        *   Uptime Score = Active Snapshots / Total Snapshots
            
        
    
*   **Blended Score** = (Volume Score \* Volume Weight) + (Liquidity Score \* Liquidity Weight)
    
*   **Reward** = Blended Score \* Monthly Reward Pool
    

Volume Score has a weight of 0.8 and Liquidity Score 0.2.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#optional-liquidity-kpis)

Optional Liquidity KPIs

Market Makers (MMs) who maintain liquidity KPIs for at least 80% of the month will receive a 30% boost to both their Volume Score and Liquidity Score on the relevant pairs where they hit the KPI. This boost is taken from the allocation that would otherwise go to MMs who fall below the 80% uptime threshold.

See optional liquidity KPIs below:

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FMvI7KuogCbhA0uiToIj4%252FBackpack%2520Depth%2520and%2520Spread.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D78bf2076-a184-4934-9faf-4f16e27f7ec7&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=b061bdba&sv=2)

The One-Sided Depth values shown above represent the minimum liquidity required on each side of the order book (bid and ask) independently. For example, a $10,000 depth requirement means market makers must maintain at least $10,000 worth of orders on both the buy side and the sell side separately, within the specified spread level.

Note that the KPIs may change as volumes and liquidity on the exchange evolve. If a market doesn't have liquidity KPIs specified for it, the 30% rewards boost is not applicable for that market.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#mm-reward-calculation-example)

MM Reward Calculation Example

The example below illustrates how rewards are calculated for a single market.‍

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FkvxoezdCxsSkuhE9fDUM%252FChart%2520Inputs.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd26bf7d7-bf49-4056-b290-ba2294309f38&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=8b03cb9c&sv=2)

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FlXPvjpdviykjMqVabpzj%252FBackpack%2520Maker%2520Volume%2520Size%2520and%2520Spread.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D50ed7a37-ae9e-46a5-a2c0-c0bc47c97058&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=dd5f2a9b&sv=2)

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252Fj0PwGDM2JRM8AetgJ5x6%252FBackpack%2520Maker%2520Scores%2520and%2520Rewards.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D9bc089b7-3b2a-42ca-9ee1-571746bc3175&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=9bc71c27&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/market-maker-program#further-information)

Further Information

*   All new MMs will enjoy MM5 maker fees for their first month; thereafter, fees will be determined based on the user’s market share and volumes at the end of the fee holiday period. **Backpack Exchange does not, and will not entertain special fee deals with any MM. Fee schedules with all MMs are the same.**
    
*   MMs participating in this program are not able to participate in other programs.
    
*   MMs must receive at least 1% of the total allocation in order to be eligible for equity rewards in a given month. Additionally, MMs with an average size lower than $2000 or a volume score lower than 1.5% won't be eligible for rewards on a given market.
    
*   For the liquidity score calculation, we will only consider orders placed within 100bps from the mid.
    
*   For a limited time, each MM has the option to have Backpack buy back the awarded equity every month at the previous fundraise valuation ($120m as of February 2024). If a market maker wishes to receive their month’s reward in cash, the market maker must let Backpack know before the end of the following month. For example, a MM has until November 30 to state how they want to receive their October rewards. Previous equity rewards that have not been claimed in cash cannot be converted.
    
*   MMs must comply with Backpack Exchange's User Agreement and all applicable policies and procedures.
    
*   Backpack Exchange reserves the right to waive certain KPI requirements for a certain period of time in its sole discretion.
    
*   Backpack Exchange reserves the right to modify this program any time when appropriate.
