Name the new API key and select 'Create' button, in order to create the API Key you’ll need to enter your authenticator app code](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/generate-api-keys-for-backpack-exchange#id-4.-name-the-new-api-key-and-select-create-button-in-order-to-create-the-api-key-youll-need-to-enter)
    
*   [5\. Record your API key and API secret in a secure location](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/generate-api-keys-for-backpack-exchange#id-5.-record-your-api-key-and-api-secret-in-a-secure-location)

# Backpack Exchange Python API guide | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#prerequisites)

Prerequisites

Get your API keys if you are going to use account endpoints: [https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/settings/api-keysarrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/portfolio/settings/api-keys)

Install the required Python libraries:

*   cryptography – for X-Signature ([account endpointsarrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Account)
     only)
    
*   requests – for making HTTP requests (or aiohttp if you prefer async)
    

Copy

    pip3 install cryptography requests 

Install dotenv-python to securely manage your keys using environment variables if you are going to use [account endpointsarrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Account)

Copy

    pip3 install python-dotenv

Create a .env file and store your keys like this:

Copy

    PUBLIC_KEY=zDIJj9qneWIY0IYZ5aXoHcNMCm+XDhVcTssiT0HyY0A=
    SECRET_KEY=4odxgSUxFrC/zsKWZF4OQwYAgnNu9hnWH3NxWfLAPz4=

Create a .gitignore file and add .env to exclude it from version control.

Copy

    .env

For all examples, we will use the synchronous requests library. Let's import it:

Copy

    import requests

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#public-endpoints)

Public endpoints

For public endpoints, simply send a GET request.

No API keys are required.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#example-accessing-public-data)

Example: Accessing Public Data

**Note:** If you have more than one argument, join them using the & symbol.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#private-endpoints)

Private endpoints

For private endpoints, we need to create specific headers and a request body (for POST requests). This requires authentication with your API keys.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#example-retrieving-a-deposit-address)

Example: Retrieving a Deposit Address

Let's see how to retrieve a deposit address using the API: [https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Capital/operation/get\_deposit\_addressarrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Capital/operation/get_deposit_address)

Now that we have our authentication components ready (`X-Timestamp`, `X-Window`, and `X-API-Key`), let's create the signature.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#signing-the-request)

Signing the Request

Now let's sign the request with our private key:

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#creating-headers)

Creating Headers

Create the required headers for the API request:

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#sending-the-request)

Sending the Request

Now we can send the authenticated request to the API:

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#using-post-requests)

Using POST Requests

For `POST` requests, you need to include the JSON body and use the `post()` method instead of `get()`.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#example-executing-an-order)

Example: Executing an Order

Let's see how to submit an order to the matching engine for execution using the API: [https://api.backpack.exchange/api/v1/orderarrow-up-right](https://api.backpack.exchange/api/v1/order)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/backpack-exchange-python-api-guide#sources)

Sources

SDK makes the development process much easier.

Example SDK: [https://github.com/sndmndss/bpx-pyarrow-up-right](https://github.com/sndmndss/bpx-py)

For more information, visit the official documentation: [https://docs.backpack.exchange/arrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/)

    # https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Markets/operation/get_open_interest

    symbol: str = "SOL_USDC_PERP" # let's specify the symbol
    result_url: str = f"{BASE_URL}api/v1/openInterest?symbol={symbol}"  # add your argument as a query string. For GET requests you need only query string

Copy

    from json import JSONDecodeError
    
    response = requests.get(url=result_url) # make a get request
    print(f"response status code: {response}")
    if response.status_code == 200:
        # make your code safe in case you receive unexpected data
        try:
            print(f"response json: {response.json()}") 
    
            open_interest: str = response.json()[0]["openInterest"]
            print(f"open interest: {open_interest}")
        except JSONDecodeError:
            print(f"response text if response isn't json: {response.text}")
    else:
        ...

Copy

    import base64  # for base64 encoding the signature
    from time import time  # for timestamp generation
    import os  # to access environment variables
    
    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ed25519  # to create a private key for signing
    # from dotenv import load_dotenv, find_dotenv  # to load environment variables from .env file

Copy

    # In production code, use this approach:
    # load_dotenv(find_dotenv())
    # public_key: str = os.getenv("PUBLIC_KEY")
    # secret_key: str = os.getenv("SECRET_KEY")
    
    # For demonstration purposes only - don't hardcode keys in production
    public_key: str = "5+yQgwU0ZdJ/9s+GXfuPFfo7yQQpl9CgvQedJXne30o="
    secret_key: str = "TDSkv44jf/iD/QCKkyCdixO+p1sfLXxk+PZH7mW/ams="  
    
    # Create private key from secret key
    private_key = ed25519.Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes(
                base64.b64decode(secret_key)
            )

Copy

    # Generate timestamp and window parameters
    timestamp = int(time() * 1e3)  # Unix time in milliseconds
    window: str = "5000"  # Time window in milliseconds that the request is valid for

Copy

    # Define the instruction for this API call
    instruction: str = "depositAddressQuery"
    sign_str = f"instruction={instruction}"
    
    # This endpoint requires the "blockchain" parameter
    params: dict = {
        "blockchain": "Solana",
    }
    
    # Generate a valid query string from parameters
    sorted_params_list = []
    for key, value in sorted(params.items()):
        if isinstance(value, bool):  # boolean variables should be lowercase in query strings
            value = str(value).lower()
        sorted_params_list.append(f"{key}={value}")
    sorted_params = "&".join(sorted_params_list)
    
    # Combine all parts of the signature string
    if sorted_params:
        sign_str += "&" + sorted_params
    sign_str += f"&timestamp={timestamp}&window={window}" 
    
    print(f"Signature string: {sign_str}")

Copy

    # Sign the string with our private key
    signature_bytes = private_key.sign(sign_str.encode())
    encoded_signature = base64.b64encode(signature_bytes).decode()
    print(f"Base64 encoded signature: {encoded_signature}")

Copy

    # Prepare the headers with all required authentication parameters
    headers = {
        "X-API-Key": public_key,
        "X-Signature": encoded_signature,
        "X-Timestamp": str(timestamp),
        "X-Window": window,
        "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
    }

Copy

    # Send the GET request with our authentication headers
    url = "https://api.backpack.exchange/wapi/v1/capital/deposit/address"
    response = requests.get(url=url, headers=headers, params=params)
    print(response.json())

Copy

    # Generate timestamp and window parameters
    timestamp = int(time() * 1e3)  # Unix time in milliseconds
    window = "5000"  # Time window in milliseconds that the request is valid for
    
    # Define the instruction for this API call
    instruction = "orderExecute"
    sign_str = f"instruction={instruction}"
    
    # Create the order request body
    order_params = {
        "symbol": "SOL_USDC",       # Required: The market for the order
        "side": "Bid",              # Required: "Bid" (buy) or "Ask" (sell)
        "orderType": "Limit",       # Required: "Market" or "Limit"
        "price": "170.50",           # The order price (required for limit orders)
        "quantity": "1.0",          # The order quantity
        "timeInForce": "GTC",       # "GTC" (Good Till Cancelled), "IOC" (Immediate or Cancel), "FOK" (Fill or Kill)
        "clientId": 123456,         # Custom order ID (optional)
        "selfTradePrevention": "RejectTaker"  # Optional: "RejectTaker", "RejectMaker", "RejectBoth"
    }
    
    # Generate a valid query string from parameters
    sorted_params_list = []
    for key, value in sorted(order_params.items()):
        if isinstance(value, bool):  # boolean variables should be lowercase in query strings
            value = str(value).lower()
        sorted_params_list.append(f"{key}={value}")
    sorted_params = "&".join(sorted_params_list)
    
    # Combine all parts of the signature string
    if sorted_params:
        sign_str += "&" + sorted_params
    sign_str += f"&timestamp={timestamp}&window={window}" 
    
    print(f"Signature string: {sign_str}")

Copy

    # Sign the string with our private key
    signature_bytes = private_key.sign(sign_str.encode())
    encoded_signature = base64.b64encode(signature_bytes).decode()
    print(f"Base64 encoded signature: {encoded_signature}")

Copy

    # Prepare the headers with all required authentication parameters
    headers = {
        "X-API-Key": public_key,
        "X-Signature": encoded_signature,
        "X-Timestamp": str(timestamp),
        "X-Window": window,
        "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
    }

    # Send the POST request with our authentication headers and order parameters
    url = "https://api.backpack.exchange/api/v1/order"
    response = requests.post(url=url, headers=headers, json=order_params)
    print(f"Response status code: {response.status_code}")
    try:
        print(f"Response JSON: {response.json()}")
    except JSONDecodeError:
        print(f"Response text: {response.text}")

# Bug Bounty Program | Backpack Exchange

> 📢 **Submit your reports and find full details on our Hackenproof page:** 🔗 [Backpack Web & API Bug Bounty @ Hackenproofarrow-up-right](https://hackenproof.com/programs/backpack-web-and-api)

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/bug-bounty-program#rules)

Rules


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Rewards are categorized into five tiers** based on the severity of the identified vulnerabilities.

The reward amounts are as follows:

*   **Critical**: $10,000 - $100,000 USD
    
*   **High**: $5,000 - $10,000 USD
    
*   **Medium**: $500 - $5000 USD
    
*   **Low**: $50 - $500 USD
    

‍

Upon acceptance of your bug or vulnerability report, rewards will be disbursed in USDC. Please note that the threat level will be assessed by the Backpack security team, and Backpack reserves the sole discretion to determine whether a report meets the reward criteria.

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/bug-bounty-program#scope-of-vulnerabilities)

Scope of Vulnerabilities‍


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following modules are within the scope of the bug bounty program:

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66e9549271c9cf4677a05828_66e9545ea8255b2c03f1e8f9_backpack_bug_bounty_program.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=1281bbbb&sv=2)

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/bug-bounty-program#criteria)

Criteria


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Reports should focus on the following types of vulnerabilities:**

*   Issues with business logic that may result in the loss of user assets.
    
*   Payment manipulation.
    
*   Remote code execution (RCE).
    
*   Leakage of sensitive information.
    
*   Critical OWASP issues such as XSS, CSRF, SQLi, SSRF, IDOR, and similar vulnerabilities.
    
*   Other vulnerabilities that may result in potential loss.
    

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/programs/bug-bounty-program#exclusions)

Exclusions


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**The following issues are not within the scope of the bug bounty program:**

*   Theoretical vulnerabilities that have not been proven.
    
*   Flaws in email verification codes, expired password reset links, and password complexity policies.
    
*   Clickjacking and UI redirection with minor security impact.
    
*   Vulnerabilities in third-party applications or software.
    
*   Zero-day exploits that are less than 30 days old.
    
*   Social engineering and phishing attacks.
    
*   Denial of Service (DoS) attacks.
    
*   Enumeration of email, phone number, or username information.
    
*   Known issues, duplicate submissions, or vulnerabilities already disclosed.
    
*   Physical attacks.
    
*   Vulnerabilities that can only be exploited in older versions of browsers or platforms.
    
*   Using known codebase vulnerabilities without actual proof.
    
*   Lack of security flags in cookies.
    
*   Issues related to insecure SSL/TLS sockets or protocol versions.
    
*   Content-based deception.
    
*   Cache management issues.
    
*   Internal IP or domain name leakages.
    
*   Missing security headers that cannot be directly exploited.
    
*   CSRF issues with negligible impact (such as adding to favorites, adding to cart, subscribing, etc.)
    
*   Issues without any security impact.
    

‍

**For any queries or submissions, please contact us at** [**\[email protected\]**envelope](https://support.backpack.exchange/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#8fedfae8ede0fae1fbf6cfedeeece4ffeeece4a1eaf7ece7eee1e8ea)
**.**

‍

We look forward to your participation and thank you for helping us maintain a secure and trustworthy trading environment.

# FAQs | Backpack Exchange

chevron-rightWhat APIs does Backpack support?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#what-apis-does-backpack-support)

Backpack offers two ways to integrate, depending on whether you need request/response actions or real-time streaming data.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#rest-api)

REST API

Use the REST API for on-demand actions like fetching market/account data and managing orders.

Common endpoints include:

*   GET /markets
    
*   GET /balances
    
*   POST /orders
    
*   DELETE /orders
    

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#websocket-api)

WebSocket API

Use the WebSocket API for low-latency, real-time streams

Common use cases include:

*   Subscribing to order book updates
    
*   Subscribing to candlestick (kline) streams
    
*   Subscribing to authenticated account events (e.g., order and position updates)
    

chevron-rightCan the API be used for futures trading?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#can-the-api-be-used-for-futures-trading)

Yes. Backpack supports both spot and perpetual futures through the same order and account endpoints. To trade perps, use a perp market symbol For example: POST /orders - SOL\_USDC\_PERP

chevron-rightCan I withdraw assets using the API?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#can-i-withdraw-assets-using-the-api)

To enable withdrawals, strict security controls are required (including two-factor authentication).

To configure a withdrawal address with mandatory 2FA, **please use:** [https://backpack.exchange/settings/withdrawal-addresses?twoFactorWithdrawalAddress=truearrow-up-right](https://backpack.exchange/settings/withdrawal-addresses?twoFactorWithdrawalAddress=true)

**twoFactorToken parameter** (https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Capital/operation/request\_withdrawal:~:text=asset%20to%20withdraw.-,twoFactorToken,-string)is currently disabled

chevron-rightWhat are the API rate limits? Are they different per server or per subaccount?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#what-are-the-api-rate-limits-are-they-different-per-server-or-per-subaccount)

Rate limits are applied per subaccount, not per server. By default, each subaccount can make up to 2000 requests per minute across standard REST endpoints Historical market data endpoints are limited at 30 requests per minute per subaccount. (“Historical endpoints” refers to REST routes that return time-range data such as candles/trades.)

If you hit a limit, you’ll receive HTTP 429 responses. Consider using WebSocket streams for high-frequency market data.

chevron-rightWhat are the common errors when placing orders?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#what-are-the-common-errors-when-placing-orders)

Error Code

Description

Resolution

INVALID\_CLIENT\_REQUEST'

Invalid signature

Verify query string construction method (sorting, instruction value). Also verify that your boolean values in query string are lowercase. “True” should be “true”.

INVALID\_CLIENT\_REQUEST'

Request has expired

Time that took request to reach exchange servers exceeded X-Window and window value. Try to increase the window

INVALID\_CLIENT\_REQUEST'

Quantity decimal too long

Adjust quantity to stepSize

INVALID\_CLIENT\_REQUEST'

Invalid X-Signature Header

Query string was signed with wrong keypair Verify that you use ED25519

429

Too many requests

Reduce request frequency; consider WebSocket streaming

chevron-rightWhere can I find SDKs?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#where-can-i-find-sdks)

The official SDK is currently available only in Rust: [https://github.com/backpack-exchange/bpx-api-clientarrow-up-right](https://github.com/backpack-exchange/bpx-api-client)
 Please refer to the following page for community-maintained examples in various languages: [https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/api-clientsarrow-up-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/api-clients)

chevron-rightHow can I get real-time market data for a trading pair?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#how-can-i-get-real-time-market-data-for-a-trading-pair)

Use WebSocket streams for live market updates:

[Book Tickerarrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Streams/Public/Book-ticker)
 (bookTicker.<symbol>) Best bid/ask prices and quantities in real time. Ideal for top-of-book displays.

[Deptharrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Streams/Public/Depth)
 (depth.<symbol>) Incremental order-book updates. Start by fetching an initial snapshot via REST, then apply depth deltas from the stream. Depth is available in real-time or aggregated modes (e.g., 200ms / 600ms / 1000ms) to reduce traffic. See the full list of streams here: [https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Streamsarrow-up-right](https://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Streamshttps://docs.backpack.exchange/#tag/Streams)

chevron-rightHow can I improve the stability of my trading bot?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/faqs#how-can-i-improve-the-stability-of-my-trading-bot)

Recommended best practices:

*   Prefer WebSocket subscriptions for real-time order/trade updates instead of polling REST
    
*   Implement retry + exponential backoff for REST requests and WS reconnects
    
*   Keep a local cache of frequently used reference data (markets, symbols, filters) and refresh periodically
    
*   Monitor and alert on critical API errors (auth failures, expired requests, and 429 rate limits)
    

[PreviousPython WebSocket Guide for Backpack Exchange APIchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/exchange/api-and-developer-docs/python-websocket-guide-for-backpack-exchange-api)
[NextWalletchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet)

# Import/Recover Wallet | Backpack Exchange

If you have lost access to your Wallet extension or mobile app you can recover it using a **Seed Recovery Phrase** or **Private Keys**.

Note: If you do not have your Seed Recovery Phrase or Private Keys then Backpack will not be able to help you recover the Wallet.

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started/import-recover-wallet#import-or-recover-a-backpack-wallet-using-a-seed-phrase-mobile)

Import or Recover a Backpack Wallet Using a Seed Phrase (Mobile)


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A seed phrase is the ONLY way to recover your account if you lose your device. We cannot help you recover your account if you do not have the seed phrase.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started/import-recover-wallet#id-1.-select-advanced-wallet-import)

1\. Select "Advanced wallet import"

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66971084f4afe6c415844827_9c19ee11-5d15-4fe2-a346-791960a3ac47.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=b9bfeacc&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started/import-recover-wallet#id-2.-select-private-key)

2\. Select "Private key"

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F6697108407ab18597b8a7288_3c75c264-1ae2-4bae-83dd-7a8939bb5699.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=798cc650&sv=2)

‍

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started/import-recover-wallet#id-3.-select-derive-from-recovery-phrase)

3\. Select "Derive from recovery phrase"

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66971086cee6ea7beb996b71_c0f0bbc5-3d55-4948-93c0-945037e94fce.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a6d05da2&sv=2)

‍

###

# Custom RPC Address | Backpack Exchange

Backpack Wallet supports your right to choose RPC (Remote Procedure Call) providers. They allow crypto dApps (such as Tensor & Jupiter) to communicate and interact with a blockchain.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/custom-rpc-address#how-to-use-custom-rpcs-with-backpack-wallet)

How to use custom RPCs with Backpack Wallet


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Open the main menu and choose the appropriate network
    
2.  Select 'RPC Connection'
    
3.  Enter the Custom menu and enter RPC address. Update.
    

[PreviousWallet Multi-Accountschevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts)

# Add Network | Backpack Exchange

Backpack currently supports Solana, Ethereum, Optimism, Arbitrum, Polygon, Base and Sonic with more on the way.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/add-network#how-to-add-new-networks-on-mobile)

How to add new networks on Mobile


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Open the main menu at top left of app
    
2.  Select the new network in the Networks section
    
3.  Proceed with new wallet creation
    

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/add-network#how-to-add-new-networks-on-extension)

How to add new networks on Extension


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Open the drop down menu at the top of the app
    
2.  Open the network selector and select "Add Network" option
    
3.  Choose the new network and process with wallet creation

###

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F679a44b60ad1494669f9d034_679a444eaec81e223c086e97_how_to_swap_backpack_wallet2.jpeg&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=68a70624&sv=2)

1.  Select the Approve button

# Wallet Multi-Accounts | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts#feature-overview)

Feature Overview

Wallet Multi-Accounts enables you to:

*   Create separate wallet groups
    
*   Maintain clearer identity boundaries
    
*   Organize wallets based on usage or ownership
    

This ensures improved asset management, enhanced security, and an overall more structured wallet experience.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts#setting-up-multi-accounts-on-mobile)

Setting Up Multi-Accounts on Mobile

Follow the steps below to configure and manage multiple wallet accounts on your Backpack mobile app.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts#id-1.-manage-an-existing-group)

1\. Manage an Existing Group

*   Navigate to the **Wallet Management** screen.
    
*   Under the **Wallets** section, click **Account 1** (or the group you wish to manage).
    
*   Tap the **three dots** icon located at the bottom right corner.
    
*   From here, you may:
    
    *   **Edit the account name**
        
    *   **Remove the account** from the list
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FlW38OY7Tal9bnob1d31d%252F0e72d498-94db-4b93-be9a-799c208c8463.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D63c9309d-30b7-474d-99b3-1fc5b544a0c0&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=bb9b00c7&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts#id-2.-add-a-new-group)

2\. Add a New Group

*   Navigate to the **Wallet Management** screen.
    
*   Under the **Wallets** section, tap **Account 1** (or any existing group).
    
*   Select **Add Account**.
    
*   Enter a **name** for the new account.
    
*   Choose one of the following options:
    
    *   [**Import an existing wallet**](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started/import-recover-wallet)
         using a seed phrase or private key
        
    *   **Create a new wallet** with a fresh seed phrase
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252FIDW20Ne7GZBPHSMxedqm%252Fd8d87c52-b9fd-40a6-b57f-3e4ba954a4b7.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D4feb4c46-7a32-44d7-89a7-170ca9a058fb&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=352389ac&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts#id-3.-add-a-new-wallet-within-a-group)

3\. Add a New Wallet Within a Group

1.  In the **Wallet Management** screen, locate **Account 1** (or the group you wish to manage) under the _Wallets_ section.
    
2.  Tap the **plus (+) icon** beside the account.
    
3.  Select the **blockchain network** you wish to use.
    
4.  Choose one of the following options:
    
    *   [**Import an existing wallet**](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/get-started/import-recover-wallet)
         on that chain
        
    *   **Create a new wallet** within the same group
        
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1790800155-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252F3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k%252Fuploads%252F0VIG3G0cNGOKFZYS9jz3%252Fe243d6bb-9880-4f31-aafa-c2ce63682999.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D64fae92c-75cc-4b67-aad7-606cbb6a0239&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=acc877ad&sv=2)

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts#example-use-cases)

Example Use Cases

You can create different account groups for various purposes, such as:

*   **Personal Wallets** – for individual transactions and asset management
    
*   **Corporate Wallets** – for company-owned funds or operational usage
    
*   [**Multisig Wallets**](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig)
     – requiring multiple approvals for transactions
    

This structure enhances clarity, helps segregate responsibilities, and improves workflow efficiency for you managing diverse assets.

[PreviousAdd Networkchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/add-network)
[NextCustom RPC Addresschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/custom-rpc-address)

Last updated 1 month ago

*   [3\. Add a New Wallet Within a Group](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/wallet-multi-accounts#id-3.-add-a-new-wallet-within-a-group)

# Buy Crypto in Backpack Wallet with MoonPay | Backpack Exchange

MoonPay lets you purchase supported tokens directly inside Backpack using Apple Pay, debit/credit card, or other local payment rails.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/how-to-buy-and-sell-crypto-with-moonpay/buy-crypto-in-backpack-wallet-with-moonpay#step-by-step)

**Step by Step**

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/how-to-buy-and-sell-crypto-with-moonpay/buy-crypto-in-backpack-wallet-with-moonpay#id-1.-select-cash-button)

1\. Select “Cash” button

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXfAaMF9qoFCUB90FBsmZB9gK6CPYO3HnFIory2sHRGMMMoBJOf4YjQjZ1RP9Dxi6JIgnXbjUXCGYZLPVLHPMUNL7cD7cln2JN9rL_GvKPPlShpd-nbTY3CvZaO9wH9a0-ZuOKiP%3Fkey%3DXZPMa1hZFtab0Vfrt-uMPg&width=300&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f6ab7585&sv=2)

* * *

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/how-to-buy-and-sell-crypto-with-moonpay/buy-crypto-in-backpack-wallet-with-moonpay#id-2.-choose-buy-crypto)

2\. Choose “Buy Crypto”

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh7-rt.googleusercontent.com%2Fdocsz%2FAD_4nXfaJJPxrx3xuV5XGqCTqVfTRO4XeNZZijmzRB8U8rze6opaFUhwwppQwDCYw4dVsHvTGAydWi-j6yjF2HHZSRlz3a2esEyQd2E2RI16iWoxmtkGwZeK1GITi-Ocrtt1GuYGg69C%3Fkey%3DXZPMa1hZFtab0Vfrt-uMPg&width=300&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=24fe588d&sv=2)

**Need help with a MoonPay transaction?**

**Important:** Backpack does **not** handle your card payment. All billing, settlement, and refunds are processed by **MoonPay**.

If you have any questions or issues related to your MoonPay transaction — including payment status, delays, refunds, or verification, we recommend reaching out to their support team directly via: [https://support.moonpay.comarrow-up-right](https://support.moonpay.com/)

# Stake SOL | Backpack Exchange

There are two main ways to stake Solana on Backpack:

1.  Liquid Staking: stake SOL while maintaining liquidity through tokenized staking derivatives like bpSOL
    
2.  Native Staking: delegate SOL directly to a validator through your wallet.
    

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#how-to-how-to-liquid-stake-sol-for-bpsol)

How To How To Liquid Stake SOL for bpSOL


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Backpack staked SOL, or bpSOL, can boost your rewards by up to 15% (rates vary and change frequently). Follow these steps to convert your existing SOL to bpSOL and start earning today.

*   Swap SOL for bpSOL in the Swap tab
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#how-to-natively-stake-sol)

How To Natively Stake SOL


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Backpack recommends 2 validators, the Backpack Validator and the Mad Lads validator. Follow these steps to natively stake your existing SOL through a Solana validator and start earning today.

1.  Open Solana (SOL) from the token list
    
2.  Select the 'Stake SOL to Earn' button
    
3.  Choose the 'Add Stake' button
    
4.  Enter the amount of SOL you wish to stake and select the 'Stake' button
    

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#faqs-about-staking-sol-with-backpack)

**FAQs about staking SOL with Backpack**


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

chevron-right**1\. What is the minimum amount of SOL needed to stake?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#id-1.-what-is-the-minimum-amount-of-sol-needed-to-stake)

0.01 SOL

chevron-right**2\. How long does it take for staked SOL to activate?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#id-2.-how-long-does-it-take-for-staked-sol-to-activate)

This period can vary however a new stake will activate within 24 hours.

chevron-right**3\. Can I unstake my SOL at any time?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#id-3.-can-i-unstake-my-sol-at-any-time)

You can unstake SOL at any time however you must wait for the next Solana epoch to receive the tokens in your Wallet.

[See the Solana Epoch status herearrow-up-right](https://explorer.solana.com/)

chevron-right**4\. What is the Mad Validator?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#id-4.-what-is-the-mad-validator)

The Mad Validator is Backpack owned and managed. It began operations after the Mad Lads NFT collection launch.

[See details about the Mad Validator here.arrow-up-right](https://www.validators.app/validators/CjmXSapt1ouz3CZzgkRJckBEwMSo5fVdVrizLeRscwYD?locale=en&network=mainnet)

chevron-right**5\. What is staked SOL used for?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#id-5.-what-is-staked-sol-used-for)

Staked SOL is used to validate blockchain transactions on the Solana network.

chevron-right**6\. How long should I stake my SOL tokens?**[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#id-6.-how-long-should-i-stake-my-sol-tokens)

SOL is typically staked for long periods of time

chevron-right7\. What is the difference between native staking and liquid staking on Solana?[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol#id-7.-what-is-the-difference-between-native-staking-and-liquid-staking-on-solana)

The key difference between native staking and liquid staking on Solana comes down to liquidity and flexibility:

*   Native Staking
    
    *   You delegate SOL directly to a validator.
        
    *   Your SOL is locked and cannot be used elsewhere until you unstake (which takes 2-3 days).
        
    *   You earn staking rewards but have no immediate liquidity.
        
    
*   Liquid Staking
    
    *   You stake SOL through a liquid staking protocol (like Sanctum)
        
    *   You receive a liquid staking token (e.g. bpSOL) that represents your staked SOL.
        
    *   This token can be traded, used in DeFi, or swapped back to SOL.
        
    

[PreviousSell Crypto in Backpack Wallet with MoonPaychevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/how-to-buy-and-sell-crypto-with-moonpay/sell-crypto-in-backpack-wallet-with-moonpay)
[NextSOL/ETH Bridgechevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/sol-eth-bridge)

Last updated 2 months ago

# SOL/ETH Bridge | Backpack Exchange

Access the Solana <-> Ethereum crypto bridge.

Users must create both a Solana and Ethereum wallet in the same Account before the Solana/Ethereum token bridge option will appear.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/sol-eth-bridge#how-to-bridge-assets-in-backpack-wallet)

How to bridge assets in Backpack Wallet:

1.  Press the **Swap** button
    
2.  Choose the **token** you want to send
    
3.  Select the token you want to receive menu
    
4.  Select the **Ethereum** tab
    
5.  Choose the token you want to receive
    
6.  Review transaction and Approve
    

[PreviousStake SOLchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/stake-sol)

**Need help with a MoonPay transaction?**

**Important:** Backpack does **not** handle your card payment. All billing, settlement, and refunds are processed by **MoonPay**.

If you have any questions or issues related to your MoonPay transaction — including payment status, delays, refunds, or verification, we recommend reaching out to their support team directly via: [https://support.moonpay.comarrow-up-right](https://support.moonpay.com/)

# Secure NFTs | Backpack Exchange

Collection Locking is a simple function that automatically rejects transaction requests when an NFT is Locked in the Backpack Wallet. Anytime an application tries to modify your NFT, Backpack will imm

‍**It only works when you use Backpack Wallet exclusively.**

If you import a Backpack Wallet into another service provider, NFTs will not be locked in that wallet.

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/secure-nfts#secure-nfts-in-with-collection-locking-in-backpack-wallet)

Secure NFTs in with Collection Locking in Backpack Wallet


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Navigate to the Collectibles menu
    
2.  Open the NFT collection you wish to secure
    
3.  Toggle on the the lock at top of screen
    

**Note:** It's important to remember that this only locks the NFTs on that specific Wallet deployment.

[PreviousSOL/ETH Bridgechevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/sol-eth-bridge)
[NextConnect Hardware Walletchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet)

# Connect Hardware Wallet | Backpack Exchange

Backpack Wallet is the only Solana wallet to support three of the world's most trusted cold storage hardware providers: Ledger, Trezor and KeystoneBackpack Wallet is also the only Solana wallet to support 3-of-3 multisig; the most secure method for securing crypto assets.Learn more about multisig crypto protection in Backpack's [**How To Use Backpack Wallet For Multisig Protection.**arrow-up-right](https://app.gitbook.com/o/1pzK7I96ua1kPK2SCiPg/s/3kiM6CAFBZoTCJcZ8X7k/~/changes/510/wallet/actions/multisig)
​​

* * *

**Important steps before importing a hardware wallet to Backpack**

*   Complete hardware device setup
    
*   Update hardware device firmware
    
    *   ‍[Ledger firmwarearrow-up-right](https://www.ledger.com/ledger-live)
        ​[‍arrow-up-right](https://trezor.io/trezor-suite)
        ​
        
    *   ​[Trezor firmwarearrow-up-right](https://trezor.io/trezor-suite)
        ​[‍arrow-up-right](https://keyst.one/firmware)
        ​
        
    *   ​[Keystone firmwarearrow-up-right](https://keyst.one/firmware)
        ‍
        
    
*   Download the apps needed to support your crypto assets
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#heres-how-to-connect-and-import-a-cold-storage-hardware-wallet-to-backpack)

Here's how to connect and import a cold storage hardware wallet to Backpack.

#### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-1.-download-the-backpack-wallet)

1\. Download the Backpack Wallet

*   ​[Download Backpack Wallet on Androidarrow-up-right](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=app.backpack.mobile)
    ​
    
*   ​[Download Backpack Wallet on iOSarrow-up-right](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/backpack-crypto-wallet/id6445964121)
    ​
    
*   ​[Download Backpack Wallet browser extensionarrow-up-right](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/backpack/aflkmfhebedbjioipglgcbcmnbpgliof)
    ​
    

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.gitbook.com%2F__img%2Fdpr%3D2%2Cwidth%3D1168%2Conerror%3Dredirect%2Cformat%3Dauto%2Csignature%3D476999425%2Fhttps%253A%252F%252Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%252F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%252F66a08b80529f0b81b639d230_221b702f-e4b0-4468-bda3-6e3f32fb0647.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=b15adbc9&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-2.-after-creating-an-account-open-the-main-menu-in-backpack-and-select-add-account)

2\. After creating an Account, open the Main Menu in Backpack and select 'Add Account'.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66a08b818b97e26c34e376a2_84bb5eed-568a-4206-bf3d-b3db0a5a382e.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f2ed7f6d&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-3.-select-import-wallet)

3\. Select 'Import Wallet'.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66a08b809fd902817681c770_b49479b5-488f-4171-b619-0b30bda8f81a.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=f1d3957a&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-4.-select-import-wallet-and-choose-the-appropriate-network)

4\. Select 'Import Wallet' and choose the appropriate network.

**Note:** Ledger, Trezor and Keystone each support thousands of crypto tokens and networks. Any choice during this step will allow connection.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66a08b8019ffc49973bb3ff4_62dccfa5-52aa-4af2-a399-d30300800380.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=a7c86bf0&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-5.-select-use-a-hardware-wallet)

5\. Select 'Use a hardware wallet'.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66a08b80f49b5b79bc036452_1aa09262-3483-4d2b-98db-00e731dc979e.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=197b5288&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-6.-select-the-appropriate-hardware-wallet-and-approve-connection-requests)

6\. Select the appropriate hardware wallet and approve connection requests.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66a08b8023f8ae1d3d8c9572_0b6aa412-890c-4992-a452-0a42596a6432.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=7d708112&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-7.-choose-the-appropriate-funded-address-es-and-select-import-wallet)

7\. Choose the appropriate Funded Address(es) and select 'Import Wallet'.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66a08b80c03d42115356fa0e_35b5f20f-5b6f-4081-8ec2-552eba7a19b4.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=e857dc0f&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet#id-8.-open-backpack-wallet-and-access-your-hardware-wallet-account)

8\. Open Backpack Wallet and access your hardware wallet account.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F66a08b80d7e1771ff40a84ad_ea2de9a5-a6cc-410d-ac8e-5bb1f0ef6754.png&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=b465d23&sv=2)

# Multisig | Backpack Exchange

Import your cold storage hardware wallets into Backpack and then connect Backpack to a multisig creation platform.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig#heres-the-step-by-step)

**Here’s the step-by-step.**

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig#id-1.-import-your-cold-storage-hardware-wallet-s-to-backpack)

**1\. Import your cold storage hardware wallet(s) to Backpack.**

*   Open the Settings menu and select 'Add Account'
    
*   Select 'Import Wallet' and choose the appropriate network
    
*   Select 'Use a hardware wallet' and choose 'Use a Keystone wallet'
    
*   Follow directions on device to approve connection
    
*   Proceed with wallet setup instructions
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig#id-2.-visit-a-reputable-multisig-wallet-platform-that-has-integrated-backpack-wallet-and-initiate-the-s)

**2\. Visit a reputable multisig wallet platform that has integrated Backpack Wallet and initiate the setup process.**

*   [Squads (Solana) (Recommended)arrow-up-right](https://squads.so/)
    
*   [Realms (Solana)arrow-up-right](https://app.realms.today/realms/new)
    
*   [Safe (Ethereum)arrow-up-right](https://safe.global/)
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig#id-3.-utilize-backpack-wallet-as-the-interface-to-manage-signatures-and-transactions)

**3\. Utilize Backpack Wallet as the interface to manage signatures and transactions.**

**Note:** You will sign transactions on your cold storage hardware device using Backpack Wallet as the connection point.

[PreviousConnect Hardware Walletchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/connect-hardware-wallet)
[NextAdd Developer Testnetschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/add-developer-testnets)

Last updated 2 months ago

*   [Here’s the step-by-step.](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig#heres-the-step-by-step)
    
*   [1\. Import your cold storage hardware wallet(s) to Backpack.](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig#id-1.-import-your-cold-storage-hardware-wallet-s-to-backpack)
    
*   [2\. Visit a reputable multisig wallet platform that has integrated Backpack Wallet and initiate the setup process.](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/multisig#id-2.-visit-a-reputable-multisig-wallet-platform-that-has-integrated-backpack-wallet-and-initiate-the-s)

# Add Developer Testnets | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/add-developer-testnets#how-to-add-developer-testnets-with-backpack-wallet)

How to add developer testnets with Backpack Wallet

1.  Enable Developer Mode in the Settings menu (located in Preferences submenu)
    
2.  Open the Wallet menu at top of app
    
3.  Select the drop down menu network selector
    
4.  Choose 'Add Network' and pick the appropriate network

# Wallet Not Loading | Backpack Exchange

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/wallet-not-loading#extension)

Extension

1.  Verify that the extension is updated to the most recent version. ‍
    
2.  Turn the extension on and off in the browser extension menu.
    

If those actions do not solve issue then you may need to reinstall the extension. ‍

**Note: Ensure that your Seed Phrase or Private Keys are saved and available before trying to reinstall and import your wallets.**

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/wallet-not-loading#mobile)

Mobile

1.  Verify that the app is updated to the most recent version.
    
2.  Force close the app and re-open. Repeat this multiple times if needed.
    

If those actions do not solve issue then you may need to reinstall the app. ‍

**Note: Ensure that your Seed Phrase or Private Keys are saved and available before trying to reinstall and import your wallets.**

[PreviousHide Tokenschevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-tokens)
[NextView Secret Recovery Phrases and Private Keyschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/view-secret-recovery-phrases-and-private-keys)

# Collaboration Application Form | Backpack Exchange

Apply to collaborate or integrate your project with Backpack Wallet. Learn about the benefits, criteria, and how to submit your application.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/collaboration-application-form#why-collaborate-with-backpack-wallet)

Why Collaborate with Backpack Wallet?

*   **Expand Your Reach:** Engage directly with the active and growing Backpack Wallet community, connecting your project to dedicated crypto enthusiasts.
    
*   **Innovative Integrations:** Seamlessly integrate your project's functionality, offering users enhanced utility and experiences.
    
*   **Build Trust:** Partnering with Backpack Wallet demonstrates credibility and positions your project as a trusted solution in the crypto ecosystem.
    

Learn more about our community and vision on the [Aboutarrow-up-right](https://learn.backpack.exchange/about)
 page.

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/collaboration-application-form#how-to-submit-your-application)

How to Submit Your Application

1.  **Complete the Application Form:** Clearly outline your project's goals, unique aspects, and relevant documentation.
    
2.  **Await Follow-up:** After submission, our team will reach out for any additional details or clarifications needed.
    

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/actions/collaboration-application-form#apply-now)

Apply Now

Ready to integrate or collaborate and accelerate your project's growth with Backpack Wallet? Complete the application form below, and our team will get back to you soon.

# View Secret Recovery Phrases and Private Keys | Backpack Exchange

Backpack recommends recording all Secret Recovery Phrases and saving them in a secure location. This is the only way to recover your account if you lose access to your devices or password.

**Warning: DO NOT SHARE your Secret Recovery Phrase with anyone. Backpack Support or staff will never ask you for your Secret Recovery Phrase.**

* * *

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/view-secret-recovery-phrases-and-private-keys#secret-recovery-phrase-mobile-app)

Secret Recovery Phrase (Mobile App)


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/view-secret-recovery-phrases-and-private-keys#id-1-navigate-to-settings-and-open-the-security-menu)

1) Navigate to Settings and open the 'Security' menu

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/view-secret-recovery-phrases-and-private-keys#id-2-choose-show-secret-recovery-phrase)

2) Choose 'Show Secret Recovery Phrase'

Keep your Secret Recovery Phrase private, never share with anyone.

![](https://support.backpack.exchange/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F669641f210a1019f2d8231fa%2F678202e9c01489edbc1e1619_678202c9c01489edbc1df4eb_view_secret_recovery_phrase_backpack_wallet6.jpeg&width=768&dpr=4&quality=100&sign=7d7dd60c&sv=2)

* * *

# Hide Spam NFTs | Backpack Exchange

* * *

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-spam-nfts#id-1-select-the-hide-some-toggle-at-the-bottom-of-the-collectibles-tab)

1) Select the "Hide Some" toggle at the bottom of the Collectibles tab.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-spam-nfts#id-2-burn-individual-nfts-inside-of-the-backpack-wallet-using-integrated-burn-function-located-in-top-r)

2) Burn individual NFTs inside of the Backpack Wallet using integrated Burn function located in top right corner of individual NFT view.

*   **Note: The integrated Burn function within the Backpack Wallet only works for traditional Solana NFTs, cNFTs are not burnable.**
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-spam-nfts#id-3-burn-nfts-using-the-sol-incinerator-tool)

3) Burn NFTs using the [**Sol-Incinerator tool**arrow-up-right](https://sol-incinerator.com/)
.

*   **Note: Sol-Incinerator is a third party tool which Backpack is not partnered with.**
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-spam-nfts#id-4-send-the-nfts-to-a-burner-wallet.-you-can-easily-create-new-wallets-in-your-backpack-account-to-he)

4) Send the NFTs to a "burner" wallet. You can easily create new wallets in your Backpack account to help manage this.

[PreviousTroubleshootchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot)
[NextHide Tokenschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-tokens)

*   [2) Burn individual NFTs inside of the Backpack Wallet using integrated Burn function located in top right corner of individual NFT view.](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-spam-nfts#id-2-burn-individual-nfts-inside-of-the-backpack-wallet-using-integrated-burn-function-located-in-top-r)
    
*   [3) Burn NFTs using the Sol-Incinerator tool.](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/troubleshoot/hide-spam-nfts#id-3-burn-nfts-using-the-sol-incinerator-tool)

# Localhost RPC Endpoints | Backpack Exchange

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#using-localhost-rpc-endpoints-in-backpack)

Using `localhost` RPC Endpoints in Backpack


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> This is intended to be a non-technical explanation for why the usage of `http://localhost:XXXX` or `http://127.0.0.1:XXXX` as a custom RPC endpoint within Backpack (and some other wallets) does not work.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#background)

Background

When engineers want to test or develop against on-chain data or programs from within an isolated environment, running an RPC on `localhost` is often the choice.

This works until peripheral systems and applications that are _not_ local, also need to connect or send requests to this `localhost` runtime.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#problem)

Problem

Using the addresses `localhost` and `127.0.0.1` from a networking perspective are like referring to yourself (i.e. using the words `me` or `I`).

Backpack, along with some other mainstream wallets, read the asset holdings of your addresses/wallets from remote servers and APIs that do not live or execute in the client. When you log into Backpack, the mobile or web application makes a request to a remote server asking for information about your wallet: balances, transaction history, NFT holdings, etc.

When you set a custom RPC endpoint within the Backpack wallet application, it is used as a stand-in for the defaults that are pre-configured in a couple of different places:

1.  Transaction serialization and sending
    
2.  Health checks
    
3.  Blockchain/network metadata retrieval
    
4.  _**Telling the Backpack API to read data directly from on-chain instead of from other faster data sources**_
    

Setting a `localhost` RPC can affect the first three in different ways, but #4 is important. Since the Backpack API is a remote server, when its told to fetch on-chain data from an RPC endpoint at `http://localhost:XXXX`, what it thinks is:

> "There is a blockchain RPC node that _I am running_ and I should send it requests."

This is obviously not correct, because to the server, `localhost` isn't a user's laptop running the RPC node potentially in a different country. Instead, the custom RPC should always be set to a publicly accessible endpoint that doesn't reference any local or internal system networks or reserved names.

So how do I use my test RPC that _is_ running on `localhost`?

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#workaround)

Workaround

The key point is that the endpoint of the custom RPC needs to be _publicly accessible_.

There are lots of tools and applications out there that allow you to tunnel your applications running on `localhost` into the public space (a few examples):

*   [ngrokarrow-up-right](https://ngrok.com/)
     (preferred)
    
*   [localtunnelarrow-up-right](https://theboroer.github.io/localtunnel-www/)
    
*   [serveoarrow-up-right](https://serveo.net/)
    

...and lots of others.

These applications allow you to choose a port on your local internal network and expose it to the public internet via local tunneling with ephemeral public domain names that are reachable from external networks.

Using `ngrok`, all you need to do after creating and verifying an account is running the following command to setup a local tunnel for your RPC endpoint which you can these set the custom RPC endpoint in the wallet for:

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#why-do-some-dapps-work-without-this)

Why do some dApps work without this?

These are some cases where, even without a `localhost` tunnel, setting the custom RPC endpoint to `localhost` may still work.

This is purely dependent on the implementation of dApps that you are connected to. If the website of dApp is programmed to run on-chain queries directly from the client code, then it will likely still work because the network requests to the RPC are actually being made from code that is executing in your browser, and thus within the same network space as your `localhost` RPC.

That is the one exception to need for local tunneling.

[PreviousTechnical Docschevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs)
[NextDeeplinkschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks)

Last updated 6 months ago

*   [Using localhost RPC Endpoints in Backpack](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#using-localhost-rpc-endpoints-in-backpack)
    
*   [Background](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#background)
    
*   [Problem](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#problem)
    
*   [Workaround](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#workaround)
    
*   [Why do some dApps work without this?](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/localhost-rpc-endpoints#why-do-some-dapps-work-without-this)
    

Copy

    $ ngrok http 8080 # whichever port number you want

# Deeplinks | Backpack Exchange

Backpack allows for iOS and Android apps to natively interact with it using either universal links **(recommended)** or deeplinks.

circle-info

These workflows are collectively known as "deeplinks."

All [Provider Methods](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods)
 follow a protocol format of:

Copy

    https://backpack.app/ul/<version>/<method>

[NextProvider Methodschevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods)

# Connect | Backpack Exchange

Before an app can interact with Backpack, it must first establish a connection. This connection request prompts the user to share their public key, signaling their consent to proceed.

Once the user connects, Backpack provides a session parameter that should be used for all subsequent methods.

circle-info

Please see [Handling Sessions](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/handling-sessions)
 for more information on sessions.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect#base-url)

Base URL


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy

    https://backpack.app/ul/v1/connect

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect#query-string-parameters)

Query String Parameters


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `app_url` (required): A URL that provides app metadata (e.g., title, icon). It must be URL-encoded.
    
*   `dapp_encryption_public_key` (required): A public key for end-to-end encryption, used to generate a shared secret. Refer to the [Encryption](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/encryption)
     section for details on how Backpack manages shared secrets.
    
*   `redirect_link` (required): The URI where Backpack should redirect the user upon connection. See [Specifying Redirects](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/specifying-redirects)
     for more details. Must be URL-encoded.
    
*   `cluster` (optional): Specifies the network for subsequent interactions. To connect to Solana mainnet, leave the parameter unset or set it to `mainnet-beta`. To connect to Eclipse, set the parameter to `solana:EAQLJCV2mh23BsK2P9oYpV5CHVLDNHTxY`.
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect#returns)

Returns


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect#approve)

Approve

*   `wallet_xxx`: A base58-encoded encryption public key used by Backpack to build a shared secret between the connecting app and Backpack.
    
*   `nonce`: A nonce encoded in base58 that is used to encrypt the response.
    
*   `data`: JSON string that has been encrypted. Learn how apps can decrypt data using a shared secret in [Encryption](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/encryption)
    . Base58 is used to encode encrypted bytes.
    
    Copy
    
        // content of decrypted `data`-parameter
        {
          // base58 encoding of user public key
          "public_key": "3huUrtWW5s5ew98eUFRYz9LPsDUQTujNzzYaB9DBkppQ",
        
          // session token for subsequent signatures and messages
          // apps should send this with any other deeplinks after connect
          "session": "..."
        }
    
    *   `public_key`: The user's public key, represented as a base58-encoded string.
        
    *   `session`: A string encoded in base58. This should be treated as opaque by the connecting app, as it only needs to be passed alongside other parameters. Sessions do not expire. For more information on sessions, please review [Handling Sessions](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/handling-sessions)
        .
        
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect#reject)

Reject

An `errorCode` and `errorMessage` as query parameters.

Please refer to [Errors](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/limitations#errors)
 for a full list of possible error codes.

[PreviousProvider Methodschevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods)
[NextDisconnectchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect)

Last updated 9 months ago

*   [Query String Parameters](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect#query-string-parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect#returns)

# Disconnect | Backpack Exchange

After the initial [Connect](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect)
 event, an app may disconnect from Backpack at any time. When Backpack is disconnected, it will reject all signature requests until a new connection is established.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#base-url)

Base URL


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy

    https://backpack.app/ul/v1/disconnect

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#query-string-parameters)

Query String Parameters


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `dapp_encryption_public_key` **(required)**: The app's original encryption public key for an existing Connect session.
    
*   `nonce` **(required)**: A base58-encoded nonce is used to encrypt the request.
    
*   `redirect_link` **(required)**: Backpack should redirect the user to this URI after completion. For more information, please see [Specifying Redirects](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/specifying-redirects)
    . URL-encoded.
    
*   `payload` **(required)**: An encrypted JSON string with the following fields:
    
    Copy
    
    Copy
    
        {
            "session": "...", // token received from the connect method
        }
    
    *   `session` **(required)**: The session token received from the Connect method. Please see [Handling Sessions](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/handling-sessions)
         for more details.
        
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#returns)

Returns


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#approve)

Approve

No query params are returned.

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#reject)

Reject

An `errorCode` and `errorMessage` as query parameters.

Please see [Errors](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/limitations#errors)
 for a complete list of possible error codes.

[PreviousConnectchevron-left](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect)
[NextSignAndSendTransactionchevron-right](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signandsendtransaction)

Last updated 9 months ago

*   [Base URL](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#base-url)
    
*   [Query String Parameters](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#query-string-parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#returns)
    
*   [Approve](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#approve)
    
*   [Reject](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/disconnect#reject)
    

Copy

    {
      "errorCode": "...",
      "errorMessage": "..."
    }

# SignAndSendTransaction | Backpack Exchange

Once connected to Backpack, an application can ask the user for permission to send transactions on their behalf.

In order to send a transaction, an application must:

1\. Create an unsigned transaction.

2\. Have the transaction be signed and submitted to the network by the user's Backpack wallet.

3\. Optionally await network confirmation using a Solana JSON RPC connection.

circle-info

For more information about the nature of Solana transactions, please review the [`solana-web3.js` docsarrow-up-right](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/)
, as well as the [Solana Cookbookarrow-up-right](https://solanacookbook.com/core-concepts/transactions.html#transactions)
.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signandsendtransaction#base-url)

Base URL


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy

    https://backpack.app/ul/v1/signAndSendTransaction  

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signandsendtransaction#query-string-parameters)

Query String Parameters


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `dapp_encryption_public_key` **(required)**: The original encryption public key used from the app for an existing [Connect](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect)
     session.
    
*   `nonce` **(required)**: A nonce used for encrypting the request, encoded in base58.
    
*   `redirect_link` **(required)**: The URI where Backpack should redirect the user upon completion. Please review [Specifying Redirects](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/specifying-redirects)
     for more details. URL-encoded.
    
*   `payload` **(required)**: An encrypted JSON string with the following fields:
    

Copy

    {
      "transaction": "...", // serialized transaction, base58-encoded
      "sendOptions": "..." // an optional Solana web3.js sendOptions object
      "session": "...", // token received from the connect method
    }

*   `transaction` **(required)**: The [transactionarrow-up-right](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/classes/Transaction.html)
     Backpack will sign and submit is serialized and encoded in base58.
    
*   `sendOptions` (optional): An optional object that specifies options for how Backpack should submit the transaction. This object is [defined in Solana web3.jsarrow-up-right](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/modules.html#SendOptions)
    .
    
*   `session` **(required)**: The session token received from the [Connect](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect)
     method. Please see [Handling Sessions](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/handling-sessions)
     for more details.
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signandsendtransaction#returns)

Returns


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signandsendtransaction#approve)

Approve

*   `nonce`: A nonce used for encrypting the response, encoded in base58.
    
*   `data`: An encrypted JSON string. Refer to [Encryption](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/encryption)
     to learn how apps can decrypt `data` using a shared secret. Encrypted bytes are encoded in base58.
    

*   `signature`: The first signature in the transaction can be used as its [transaction idarrow-up-right](https://docs.solana.com/terminology#transaction-id)
    .
    

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signandsendtransaction#reject)

Reject

An `errorCode` and `errorMessage` as query parameters. Please refer to [Errors](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/limitations#errors)
 for a full list of possible error codes.

# SignAllTransactions | Backpack Exchange

Once an app is connected, it is also possible to sign multiple transactions at once. Unlike [SignAndSendTransaction](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signandsendtransaction)
, Backpack will not submit these transactions to the network.

Applications can submit signed transactions using the [web3.jsarrow-up-right](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/classes/Connection.html#sendRawTransaction)
 `sendRawTransaction`.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signalltransactions#base-url)

Base URL


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy

    https://backpack.app/ul/v1/signAllTransactions

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signalltransactions#query-string-parameters)

Query String Parameters


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `dapp_encryption_public_key` **(required)**: The original encryption public key used from the app side for an existing [Connect](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect)
     session.
    
*   `nonce` **(required)**: A nonce used for encrypting the request, encoded in base58.
    
*   `redirect_link` **(required)**: The URI where Backpack should redirect the user upon completion. Please review [Specifying Redirects](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/specifying-redirects)
     for more details. URL-encoded.
    
*   `payload` **(required)**: An encrypted JSON string with the following fields:
    
    Copy
    
        {
          "transactions": [\
            "...", // serialized transaction, bs58-encoded\
            "...", // serialized transaction, bs58-encoded\
          ],
          "session": "...", // token received from connect-method
        }
    
    *   `transactions` **(required)**: A serialized array of [transactionsarrow-up-right](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/classes/Transaction.html)
        , encoded in base58, that Backpack will sign.
        
    *   `session` **(required)**: The session token received from the [Connect](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/connect)
         method. Please see [Handling Sessions](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/handling-sessions)
         for more details.
        
    

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signalltransactions#returns)

Returns


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signalltransactions#approve)

Approve

*   `nonce`: A nonce used for encrypting the response, encoded in base58.
    
*   `data`: An encrypted JSON string. Refer to [Encryption](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/encryption)
     to learn how apps can decrypt `data` using a shared secret. Encrypted bytes are encoded in base58.
    
    *   `transactions`: An array of signed, serialized transactions that are base58 encoded. Backpack will not submit these transactions. Applications can submit these transactions themselves via the [web3.jsarrow-up-right](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/classes/Connection.html#sendRawTransaction)
         `sendRawTransaction`.

[hashtag](https://support.backpack.exchange/wallet/technical-docs/deeplinks/provider-methods/signalltransactions#reject)
